Toyota Material Handling Europe (TMHE) helps businesses of all sizes across Europe meet today’s materials handling challenges with a full range of Toyota counterbalanced forklift trucks, BT warehouse equipment, and services and added value solutions, including service contracts, short term rental, used forklift trucks and the Toyota I_Site information service. 

Active in more than 30 countries across Europe and with headquarters in Sweden, TMHE has a European Office in Brussels and production centres in Ancenis (France), Bologna (Italy) and Mjölby (Sweden). 

With nearly 5,000 service engineers, around 10,800 employees, along with a network of independent distributors and dealers, TMHE is the European regional organisation of Toyota Material Handling Group (TMHG), which is part of Toyota Industries Corporation (TICO) – the global leader in materials handling equipment. 

For more information on TMHE, visit www.toyota-forklifts.eu.
Truck Transportation · 720 employees (+5.5% YoY) · Sweden · 43,988 followers

Toyota Insurance Management Solutions, including its affiliates Connected Analytic Services and Advanced Connectivity (collectively “TIMS”) is a joint venture between Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance Services, USA (AIS), Toyota Financial Services International Corp., and Toyota Connected, USA. The vision of TIMS is to help Toyota empower drivers with their driving data for a safer, more enjoyable life. Our objective is to sell and service innovative auto insurance products to Toyota and Lexus owners with a specific focus on telematics-enabled, usage based insurance (UBI). TIMS is part of Toyota’s global transformation from an automotive company to a mobility company, enabling a safer driving experience through data and connectivity. During our initial start-up, new hires are selected by the TIMS team, onboarded as AIS employees and seconded to work at TIMS.

“Trade groups for U.S., Japanese and other global automakers have urged the Trump administration to maintain import restrictions on China's auto industry. The trade groups argued in a letter this month to Trump administration officials that China's efforts to gain access to the U.S. auto market ‘pose a direct threat to America's global competitiveness, national security, and automotive industrial base.’ ‘The auto industry favors global trade and competition, but that trade and competition must be fair. Unfortunately, China’s auto industry operates under a system that undermines those principles,’ states the letter signed by five organizations representing automotive companies operating in the U.S., including the Association and the Alliance for Automotive Innovation, a group that counts GM and Toyota Motors among its members. The letter calls on the Trump administration to maintain the regulations.” “Regulations enacted by the Biden administration to exclude software from China and Russia. Currently, very few cars using parts or software from China or Russia are on U.S. roads. The Biden administration adopted the regulations with the aim of preventing an influx of Chinese cars. Although only a few companies actually use Chinese software, the auto industry in the U.S. is on guard against a potential rush of Chinese cars into the market. During a January rally in Michigan, Trump made remarks indicating he would be open to Chinese auto companies entering the U.S. Chinese manufacturers can sell cars in the U.S., but vehicles and parts from China are subject to steep tariffs. TDK Corporation is a world leader in electronic solutions for the smart society based in Tokyo, Japan. Built on a foundation of material sciences mastery, TDK welcomes societal transformation by resolutely remaining at the forefront of technological evolution. It was established in 1935 to commercialize ferrite, a key material in electronic and magnetic products. TDK’s comprehensive, innovation-driven portfolio features passive components such as ceramic, aluminum electrolytic and film capacitors, as well as magnetics, high-frequency, and piezo and protection devices. The product spectrum also includes sensors and sensor systems such as temperature and pressure, magnetic, and MEMS sensors. In addition, TDK provides power supplies and energy devices, magnetic heads, software and more. These products are marketed under the product brands TDK, EPCOS, InvenSense, Micronas, Tronics, and TDK-Lambda. TDK focuses on demanding markets in automotive, industrial and consumer electronics, and information and communication technology. The company has a network of design and manufacturing locations and sales offices in Asia, Europe, and in North and South America. In fiscal 2025, TDK posted total sales of USD 14.4 billion and employed about 105,000 people worldwide.

Yesterday, CMTA's President & CEO, [Lance Hastings](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lance-hastings-113bba6?trk=public_post-text), joined the [Public Policy Institute of California](https://www.linkedin.com/company/public-policy-institute-of-california?trk=public_post-text) for a panel discussion alongside [Lorena Gonzalez Fletcher](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lorena-gonzalez-fletcher-1b66287?trk=public_post-text) of the California Labor Federation AFL-CIO, and PPIC’s Tani Cantil-Sakauye and [Sarah Bohn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sarah-bohn-72949b8?trk=public_post-text). The panelists explored California’s economy, the rise of AI, and the future of our workforce. Bringing together perspectives from manufacturing, labor, and policy leaders underscores the importance of public policy in balancing the needs of workers and employers. Thank you to PPIC and the panelists for a thoughtful and engaging discussion!

Fully integrated into NVIDIA's Vera Rubin DSX reference design and compatible with the NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint, the three-dimensional (3D) simulation model of Hitachi’s solution replicates power behavior across the full electrical chain—from the utility grid to the data center rack—to help data center developers and utilities design, simulate, and deploy AI factories faster and with confidence. The solution, designed as SimReady assets, was announced - and is on display - at NVIDIA GTC 2026 this week. We ship 25 to 30 product updates a month. That's not a quarterly release cycle. That's not a big annual update with a flashy announcement. Almost every single day, something inside Recruit CRM gets better. Here's what most people don't realise about how we do it. 60-65% of all the code we deploy today is written by AI. Not by a human sitting and typing it line by line. Engineers direct, review, and ship what AI produces. And the output is a product that moves faster than it ever has before. When people ask me what AI has actually changed about building software, this is my answer. It's not the chatbot in your product. It's not the fancy demo. It's the speed at which a small, focused team can now build and ship something genuinely good. That's the real advantage.

I’ve spoken to 70+ recruitment agency owners this year. And honestly… Most of them are saying the same thing. “If I had a partner to handle BD, my revenue would skyrocket.” “It sucks the life out of me… cold calling is terrible.” “I’ve spent $25,000 and got zero clients from it.” “My biggest constraint? Just getting that first conversation.” “I’m tired, Josh… I’ve burned a lot of money.” “9 months in and nothing to show for it.” “I’ve spent $15K–$20K on marketing… ZERO results.” “I haven’t found anything that actually works.” It’s not a lead problem. It’s a system problem. Most agencies: → Rely on outdated methods → Chase roles too late → Burn cash on the wrong channels → Have no predictable way to start conversations So BD becomes frustrating. Inconsistent. And draining. But when you fix the way you approach it… Everything changes. You don’t need more candidates. You need more conversations with the right hiring managers. It’s the only way to truly understand how the business operates, how deeply we’re penetrating the market, and what success actually looks like. Job descriptions don’t tell you that. Interviews don’t tell you that. The field does. These visits directly shape how we hire: → what “great” really looks like → which profiles will actually perform → where we’re misaligned with reality At Wave, we’re encouraged to stay close to the business, not just the pipeline. That flexibility is a real strength — and something I don’t take for granted.🩵 Because at the end of the day, recruiting isn’t about filling roles. It’s about building teams that win in the real world.

Absolutely. Directly meeting with end-users of products and services we offer exposes us to hear their feedbacks and complaints they have, and this helps shape hiring decisions: what type of professionals to bring onboard that'd close the gaps excellently.

In 2026, the biggest competitor for your B2B SaaS isn’t Vendor A or Vendor B. It’s your buyer’s own team, armed with Claude Code. 🛠️ The competitive landscape has fundamentally shifted. We are no longer just fighting in a "Vendor vs. Vendor" war. We are fighting "Vendor vs. In-House Development." With the barrier to building software dropping to near zero, how do B2B SaaS companies maintain their edge when buyers suddenly have a very viable "we can just build this ourselves" option? If you are a PMM or SaaS Marketer, your GTM strategy needs an immediate update: 📊 1. The Feature Comparison Matrix Your website’s comparison page usually lists "Us vs. Competitor A vs. Competitor B." It’s time to add a new column: "Build It In-House." You must clearly articulate the true cost of ownership. It’s not just about the ease of building the initial product—it’s about the hidden nightmare of maintaining it, scaling it, and dealing with technical debt over time versus the continuous innovation your SaaS provides. ⚔️ 2. The "In-House" Battlecard "In-House Development" needs its own dedicated sales battlecard. When a prospect says on a demo call, "Our engineering team thinks they can just build this internally," does your sales team have a unified, bulletproof objection-handling framework ready? They need one today. The value of SaaS is no longer just "the code." It's the ongoing reliability, expertise, and operational peace of mind. We have to treat "In-House" as the formidable competitor it has become. Are you a B2B SaaS Marketer or PMM running into this exact objection more often on the front lines? If so, I’d love to connect. Let’s have a discussion on how we can strategically position our products against the rising tide of AI-powered in-house development. Drop a comment below or send me a DM! ## About

I help foreign brands evaluate, launch, grow, and succeed in the Japanese market. So what does that look like? Most global brands take a playbook that worked in the US, Europe, or China—and try to copy-paste it into Japan. That rarely works. Japan is different. The consumer journey is longer, more personal, more tactile. Brand loyalty is hard to win—and even harder to regain if you get it wrong. I've seen even the best brands stumble here. Why? They underestimate the complexity. They rely on partners who don’t challenge them. They use a cookie-cutter approach to a market that rewards nuance and precision. That’s where I come in. With an AI-driven framework and decades of real-world experience, I help each brand craft a bespoke launch strategy—tailored to their DNA, product category, and the realities of Japanese retail and consumer behavior. No two launch plans are the same, because no two brands are the same. Sometimes I advise at a strategic level. Other times, I step in and run the business—negotiating distributor terms, shaping pricing, building out EC, or leading the local team. I bring the designer’s eye, the strategist’s mind, and the operator’s hands. Clients work with me because I’m loyal, clear, and execution-oriented. They stay with me because I get results—without the theater. If you're serious about Japan, I can help you succeed here.

The North America CDX index (a benchmark for credit default swap spreads) reached a 9‑month high in March 2026. At the same time, the S&P 500 remains close to its historical highs, only a few percent below the records from the beginning of the year. We have seen such a divergence between these two markets before. In 2007, credit spreads started to rise many months before the equity market recognised the scale of the real problems in the financial sector. In 2022, the tightening of credit conditions preceded a correction of around 25% in the S&P 500. In 2015, signals from the corporate bond market foreshadowed an extended period of elevated volatility before equity indices showed visible moves. NBER research documents that periods of rising credit risk have historically been associated with lower future equity returns what only proves to be a recurring pattern in data across many decades and markets. Today, the CCC‑and‑below segment trades at a spread close to 9.7%. The ICE BofA HY OAS fluctuates around 3.2–3.3%: a level clearly above the lows of 2021. The market is starting to differentiate risk: higher‑quality issuers remain relatively stable, while weaker ratings are increasingly punished. The credit market is not omniscient. At times it is late, at times overly cautious. But historically when equities ignored signals from credit it was usually equities that adjusted, not the other way around. The data are what they are. How they are interpreted is up to each individual investor. [image by Claude Sonnet] [#CreditMarkets](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2F

🌏 Insurance AI: 900+ use cases — and still not scaling 🔍 More use cases do not mean more scale Allianz reports more than 900 AI use cases internally registered worldwide. Yet, speaking at InsurTech Insights, Jason Howes of Allianz UK noted that scaling AI remains difficult, as reported by Insurance Post (Mar 19, 2026). This is not a contradiction. “900 use cases” does not mean 900 scaled deployments. It reflects a portfolio of initiatives — from pilots to partial implementations — across the group. AI is already widespread. But most use cases remain local. They work within specific teams, on specific workflows, under specific assumptions. What they rarely do is scale across: systems workflows decision ownership That is where scaling becomes difficult. ⸻ ⚖️ Not a regulatory issue The EU, under the EU AI Act, pushes for structured, risk-based deployment. The UK, guided by institutions such as the Financial Conduct Authority, takes a more principles-based approach. Different models. Same pattern. Even in more flexible environments, AI does not automatically scale. Because the constraint is not regulatory permission. It is operational integration. ⸻ 🧩 What this means for insurers Scaling AI is not about launching more use cases. It requires: • standardising workflows where decisions can be made • defining clear ownership of AI-driven decisions • designing systems to integrate, not isolate, AI capabilities Without this, AI remains a collection of initiatives rather than an operating capability. ⸻ 🧭 Key message AI does not fail at scale because of individual firms. It fails because large organisations are not designed to absorb it consistently. Jesus Calling: March 29th, 2026 Jesus Calling: March 29 Stop trying to work things out before their times have come. Accept the limitations of living one day at a time. When something comes to your attention, ask Me whether or not it is part of today's agenda. If it isn't, release it into My care and go on about today's duties. When you follow this practice, there will be a beautiful simplicity about your life: a time for everything, and everything in its time. A life lived close to Me is not complicated or cluttered. When your focus is on My Presence, many things that once troubled you lose their power over you. Though the world around you is messy and confusing, remember that I have overcome the world. I have told you those things, so that in Me you may have Peace. RELATED SCRIPTURE: Ecclesiastes 3:1 (NLT) A Time for Everything 3 For everything there is a season, a time for every activity under heaven. John 16:33 (NLT) 33 I have told you all this so that you may have peace in me. Here on earth you will have many trials and sorrows. But take heart, because I have overcome the world.” Today's Prayer: Dear Jesus, Help us to trust in Your timing and embrace the simplicity of living one day at a time. Grant us the wisdom to discern what truly matters today and release the rest of our worries and concerns into Your care. As we draw near to You, may our lives find clarity and peace amidst the world's chaos. Let us focus on Your presence, knowing that You have overcome the world and given us peace that surpasses all human understanding. Guide us, Lord, to align our hearts with Your perfect timing and to find contentment in Your plan for each moment. Your way is better than any way we could imagine for ourselves. In Jesus' name, Amen.

We believe the next generation of B2B platforms will not just manage workflows. They will move money. But cross-border financial infrastructure is still too fragmented: payments in one place, compliance in another, cards somewhere else, and too much operational complexity in between. HeroWave is being built to change that. We are building cross-border finance infrastructure for B2B platforms — so teams can launch payouts, collections, and spend controls from one integration. The goal is simple: make cross-border financial workflows easier to launch, easier to manage, and easier to scale. This is not just a product story. It is a market story. As B2B platforms expand globally, the need for programmable, scalable, and embedded financial infrastructure will only grow That is the space HeroWave is going after. If you are: building a platform with cross-border payment needs exploring embedded finance infrastructure investing in fintech, payments, or B2B software we’d love to connect. CTD vs ACTD – Key Differences in Regulatory Submissions In global pharmaceutical registration, choosing the correct dossier format is critical for successful approvals. CTD (Common Technical Document) A globally harmonized format developed by ICH, used in regions like USA, Europe, Japan, and many other countries. It consists of 5 modules covering administrative, quality, non-clinical, and clinical data. ACTD (ASEAN Common Technical Dossier) A regional dossier format used in ASEAN countries such as Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, Philippines, and Vietnam. ACTD structure includes: • Part I – Administrative data • Part II – Quality documentation • Part III – Non-clinical overview • Part IV – Clinical overview Key Difference: CTD is more detailed and globally standardized, while ACTD is a simplified format adapted for ASEAN regulatory requirements. Understanding these formats ensures smooth submissions and avoids regulatory delays. At Novalence, we support pharmaceutical organizations in CTD dossier compilation and regulatory documentation for global markets. 🌏 Insurance AI: 900+ use cases — and still not scaling 🔍 More use cases do not mean more scale Allianz reports more than 900 AI use cases internally registered worldwide. Yet, speaking at InsurTech Insights, Jason Howes of Allianz UK noted that scaling AI remains difficult, as reported by Insurance Post (Mar 19, 2026). This is not a contradiction. “900 use cases” does not mean 900 scaled deployments. It reflects a portfolio of initiatives — from pilots to partial implementations — across the group. AI is already widespread. But most use cases remain local. They work within specific teams, on specific workflows, under specific assumptions. What they rarely do is scale across: systems workflows decision ownership That is where scaling becomes difficult. ⸻ ⚖️ Not a regulatory issue The EU, under the EU AI Act, pushes for structured, risk-based deployment. The UK, guided by institutions such as the Financial Conduct Authority, takes a more principles-based approach. Different models. Same pattern. Even in more flexible environments, AI does not automatically scale. Because the constraint is not regulatory permission. It is operational integration. ⸻ 🧩 What this means for insurers Scaling AI is not about launching more use cases. It requires: • standardising workflows where decisions can be made • defining clear ownership of AI-driven decisions • designing systems to integrate, not isolate, AI capabilities Without this, AI remains a collection of initiatives rather than an operating capability. ⸻ 🧭 Key message AI does not fail at scale because of individual firms. It fails because large organisations are not designed to absorb it consistently. Shareholders are supreme under Japanese corporate law, but shareholders' economic interests are not. What is the distinction? Japan is (in)famous for its webs of cross-shareholdings. Suppliers, customers, financial institutions own shares in each other, and always support incumbent management with their votes. Even to the point of supporting decisions that result in transfers of value to the incumbent management or founding family, away from aligned and non-aligned shareholders alike. The larger the management-aligned bloc, the greater likelihood of value transfer, especially in a take-private deal situation. Three recent situations highlight this. [Steel Partners Holdings](https://www.linkedin.com/company/steel-partners?trk=public_post-text) recently approach Sanko Sangyo with an indicative offer at a significant premium to an agreed, but under-priced, management buyout. [Warren Lichtenstein](https://www.linkedin.com/in/warrenlichtenstein?trk=public_post-text) announced publicly that Steel's return to public market takeovers in Japan was not an activist play, and that Steel seeks a friendly deal. The Silent Supply Chain Shift That’s Making IT Maintenance Unaffordable What changed and why Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron make almost all the world's memory chips. In 2024-25 they shifted their factory capacity away from standard memory toward HBM the specialised chips inside AI servers and Nvidia GPUs. HBM is far more profitable per wafer. It was a rational business decision. Nobody asked the laptop industry. AI infrastructure projects are now consuming up to 40% of global DRAM output. That capacity had to come from somewhere. It came from the commodity memory that laptops, tablets, and phones run on. Why LPDDR specifically LPDDR is the memory soldered directly onto laptop motherboards. It was always considered boring cheap and abundant. Then Nvidia started putting LPDDR5X into AI servers alongside HBM. Suddenly the same chip that goes into a laptop is competing for fab capacity with AI infrastructure. Nvidia buys at a scale that dwarfs any laptop OEM. LPDDR lost that competition quietly and quickly. The numbers DRAM prices rose roughly 2X through 2025. LPDDR4 the memory in most refurbished and mid-range laptops is up 23-28%. Lead times for new LPDDR orders are now taking longer tpo be delivered and DDR4 production is projected to fall to 20% of 2025 levels in 2026. Tariffs on memory from Japan and South Korea added another 25% on top. Most laptops built between 2016 and 2022 run LPDDR4 or LPDDR4X. These are functional machines. Millions of them are still in daily use across businesses, schools, and households particularly in price-sensitive markets like India. They don't need replacing. They just occasionally need a memory repair or upgrade. That repair is about to become economically unviable. Not because the laptop failed But simply because the chips needed to fix them are no longer being manufactured at scale. The fabs

Lithium Ion Battery ICs Market Sizing and Forecast

Lithium Ion Battery ICs, or integrated circuits, are essential components designed to manage and control the charging, discharging, and overall functionality of lithium-ion batteries. Their importance lies in ensuring efficiency, safety, and longevity of batteries used in various applications, including consumer electronics, electric vehicles, and renewable energy storage systems. As the demand for these applications surges, the Lithium Ion Battery ICs market is projected to experience a significant Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) from 2026 to 2033.

Several trends are shaping this market's future growth, including the increasing adoption of electric vehicles, advancements in battery management systems, and the rising demand for efficient energy storage solutions. Additionally, the push for sustainable energy practices and regulations favoring cleaner technologies are propelling this market forward.

猿渡哲也氏は『高校鉄拳伝タフ』、『TOUGH-タフ-』などシリーズ作をヒットさせ、現在は『TOUGH 龍を継ぐ男』を連載中。シリーズ累計発行部数は1000万部を突破している。 『高校鉄拳伝タフ』や『TOUGH-タフ-』の主人公である宮沢熹一（キー坊）は、当初はお調子者だが、格闘家としてさまざまな死線をくぐり抜ける中で次第に成長を遂げていく。 迫力ある作画で描かれる格闘シーンは読者に強いインパクトをもたらし、また台詞もあわせたそのキャッチーさはたびたび読者のあいだで話題になる。キー坊の「ウ…ウソやろ こ…こんなことが こんなことが許されていいのか」の1コマを知っている人は多いことだろう。

『高校鉄拳伝タフ』

ほかにも、先日には所ジョージさんが司会を務める日テレのテレビ番組『1億人の大質問!?笑ってコラえて！』の街頭インタビューで偶然にも猿渡氏が登場したことでも話題に。なにかと『タフ』や猿渡氏自身への言及がネット上で増えていたタイミングでのアカウント開設となった形だ。 今後のコミックスの発刊やイベントの告知などの情報発信をX（Twitter）アカウントで行っていくとのことで、さっそく『ブラック・エンジェルズ』の作者・平松伸二氏と共同で行う握手会が開催される“ひめじSubかる☆フェスティバル”に関する投稿をリポスト（リツイート）している。

Nishikata Film Review: Renzo & Sayoko Kinoshita

This month marks the 12th anniversary of the passing of independent animation pioneer Renzō Kinoshita (木下蓮三, 3 Sept. 1936 - 15th January 1997). Kinoshita, along with his wife and collaborator Sayoko (木下小夜子, b. 1945) made a number of internationally acclaimed films together and were the founders of the biannual [Hiroshima International Animation Festival](http://hiroanim.org/).

Kinoshita grew up in Osaka where, according to [an interview](http://www.awn.com/mag/issue1.11/articles/deneroff-kinoshita1.11.html) with Sadao “Miyasan” Miyamoto (宮本貞雄), he did his training at a small animation studio. Later Miyamoto and Kinoshita would both work together on the Astro Boy series at Tezuka’s Mushi Productions in Tokyo. However, unlike Miyamoto, who would continue in mainstream animation (working on Gatchaman and later at Disney), Kinoshita grew weary of the constraints of commercial animation. He founded his own animation studio Studio Lotus (スタジオロータス) in 1967. I imagine that the name of the studio derives its inspiration from the kanji “ren” in his given name, which means “lotus”. In 1969 Sayako, whom Kinoshita had met at Mushi Pro, joined the production team at Studio Lotus, and they married soon after.

Kinoshita became well known in Japan in the late 1960s when he developed the character Geba Geba Ojisan (ゲバゲバおじさん) for the popular television series Kyosen x Maetake Geba-Geba 90-pun! ([巨泉×前武ゲバゲバ90分!](http://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%B7%A8%E6%B3%89%E3%83%BB%E5%89%8D%E6%AD%A6%E3%81%AE%E3%82%B2%E3%83%90%E3%82%B2%E3%83%9090%E5%88%86%21%21)) on Nippon Terebi. This included a series of comical shorts as well as creating comedic sequences in which Geba Geba Ojisan interacts with the actors themselves. The history of using live action people in combination with animated characters dates back to the earliest animated films with Winsor McCay’s early short film Gertie the Dinosaur (1914) showing McKay interacting with Gertie. The comedic possibilities of live animation together with animation were proven by the popularity of Disney’s Alice Comedies in the 1920s. In [this sequence](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9DWrBvwjmsA) from a Geba Geba Best Selection episode, Kinoshita has animated a dance sequence with the Geba Geba character and an actor in a style reminiscent of Gene Kelly dancing with Jerry Mouse in Anchors Aweigh (1945).

Along with his work for Nippon Terebi (ie. [Karikyura Machine](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yll5J_Ne3To)) in the 1970s, the Kinoshitas produced commercials in order to fund their most important work: the independent films. The first film that the couple made, with Sayako taken charge of the planning, scripting, animation, and production with Renzo did the key animation and direction, was Made in Japan. The film is a tongue-in-cheek criticism of post-war Japan and it won the Grand Prize at the New York International Film Festival. The Kinoshitas were buoyed by their success with the film

В 1928 году в сельской школе на острове Сёдо начинает работу учительница Хисако Оиси. Ее подопечные — двенадцать ребятишек из начального класса. Хисако проводит уроки на природе, вместо шаблонных гимнов разучивает с детьми народные песенки. Из-за шалости одного из ребят она получает травму, и ее переводят в другую школу. Но именно там она вновь встретится со своими повзрослевшими учениками, которых — вопреки нагнетанию военного кризиса — будет учить здравому смыслу, состраданию, надежде.

Ballad of Narayama

Баллада о Нараяме | Легенда о Нараяме 1958 Keisuke Kinoshita 木下 恵介 98 min, Color (Fujicolor) [IMDB](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0051980/)

Экранизация новеллы Ситиро Фукадзавы «Сказ о горе Нараяма» (по-русски она опубликована в сборнике «Мир по-японски» под редакцией Г.Чхартишвили, СПб., 2000). Один из самых значительных "сенситивных" японских фильмов, признанных международной "арт хауз" общественностью в начале 60-х годов. Изысканно стилизованная картина о старинной традиции избавляться от стариков. Тех, кто не может уже работать и приносить пользу, относили на гору Нараяма, где души их вскоре отлетали к богам. Одна из женщин, уже под семьдесят лет, стоически принимает свою судьбу, выживая только на одной силе духа. Другая жертва противится смерти от рук жестокого сына, который убивает старика и сам, в свою очередь, становится жертвой убийства. Технически картина снята таким образом (завораживающая операторская работа), что зритель дистанциируется от дикой жестокости драмы — этому способствуют осторожный, театрализованный выбор натуры, освещения и авторский текст Кабуки. В отличие от римейка 1983 года, снятого Имамурой, ставящего ударение как на жестокости обычаев, так и на связи человека с природой, используя безжалостный реализм, Киношита располагает свою ленту в царстве фантазии. (М. Иванов)

Nishikata Film Review: Mabo as Tokichiro Kinoshita (マー坊の木下藤吉郎, 1938)

The early animated talkie Mabo as Tokichiro Kinoshita (マー坊の木下藤吉郎, Mābō no Kinoshita Tōkichirō, 1938) brings together a popular cartoon figure of the 1930s, the young boy hero Mābō (マー坊), and a legendary figure of Japanese history, Toyotomi Hideyoshi (1537-1598), who in his youth was known by the name Tōkichirō Kinoshita.

Hideyoshi was a great daimyō of the turbulent Sengoku Period. He was the second of three men known as the great “Unifiers of Japan” because of his role in bringing peace and stability to the region which was at the time in a state of constant civil war. Hideyoshi was an ideal hero for Japanese propaganda of the Fifteen Years’ War (1931-45), not only for his role as a unifier but also because of his hard stance against foreign religion. He notoriously ordered the execution by crucifixion of 26 Christians in Nagasaki in 1597, known as [The 26 Martyrs of Japan](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twenty-six_Martyrs_of_Japan).

As this is propaganda aimed at children, the unknown filmmakers have chosen to focus on the adventures of Hideyoshi’s youth when he was known as Tōkichirō Kinoshita. He is played by Mābō – a popular figure of the time in comics and animated shorts. It is not clear how many Mābō films were made – at least one other film that survives, [Mābō’s Big Race](http://nishikataeiga.blogspot.de/2014/03/mabos-big-race-1936.html)(1936) shows Mābō competing for Japan at the Berlin Olympics. The makers of both films are currently uncertain, but it is clear that Mabo as Tokichiro Kinoshita is a more sophisticated short (either a different animator, or the animator has improved his skills in the intervening years) than the earlier film. Mābō looks very different in 16th century garb, but his face is recognisable with his snub nose, big ears, large eyes, slightly jutting chin and small, high eyebrows.

Having popular cartoon characters take on other roles is something that began early in animation. For example, Oswald the Lucky Rabbit, the precursor to Mickey Mouse, often takes on other roles. He’s a fire fighter in Fiery Fireman (1928) and in Yanky Clippers (1929) he’s a barber. One of my favourite examples of a popular cartoon character taking on another role is the much later film Mickey and the Beanstalk (1947) with Mickey replaying Jack in the famous English fairy tale. It’s a clever way to bring together the popularity of a character with a proven storyline. In the case of Hideyoshi, his exploits as a child had taken on a legendary status through retellings over the centuries.

Summary of Mabo as Tokishiro Kinoshita:

Part I – In Search of a Lord to Serve

A beautifully painted intertitle tells us that Mābō /Kinoshita is in search of a lord to serve. He encounters a fortune teller who offers to tell Mābō’s fortune using a mirror. ### أفضل أطباء أشعة تداخلية فى مصر : دكتور محمود عب

TDK Corporation is a world leader in electronic solutions for the smart society based in Tokyo, Japan. Built on a foundation of material sciences mastery, TDK welcomes societal transformation by resolutely remaining at the forefront of technological evolution. It was established in 1935 to commercialize ferrite, a key material in electronic and magnetic products. TDK’s comprehensive, innovation-driven portfolio features passive components such as ceramic, aluminum electrolytic and film capacitors, as well as magnetics, high-frequency, and piezo and protection devices. The product spectrum also includes sensors and sensor systems such as temperature and pressure, magnetic, and MEMS sensors. In addition, TDK provides power supplies and energy devices, magnetic heads, software and more. These products are marketed under the product brands TDK, EPCOS, InvenSense, Micronas, Tronics, and TDK-Lambda. I’m delighted to share that our paper, “KAN-Refine: Compact KAN-Based Appearance Modeling for 3D Gaussian Splatting,” has been accepted at the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition ([#CVPR2026](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcvpr2026&trk=public_post-text)) Workshop on Generative 3D Reconstruction (GenRecon3D)! 💫🎉 Our work explores compact KAN-based appearance modeling for 3D Gaussian Splatting, with the goal of improving efficient and expressive 3D scene representation. Grateful to my wonderful co-authors [Sanjan Baitalik](https://in.linkedin.com/in/sanjanbaitalik?trk=public_post-text), [Ritankar Mondal](https://in.linkedin.com/in/ritankar-mondal-910257365?trk=public_post-text) and mentors [Dr. Amit Kumar Das](https://in.linkedin.com/in/dr-amit-kumar-das?trk=public_post-text) and Dr. [Sruti Das Choudhury](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sruti-das-choudhury-67b20610a?trk=public_post-text) for their support throughout this work. Looking forward to sharing more details about the paper soon! Vietnam has taken a major step toward modernizing its power market with Circular 62, introducing the country’s first pricing and PPA framework for battery energy storage systems (BESS). The new rules bring a capacity-based revenue model that aligns Vietnam more closely with global best practices and offers clearer signals for investors and lenders. It’s also a strong signal of momentum toward PDP8, which targets up to 16.3 GW of battery storage by 2030. Heavy industry is still one of the least automated sectors in the world — but that’s starting to change. [KEWAZO](https://de.linkedin.com/company/kewazo?trk=public_post-text) has raised a $16M Series A extension, bringing total funding to $35M, to bring [#robotics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frobotics&trk=public_post-text) into some of the toughest industrial environments. The round was led by [Schooner Capital](https://www.linkedin.com/company/schooner-capital?trk=public_post-text), with backing from [Chevron Technology Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/chevron-technology-ventures/?trk=public_post-text) and [Asahi Kasei](https://jp.linkedin.com/company/asahi-kasei?trk=public_post-text), among others. Instead of focusing on factory automation, KEWAZO is tackling a much harder problem: manual lifting and logistics in places like refineries, chemical plants, and power facilities. Its robot, LIFTBOT, replaces cranes and manual handling — helping teams work safer, faster, and more efficiently. Already live at 20+ industrial sites across North America and Europe, the product is proving that robotics can work even in complex, high-risk environments. Led by [Artem Kuchukov](https://de.linkedin.com/in/artem-kuchukov?trk=public_post-text), the company is now scaling operations and product development to meet growing demand. The bigger picture is clear: Automation is moving beyond controlled environments like warehouses… → into real-world, unpredictable industrial settings. And that’s where the next wave of impact will be. # Kenji Kawamura

AIの進化を支える『数理的DB設計』と、組織を守る『20年のPostgreSQL深層知見』。 Oracleの遺産を未来の武器に変える、CTOの戦略的技術パートナー。

フリーランスエンジニア コンサルタント at 自営業

Tokyo, Tokyo, Japan (JP)

500 connections • 796 followers

## About

■ 華やかなAIモデルの裏側で、泥臭い「データ基盤」を支える専門家 「最新のLLMを導入したが、期待した精度が出ない」 「RAG（検索拡張生成）を構築したいが、社内データがOracleやレガシーシステムに散らばっており、使い物にならない」 現在、多くの企業のDX/AIプロジェクトが直面している壁は、AIモデルの選定ミスではなく、「学習・検索させるためのデータが整っていない」という物理的な問題です。 私は、流行のAIモデルをファインチューニングするAIエンジニアではありません。 しかし、AIがその能力を最大限に発揮するために不可欠な「堅牢なデータベース」と「数学的に正しいデータ構造」を用意する、データ基盤構築のプロフェッショナルです。 30年にわたるエンジニアリング経験と、20年以上のPostgreSQL研究開発（C言語レベルでの内部解析）の知見、そして数学修士としてのバックグラウンドを武器に、貴社のAIプロジェクトを「PoC（概念実証）」の壁を超え、「実運用」へと導くための技術支援を行います。 ■ 提供価値：なぜ私がAI時代に必要なのか 私の強みは、「従来のRDBMS技術（守りのIT）」と「最新のベクトル検索技術（攻めのIT）」の両方を、深いレベルで接続できる点にあります。 1. 堅牢なベクトルデータベース設計（PostgreSQL / pgvector） 若手のAIエンジニアはライブラリを使って「とりあえず動くRAG」を作ることはできますが、「データ量が数億件に増えた時のインデックス設計」や「メモリ効率を考慮したスケーラビリティ」の設計には、RDBMSの深い知見が不可欠です。 私はPostgreSQLの研究開発・コンサルティングに20年以上従事し、C言語レベルでDBMSの挙動を理解しています。その知見に基づき、pgvector等を用いた高速かつ破綻しないベクトル検索基盤を設計します。 2. レガシーとAIの「架け橋」（データクレンジング・正規化） AIにデータを食わせる前の「前処理」こそが、私の真骨頂です。 これまでにOracleからPostgreSQLへの移行案件を100件以上経験してきました。古いシステムに塩漬けにされた「汚れたデータ」を解析し、AIが活用しやすい形にクレンジング・正規化・統合するプロセスにおいて、圧倒的な経験値を誇ります。「移行」という泥臭い作業を、「AI活用のための資産化」へと昇華させます。 3. 数学的素養に基づく精度チューニング 数学専攻（修士）にて代数幾何学を専攻。ベクトル検索の根幹である「高次元空間における距離計算」や「インデックスアルゴリズム（HNSW等）」の挙動を、ブラックボックスとしてではなく、数理的なロジックとして理解しています。エンジニアリングと数学の両面から、精度のボトルネックを特定し、最適解を導出します。 ■ 略歴 【数学への没頭と、ITへの「途中下車」】 私のエンジニアとしての原点は、13歳の時に遡ります。当時、8ビットマイコンの機械語を駆使して円周率1000桁の計算プログラムを組んだ経験が、数学の美しさに魅了されたルーツです。 その後、早稲田大学理工学部数学科へ進学。同大学院修士課程では代数幾何学を研究しました。博士課程への進学も考えましたが、より実社会に近い領域で数理的思考を活かすべく、IT業界へと転身（本人としては「途中下車」のつもりでした）。以来30年、技術の本質を追求し続けています。 【PostgreSQLの深淵へ】 新卒で入社した日立系列のソフトウェア会社、およびその後の株式会社図研では、CAD幾何演算ライブラリの開発（C++）やコンパイラ構造の理解を通じ、低レイヤー技術を習得。 2006年からはNTTグループ（NTT持株研究所、NTTデータ等）にて、PostgreSQLの研究開発およびエンタープライズ領域への適用支援に約20年間従事しました。 ここでは、単なる利用者の枠を超え、DBMSの内部構造に深く踏み込んだ活動を行いました。 ・OSS開発: PostgreSQLの周辺ツールである db_syntax_diff や orafce の開発、dblink_plus の維持管理に貢献。 ・高難度トラブルシューティング: C言語ソースコードの解析によるバグ特定や、オプティマイザの挙動解析によるSQLチューニングを実施。 ・大規模移行: Oracle等の商用DBからPostgreSQLへの移行において、技術的難易度の高い案件を100件以上リード。 現在は、これらの経験を「生成AI時代」に合わせて再定義し、PostgreSQLの拡張機能（pgvector）を用いたAIデータ基盤の構築支援や、企業の技術顧問として活動しています。 ■ 私のスタンスと働き方 「創造的なサボり」の追求 私は、無駄な長時間労働や、非効率な単純作業を良しとしません。深い知識と洞察に基づく「正しい初期設計」こそが、後の手戻りを防ぎ、プロジェクト全体の工数を劇的に削減すると信じているからです。 技術顧問（Technical Advisor）としての関わり 単なる「作業リソース」としてではなく、貴社のエンジニアチームを支える「技術の参謀」として参画します。 ・若手エンジニアが書いたSQLやDB設計のレビュー ・解決困難なパフォーマンス問題の火消し ・AI導入を見据えたデータ戦略の壁打ち これらを通じ、貴社の組織全体の技術力を底上げすることにコミットします。 ■ ターゲットとするパートナー 「AIを導入したいが、既存のレガシーデータが足かせになっている」 「若手のAIエンジニアはいるが、データベース設計に不安がある」 「Oracleからの脱却とAI活用を同時に進めたい」 こうした課題をお持ちのCTO、経営層の皆様。 貴社のデータ資産を、AI時代の新たな武器へと変えるお手伝いをいたします。ぜひカジュアルにお話ししましょう。

How AI Structuring Unlocked a Major Deal 🚀 A startup selling an impressive AI platform into pharma kept hearing the same polite feedback: “Interesting tech, let us think about where this might fit.” No follow-up. No concrete next step. ⏱ In the first 10 minutes of their pitch, pharma stakeholders still couldn’t answer a basic question: What exact problem does this solve in my world? Was it about patient stratification, biomarker discovery, trial enrichment, or something else entirely? The platform sounded powerful, but the value was abstract. 🧩 We worked with their team to reframe the conversation from “here is our AI” to “here is the specific decision we de-risk for you.” We picked one priority use case, mapped it to the pharma workflow, and rebuilt the deck so that slide 1 already made the business case crystal clear. Within a few meetings, the conversation shifted from demo features to deal structure. ❗ For B2B sales into pharma, a great AI/data platform is not enough. If leadership cannot immediately see whether you’re solving patient stratification, biomarker selection, or another concrete decision, the opportunity quietly dies in the funnel. 📩 If you’re preparing for pharma talks and want your AI story to land in the first 10 minutes, DM me — at [Beyer Consulting](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/beyer-consulting-eu?trk=public_post-text), we help teams turn complex platforms into clear, partner-ready use cases. At WEP, we support Sponsors and CRO partners with end-to-end clinical supply chain management, helping ensure investigational products reach patients on time and within specification. ## About

Dr. Prakash Chaki obtained his master's degree in electrical engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Bombay, and Ph.D. degree in information and communication engineering from The University of Tokyo. A senior researcher in wireless communications, Dr. Chaki is presently working on 5G-Advanced and 6G, at NEC laboratories, Greater Tokyo area. As a 3GPP RAN1 delegate for channel coding in the standardization of 5G new radio (NR), Dr. Chaki has actively contributed to RAN1 for 5G control channel coding scheme. He has many patented inventions covering diverse areas of WLAN, coding theory and MIMO communications, most of which have already been granted in USA, Japan, or Europe. With demonstrated skills in inventing and patenting, Dr. Chaki is a recipient of NEC Young Researcher Award in 2019, two-time winner of NEC Excellent Invention award in 2015 and 2018, NEC Patent Award for contribution in terms of large number of issued patents in 2021, and Young Researcher Award from the Institute of Electronics, Information and Communication Engineers (IEICE) in 2017. In 2023, he was recognized for Notable Services and Contributions towards the advancement of IEEE and the Engineering Professions. Dr. Chaki has served as an Executive Committee (EXCOM) member of IEEE Japan Council, as well as an EXCOM member of IEEE Tokyo Section. He has also served as the Chair of IEEE Tokyo Section Young Professionals, and as the Chair of IEEE Tokyo Section Educational Activities. He currently serves as an Editor for IEEE CTN, IEEE WCL, and IEEE OJVT, and also as a technical reviewer for many esteemed journals including IEEE Transactions on Comm., IEEE Transactions on Wireless Comm., IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Tech., IEEE Journal on Selected Topics in Signal Processing, IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Comm., IEEE Wireless Comm. Letters, IEEE Signal Processing Letters, IEEE OJCOMS, IEEE OJVT, Elsevier Physical Communications, IET, ETRI, Wiley, and IEICE journals. An avid sports enthusiast, public speaker, and a member of Toastmasters International where he also served as a Secretary, Dr. Chaki communicates in 4 languages (English, Japanese, Hindi and Bengali). In his time away from work, he is found reading, sketching, volunteering or doing sports. He is passionate about team building, with recognized leadership across diverse roles. He is an active blood donor; if you are in Tokyo area and in need of blood for medical emergency, do reach out. Dr. Chaki held a Guinness world record. Website: https://www.nec.com/en/global/rd/people/prakash_chaki.html

## About us

 

The Business Without Barriers Summit and Careers Fair is where businesses of all sizes go to engage with highly talented disabled jobseekers. Whether you are an accessibility and inclusion professional, in charge of talent and recruitment, or an individual seeking work, join us at Business without Barriers as we seek to create inclusive and accessible work opportunities and environments for the disabled community. The event is a vital platform where professionals can explore best practices and actionable steps for building inclusive organisations. Attendees can learn about Access to Work, discover solutions from assistive tech companies, and gain valuable insights from an interactive conference programme. The dedicated careers fair also provides unique opportunities to network with potential candidates and connect with diverse talent. Business Without Barriers takes place at the NEC Birmingham on the 25th March - 26th March 2026 alongside Naidex – the UK’s largest event for the disability community creating a wider platform for collaboration.

# Kenji Kawamura

AIの進化を支える『数理的DB設計』と、組織を守る『20年のPostgreSQL深層知見』。 Oracleの遺産を未来の武器に変える、CTOの戦略的技術パートナー。

フリーランスエンジニア コンサルタント at 自営業

Tokyo, Tokyo, Japan (JP)

500 connections • 796 followers

## About

■ 華やかなAIモデルの裏側で、泥臭い「データ基盤」を支える専門家 「最新のLLMを導入したが、期待した精度が出ない」 「RAG（検索拡張生成）を構築したいが、社内データがOracleやレガシーシステムに散らばっており、使い物にならない」 現在、多くの企業のDX/AIプロジェクトが直面している壁は、AIモデルの選定ミスではなく、「学習・検索させるためのデータが整っていない」という物理的な問題です。 私は、流行のAIモデルをファインチューニングするAIエンジニアではありません。 しかし、AIがその能力を最大限に発揮するために不可欠な「堅牢なデータベース」と「数学的に正しいデータ構造」を用意する、データ基盤構築のプロフェッショナルです。 30年にわたるエンジニアリング経験と、20年以上のPostgreSQL研究開発（C言語レベルでの内部解析）の知見、そして数学修士としてのバックグラウンドを武器に、貴社のAIプロジェクトを「PoC（概念実証）」の壁を超え、「実運用」へと導くための技術支援を行います。 ■ 提供価値：なぜ私がAI時代に必要なのか 私の強みは、「従来のRDBMS技術（守りのIT）」と「最新のベクトル検索技術（攻めのIT）」の両方を、深いレベルで接続できる点にあります。 1. 堅牢なベクトルデータベース設計（PostgreSQL / pgvector） 若手のAIエンジニアはライブラリを使って「とりあえず動くRAG」を作ることはできますが、「データ量が数億件に増えた時のインデックス設計」や「メモリ効率を考慮したスケーラビリティ」の設計には、RDBMSの深い知見が不可欠です。 私はPostgreSQLの研究開発・コンサルティングに20年以上従事し、C言語レベルでDBMSの挙動を理解しています。その知見に基づき、pgvector等を用いた高速かつ破綻しないベクトル検索基盤を設計します。 2. レガシーとAIの「架け橋」（データクレンジング・正規化） AIにデータを食わせる前の「前処理」こそが、私の真骨頂です。 これまでにOracleからPostgreSQLへの移行案件を100件以上経験してきました。古いシステムに塩漬けにされた「汚れたデータ」を解析し、AIが活用しやすい形にクレンジング・正規化・統合するプロセスにおいて、圧倒的な経験値を誇ります。「移行」という泥臭い作業を、「AI活用のための資産化」へと昇華させます。 3. 数学的素養に基づく精度チューニング 数学専攻（修士）にて代数幾何学を専攻。ベクトル検索の根幹である「高次元空間における距離計算」や「インデックスアルゴリズム（HNSW等）」の挙動を、ブラックボックスとしてではなく、数理的なロジックとして理解しています。エンジニアリングと数学の両面から、精度のボトルネックを特定し、最適解を導出します。 ■ 略歴 【数学への没頭と、ITへの「途中下車」】 私のエンジニアとしての原点は、13歳の時に遡ります。当時、8ビットマイコンの機械語を駆使して円周率1000桁の計算プログラムを組んだ経験が、数学の美しさに魅了されたルーツです。 その後、早稲田大学理工学部数学科へ進学。同大学院修士課程では代数幾何学を研究しました。博士課程への進学も考えましたが、より実社会に近い領域で数理的思考を活かすべく、IT業界へと転身（本人としては「途中下車」のつもりでした）。以来30年、技術の本質を追求し続けています。 【PostgreSQLの深淵へ】 新卒で入社した日立系列のソフトウェア会社、およびその後の株式会社図研では、CAD幾何演算ライブラリの開発（C++）やコンパイラ構造の理解を通じ、低レイヤー技術を習得。 2006年からはNTTグループ（NTT持株研究所、NTTデータ等）にて、PostgreSQLの研究開発およびエンタープライズ領域への適用支援に約20年間従事しました。 ここでは、単なる利用者の枠を超え、DBMSの内部構造に深く踏み込んだ活動を行いました。 ・OSS開発: PostgreSQLの周辺ツールである db_syntax_diff や orafce の開発、dblink_plus の維持管理に貢献。 ・高難度トラブルシューティング: C言語ソースコードの解析によるバグ特定や、オプティマイザの挙動解析によるSQLチューニングを実施。 ・大規模移行: Oracle等の商用DBからPostgreSQLへの移行において、技術的難易度の高い案件を100件以上リード。 現在は、これらの経験を「生成AI時代」に合わせて再定義し、PostgreSQLの拡張機能（pgvector）を用いたAIデータ基盤の構築支援や、企業の技術顧問として活動しています。 ■ 私のスタンスと働き方 「創造的なサボり」の追求 私は、無駄な長時間労働や、非効率な単純作業を良しとしません。深い知識と洞察に基づく「正しい初期設計」こそが、後の手戻りを防ぎ、プロジェクト全体の工数を劇的に削減すると信じているからです。 技術顧問（Technical Advisor）としての関わり 単なる「作業リソース」としてではなく、貴社のエンジニアチームを支える「技術の参謀」として参画します。 ・若手エンジニアが書いたSQLやDB設計のレビュー ・解決困難なパフォーマンス問題の火消し ・AI導入を見据えたデータ戦略の壁打ち これらを通じ、貴社の組織全体の技術力を底上げすることにコミットします。 ■ ターゲットとするパートナー 「AIを導入したいが、既存のレガシーデータが足かせになっている」 「若手のAIエンジニアはいるが、データベース設計に不安がある」 「Oracleからの脱却とAI活用を同時に進めたい」 こうした課題をお持ちのCTO、経営層の皆様。 貴社のデータ資産を、AI時代の新たな武器へと変えるお手伝いをいたします。ぜひカジュアルにお話ししましょう。

# MediaPerf: Open Benchmark for AI Video Understanding in Media Industry

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

 

 

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/codyaustun?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Cody Coleman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/codyaustun?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 

 5d 

 

- [ Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fcodyaustun_today-were-launching-mediaperf-the-first-activity-7442232252714917888-WB8a&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 
- 

 

Today, we’re launching MediaPerf, the first open benchmark for AI video understanding in the media industry. We built this at [Coactive AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/coactiveai?trk=public_post-text) because we kept running into the same problem with our customers: the benchmarks available for video AI don’t reflect how media teams actually use these models. Most benchmarks test short clips on generic tasks. Media production teams need to evaluate models on content tagging, summarization, and tag refinement across real video content. And they need to see quality, cost, and latency together, because in production, you can’t optimize for just one. MediaPerf v. 2026.02 benchmarks 8 multimodal models on 2,003 ad videos with real media industry tasks. The governance board includes leaders from [Amazon Web Services (AWS)](https://www.linkedin.com/company/amazon-web-services?trk=public_post-text), [Business Insider](https://www.linkedin.com/company/businessinsider?trk=public_post-text), [Harvard University](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-university/?trk=public_post-text), [Georgia Institute of Technology](https://www.linkedin.com/school/georgia-institute-of-technology/?trk=public_post-text), [NBCUniversal](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nbcuniversal-inc-?trk=public_post-text), and [NVIDIA](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nvidia?trk=public_post-text). The codebase is open source under Apache 2.0. The evaluation data is CC-BY. This is v1. The benchmark will expand to more models, more tasks, and more data. If you work in media or AI and want to get involved, check it out: [https://mediaperf.org/](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmediaperf%2Eorg%2F&urlhash=D-NV&trk=public_post-text)

There's a hidden feature on Microsoft Copilot that NO ONE is talking about 👇 If you’re using MS Copilot on a daily basis and you’re not using the / trick, you’re making life wayyyy harder than it needs to be. Here's how to do it: 1. Go to your Copilot chatbox 2. Type a forward slash ( / ) 3. A menu will pop up which will let you select a specific Sharepoint site, list or file. 4. Copilots entire focus will be locked on that particular site, doc or folder! My dad was just featured in the Washington Post for a death penalty case from 1985… But the most important thing he’s ever taught me has nothing to do with the law. He is one of fewer than 20 attorneys qualified to take on capital cases in Utah and has decades of trial experience. He represents people in high-stakes life-or-death decisions. By any measure, he’s had an incredible career. But at my brother’s wedding, when he stood up to give a toast… He didn’t talk about success. He didn’t talk about ambition. He didn’t talk about winning. He shared just one piece of advice: “Spend time with the people you love.” ❤️ Then, holding back tears, he told a story. At his own wedding to my mom (they’re now happily divorced), the room was filled with parents, grandparents, and family. Now, 30+ years later, he looked around and said: "My sweet mom is the only one left" The lesson has stuck with me. Success matters, and it’s meaningful to climb the career ladder. But nothing matters more than the people you love and the time you get with them. My dad has taught me this time and time again. — Article in the comments if you’re curious about the case

- [ Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fjennyzhangzt_github-facebookresearchhyperagents-self-referential-activity-7441867823716483072-8BMB&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 
- 

 

Introducing Hyperagents: an AI system that not only improves at solving tasks, but also improves how it improves itself. The Darwin Gödel Machine (DGM) demonstrated that open-ended self-improvement is possible by iteratively generating and evaluating improved agents, yet it relies on a key assumption: that improvements in task performance (e.g., coding ability) translate into improvements in the self-improvement process itself. This alignment holds in coding, where both evaluation and modification are expressed in the same domain, but breaks down more generally. As a result, prior systems remain constrained by fixed, handcrafted meta-level procedures that do not themselves evolve. We introduce Hyperagents – self-referential agents that can modify both their task-solving behavior and the process that generates future improvements. This enables what we call metacognitive self-modification: learning not just to perform better, but to improve at improving. We instantiate this framework as DGM-Hyperagents (DGM-H), an extension of the DGM in which both task-solving behavior and the self-improvement procedure are editable and subject to evolution. Across diverse domains (coding, paper review, robotics reward design, and Olympiad-level math solution grading), hyperagents enable continuous performance improvements over time and outperform baselines without self-improvement or open-ended exploration, as well as prior self-improving systems (including DGM). DGM-H also improves the process by which new agents are generated (e.g. persistent memory, performance tracking), and these meta-level improvements transfer across domains and accumulate across runs. Eli Lilly & Insilico Medicine: A $2.75B AI Deal… Or the Early Signals of a Much Bigger Shift?

` `` `` `` `` `` `` ` 

 

 ` `` `` `` `

 

 

 

 

It has never been a secret that I admire what [Alex Zhavoronkov](https://www.linkedin.com/in/zhavoronkov?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention) has built with [Insilico Medicine](https://www.linkedin.com/company/in-silico-medicine?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention) . Long before “AI in drug discovery” became a crowded space, he was already operating at that intersection. For more than a decade, Insilico has been pushing a vision that many in the industry initially dismissed, then cautiously observed, and are now actively trying to understand. 

 

We still do not have a fully AI-designed drug on the market. 

 

Yet, if one had to place a bet on who might get there first, Insilico would be among the most credible contenders. Not because of storytelling, but because of delivery. 

 

## From concept to clinical reality 

 

source: In-silico Medicine website 

 

What sets Insilico apart is not simply its positioning as an “AI-first” biotech. It is the consistency with which the company has translated that positioning into tangible assets. 

 

Today, the company has built a pipeline of more than 40 programs spanning fibrosis, oncology, immunology, and metabolic diseases. Its lead asset in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, INS018_055, has progressed into clinical development, with timelines from target identification to IND that are significantly shorter than industry norms. 

 

> In several cases, Insilico has demonstrated timelines of less than 18 months from concept to candidate. 

 

In a field where timelines are measured in years and attrition remains the dominant force, this is not a marginal improvement. It suggests a different way of organizing discovery itself. 

 

Equally important, and often overlooked, is the role Alex has played in educating the industry. He has consistently invested in explaining what AI-driven drug discovery actually entails, beyond the marketing layer. That effort has helped shape a more informed, and more realistic, understanding of the field.

# FDA Approves Eli Lilly's Foundayo for Weight Loss

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

1w

The US Food and Drug Administration approved the second GLP-1 pill for weight loss, adding another option to a rapidly growing arsenal of obesity therapies. The orforglipron pill, called Foundayo, is made by Eli Lilly, which also sells the GLP-1 drugs Zepbound for obesity and Mounjaro for diabetes. Those are given as weekly injections and, along with rival drugs Wegovy and Ozempic, have transformed weight-loss and diabetes treatment, with millions of people taking the medicines. Foundayo is coming to market just months after the FDA approved the pill form of Novo Nordisk’s injectable obesity drug Wegovy. Both pills offer a new way to take GLP-1 medicines, so named for the hormone they mimic that’s important for appetite, digestion and insulin regulation. But Lilly says Foundayo represents an advance because, unlike the Wegovy pill, it can be taken at any time of day and without restrictions on food and water. The legacy of Pat Summitt continues to inspire generations - on and off the court. Excited to announce that today, Sunday, March 29 at 7 p.m. ET / 4 p.m. PT on ESPN2, premieres “Celebrating Pat Summitt: Live Reunion Special”presented by Eli Lilly and Company, streaming available on the ESPN App and Disney+. This marks Lilly’s third major project following the mission of the Annenberg study released last year—highlighting a continued commitment to advancing cultural storytelling and course correcting disease depiction in entertainment. Produced by [Omaha Productions](https://www.linkedin.com/company/omaha-productions?trk=public_post-text), the special features [Nikki Fargas](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nikki-fargas-8b68021ba?trk=public_post-text) and [Andraya Carter](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andraya-carter-938a9a1bb?trk=public_post-text) alongside Candance Parker and Dawn Staley, in a conversation moderated by [ESPN](https://www.linkedin.com/company/espn?trk=public_post-text)‘s Holly Rowe. Through personal reflections, archival moments, and powerful storytelling, the program honors Coach Summitt’s extraordinary legacy as an eight-time national champion and a pioneering force in women’s sports—while also shedding light and spreading brain health awareness through her courageous battle with Alzheimer’s disease. A meaningful tribute to a true trailblazer—and a powerful example of storytelling driving both cultural impact and health awareness. When storytelling and entertainment take the time to build accurate portrayals of disease, stories get elevated while we help increase awareness of early symptoms recognition. So grateful for [Laura Steele](https://www.linkedin.com/in/laura-steele-4198435?trk=public_post-text)’s partnership in this project to help raise the depiction of Alzheimer’s through the powerful story of Coach Summit. Eli Lilly’s next-generation obesity drug retatrutide clears first late-stage diabetes trial Eli Lilly said its next-generation obesity drug retatutride cleared its first late-stage trial on Type 2 diabetes patients, helping them manage their blood sugar levels and lose weight. The drug lowered hemoglobin A1C by an average of 1.7% to 2% across different doses at 40 weeks compared to placebo, and helped patients lose an average of 16.8% of their weight. Lilly is betting big on retatrutide as the next pillar of its obesity portfolio after its blockbuster weight loss injection Zepbound and its upcoming pill, orforglipron. Lilly’s EBGLYSS® delivers breakthrough Phase III results in pediatric atopic dermatitis On March 18, 2026, Eli Lilly announced that EBGLYSS® (lebrikizumab‑lbkz), the first and only selective IL‑13 inhibitor, achieved positive Phase III outcomes in patients aged six months to 18 years with moderate‑to‑severe atopic dermatitis. 📊 Key Findings - Product: EBGLYSS® (lebrikizumab‑lbkz) - Company: Eli Lilly - Indication: Pediatric patients (6 months–18 years) with moderate‑to‑severe atopic dermatitis - Trial outcome: Phase III studies met primary endpoints, showing significant improvements in skin clearance and symptom relief - Milestone: First IL‑13 selective inhibitor to demonstrate efficacy in this broad pediatric age group 🔬 Clinical & Scientific Context - Mechanism: Lebrikizumab targets IL‑13, a central cytokine driving type 2 inflammation in atopic dermatitis. - Significance: Expands treatment options for younger patients, who often face limited therapeutic choices. - Comparison: Builds on EBGLYSS’s prior success in adults, now extending efficacy and safety data to children and adolescents. ## 

 

India’s GLP-1 and GIP market is no longer an emerging story. It is now a live, fast-expanding therapeutic and commercial category. Over the past year, India has seen Eli Lilly launch tirzepatide, Novo Nordisk launch Wegovy and then Ozempic, and semaglutide move off patent in March 2026—opening the door to a wave of lower-cost Indian brands. In the public reports available today, the market is described in terms of launches, sales, pricing, access, and market size, rather than a verified nationwide count of unique doctors who have written at least one GLP-1 or GIP prescription. So while the exact number of Indian physicians, diabetologists, and endocrinologists who have prescribed at least one incretin therapy is not publicly published, the adoption pattern itself is already visible—and it is unmistakably urban-first, specialist-led, and now beginning to broaden. 

 

The timeline of this shift is important. Eli Lilly launched Mounjaro in India in March 2025. Novo Nordisk launched Wegovy in June 2025, and Ozempic followed in December 2025. Then, in March 2026, Novo’s semaglutide patent expired in India, triggering expectations of a major generic wave. Reuters reported that more than 40 Indian companies are expected to launch over 50 semaglutide brands, while CareEdge notes that a first wave of launches is expected around March 2026 to Q1 FY27, with the bulk of launches anticipated in H2 FY27. 

 

This is why India’s incretin market has moved so quickly from niche to strategic. CareEdge estimates the Indian GLP-1 market at roughly ₹1,000–1,200 crore in 2025 and projects it could rise nearly fivefold to about ₹4,500–5,000 crore by 2030. The same report estimates that 60–70% of demand will come from type 2 diabetes, with the remainder from weight-loss use. Even more striking, CareEdge says current penetration remains negligible despite the enormous addressable population, and may still only rise to around 1% by 2030. Reuters separately reports that India’s obesity-drug market could grow from about ₹15 billion today to ₹80 billion by 2030. 

 

That combination—huge disease burden, low current penetration, and falling prices—is what makes the prescriber question so important. The real issue is not whether the class will grow in India. It will. The more important question is who is driving today’s prescriptions, and which segments of India

Thank you to [Ketjen Corporation](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ketjencorp?trk=public_post-text) for sponsoring today’s luncheon at the [American Fuel & Petrochemical Manufacturers (AFPM)](https://www.linkedin.com/company/american-fuel-&-petrochemical-manufacturers-afpm-?trk=public_post-text). Conference. It was energizing to be in a room with oil and gas industry leaders navigating complexity yet still pushing progress forward. We discussed three ways AI is changing leadership today: • Human judgment becomes a safety skill in an AI-enabled workplace. • Work must be redesigned for learning, not just efficiency. • Leadership development must evolve for human + AI teams. I shared a lot of what [Christie Smith Ph.D](https://www.linkedin.com/in/christiesmithphd?trk=public_post-text) and I wrote in Essential. And it was great to surface the work of [Mike Wirth](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mike-wirth-chevron?trk=public_post-text), CEO of Chevron, who wrote, “Essential provides actionable insights and a roadmap to reengage and empower our most vital resource: people." So many leaders I spoke to echoed this sentiment today. “At the end of the day we are in the people business.” Coming from tech it’s a breath of fresh air to see leaders doubling down on this mindset. Grateful to do this work and thankful for my [Leading Authorities, Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/leading-authorities-inc-speakers-bureau?trk=public_post-text) team who make opportunities like this possible. # Kelly Monahan, Ph.D.’s Post

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kelly-monahan-ph-d-18879413?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Kelly Monahan, Ph.D.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kelly-monahan-ph-d-18879413?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

- [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fkelly-monahan-ph-d-18879413_what-executives-can-learn-from-their-digital-activity-7441463521856471040-RErR&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I have been SO skeptical of using AI agents as respondents in research. However, [Francis Hintermann](https://www.linkedin.com/in/francis-hintermann-243a9a1?trk=public_post-text) just changed my mind on this topic. He writes, “At first, you might assume the goal of synthetic agents is to replicate human answers as closely as possible. But sometimes the most interesting insight appears when the two don’t match.” The delta between human and machine response may be the insight. Highly recommend all researchers check this out!

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/francis-hintermann-243a9a1?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-image)

[Francis Hintermann](https://www.linkedin.com/in/francis-hintermann-243a9a1?trk=public_post_reshare_feed-actor-name)

3d Edited

I am impressed how quickly AI agents are becoming part of the daily life of researchers. Last year, I wrote about what I called a “Big Bang” moment in research: what caught my attention then was the emergence of Agent-Based Research—using AI agents to simulate how leaders, consumers or employees might respond to questions. A year later, we now use simulations with AI agents regularly to assess behaviors and expectations of consumers and executives. The adoption of these new methods is increasing even faster than many of us anticipated. In a new post on Substack, "what executives can learn from their digital twins", I get into three different types of AI profiles, how we use them, the additional signals that we can now interpret and use in our work to create more value. Many thanks to all my colleagues who continuously innovate and expand our AI research activities, especially [Philippe Roussiere](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/philippe-roussiere?trk=public_post_reshare-text) [Vincenzo Palermo, Ph.D.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vincenzo-palermo?trk=public_post_reshare-text) [Gerry Botchoukova-Farkova](https://www.linkedin.com/in/gerrybotchoukovafarkova?trk=public_

I really enjoyed talking to [BambooHR](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bamboohr?trk=public_post-text) last week while in NYC, sharing what's top of mind for me today in leadership. What if you could build an agency on your terms, choosing profitability over pressure, putting life before work? As a Lifestyle Agency Advisor with over 21 years experience, I’m here to tell you, YOU CAN. I support overwhelmed solo founders who crave long-term sustainable growth, through monthly advisory and strategy development. Define your niche. Generate leads. Build your pipeline. My co-founder and I established a successful web development agency, Rubber Cheese from our kitchen tables and grew it into something really special. Working with global brands and winning creative awards. It was a true privilege to lead it for so long. In 2019 we fully pivoted the agency into its niche, establishing ourselves as the leading web design agency for visitor attractions. I created and hosted the UK’s No.1 sector podcast Skip the Queue, and developed ground breaking insight pieces such as the Visitor Attraction Website Report. I became a regular speaker at conferences internationally, and a leading figure within the attraction industry. After we sold the agency in 2022, I made it my mission to support overwhelmed solo founders build the agency they want on their terms, choosing profitability over pressure, putting life before work. Sharing the tools and tactics I used to transform ours into a specialist and saleable lifestyle agency… Founders gain clarity on direction, mission and positioning to win the right clients. They become confident in increasing prices and saying no to ‘stuff’ that sucks time and energy. They feel the excitement of building strategic partnerships that deliver dream clients. Founders without overwhelm, less stress, loving what they do, the teams they build and work they produce. It’s my vision to change the negative perception of lifestyle agencies, and define them as the future for founders that crave sustainable growth. Meaning we have an industry that isn’t a magnet for overwhelm, stress and unrelenting pressure, but instead filled with happy and fulfilled agency leaders. Want to join me? Head on over to kellymolson.co.uk/get-in-touch to book a call. People-Led, Tech-Powered: Walmart’s AI Job Shift - Knowledge at Wharton

Listen to the podcast.

#### Subscribe wherever you listen to your podcasts.

It’s a question that looms large for any business leader these days, but the stakes are even higher for those that manage massive workforces: As AI transforms the workplace, what happens to jobs and the people doing them?

On this episode of Where AI Works, host Matthew Bidwell speaks with Donna Morris, the chief people officer at Walmart, about what it means to introduce AI across one of the largest workforces on the planet; more than 2.1 million associates around the world. Morris explains the company’s “people-led, tech-powered” approach, where AI is used to augment employees rather than replace them. She also discusses the emergence of new roles like AI agent-builders, and explores the skills that matter most in an AI-driven workplace. While technical expertise is important, Morris argues that adaptability, communication, curiosity, and interpersonal skills will define the most successful employees in the years ahead. For leaders navigating this rapid change, the takeaway is simple: Invest in people, build new skills, and be sure to bring your workers along for the ride.

# Leadership Key to Unlocking AI Potential at Charter's Summit

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/donnamorris2?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Donna Morris](https://www.linkedin.com/in/donnamorris2?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

Walmart•113K followers

1w Edited

- [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdonnamorris2_ai-futureofwork-teamwalmart-activity-7439678835530342402-y36R&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I recently joined leaders in San Francisco for [Charter](https://www.linkedin.com/company/charterworks?trk=public_post-text)'s Leading with AI Summit 2026, and now they've published a playbook recapping the conversations. Some themes stood out through the lends of a people-led organization: 1. The “messy middle” is real and it is where leadership matters most. Leadership will emerge as a key unlock to leverage the potential of AI. Leaders must connect AI to business outcomes, i.e. quality, growth, speed and new capabilities. 2. AI transformation is 10% technology and 90% people. Change management, clear communication about how jobs will evolve, creating space to experiment and rewarding skill-building unlock sustainable impact. Let’s be more positive and more practical about jobs. There's too much rhetoric about everything going away. The better questions are: What will AI create? How will it elevate roles, unlock new skills and expand opportunity? We have a responsibility to shape that future intentionally by investing in development, staying transparent about change and keeping people at the center. At [Walmart](https://www.linkedin.com/company/walmart?trk=public_post-text) we talk about being people-led and tech-powered. That mindset feels more relevant than ever. Technology will continue to evolve, but our values, our culture and our commitment to opportunity will lead the way. ## Sellers Dorsey Welcomes Donna Morrow, RN, as Engagement Director of Healthcare Transformation

- Search
- [News & Media](https://www.sellersdorsey.com/insights/news-media/)

- Published: April 13, 2026

Authors:

Share:

Sellers Dorsey is pleased to welcome Donna Morrow, RN, as our Engagement Director of Healthcare Transformation. Donna brings extensive experience in delivery system reform, value-based care, and clinical operations, further strengthening our ability to help clients translate strategy into sustainable, real-world impact.

Donna joins Sellers Dorsey with a strong background in healthcare operations and implementation, particularly at the intersection of clinical care, technology, and client engagement. Her career has focused on helping organizations design, launch, and scale innovative care models, ensuring they are not only visionary but operationally sound and outcomes driven.

Most recently, Donna worked with Decimal Health as a Subject Matter Expert Engagement Consultant, where she provided leadership and clinical expertise in developing and implementing remote patient monitoring (RPM) solutions. In this role, she supported the full client engagement lifecycle, helping organizations move from program design through execution across complex, multi‑stakeholder environments.

Donna partnered closely with health plans, providers, and technology teams to ensure program goals, workflows, and user experiences were aligned. Her ability to blend clinical insight with operational leadership enabled clients to uncover opportunities, navigate complexity, and deliver meaningful improvements in care delivery.

Throughout her career, Donna has demonstrated a consistent commitment to advancing value-based care and delivery system transformation. As a registered nurse, she brings a practical understanding of clinical workflows and patient needs, paired with strong operational acumen. She is known for her ability to bridge strategy and execution, helping organizations operationalize change in ways that work for providers, patients, and communities.

In her role as Engagement Director, Healthcare Transformation, Donna will work closely with clients of Sellers Dorsey to support program design, implementation, and execution across a range of healthcare initiatives. She will play a key role in helping clients navigate complex transformations while maintaining a focus on quality, access, and measurable outcomes.

We are excited to welcome Donna to the Sellers Dorsey team and look forward to the leadership, insight, and expertise she will bring as we continue to support meaningful healthcare transformation.

# March staff profile – Donna

Posted on: [26th March 2026](https://www.magrathea-telecom.co.uk/2026/03/), by Magrathea

This month we caught up with Donna, our Client Support & Accounts Specialist. Alongside supporting clients, she also takes the lead on all things financial.

We last featured Donna in 2021, so we thought it was the perfect time to revisit her role and see what a typical day looks like for her now.

#### Donna – tell us a bit about what you do at Magrathea?

I manage the finance team, where I am responsible for producing monthly reports and invoices. I also work within the client and sales support team which keeps things varied and means no two days are ever quite the same.

#### What do you enjoy most about your job?

It is hard to pick just one thing – what stands out most is the people and the culture. There’s a real sense of teamwork at Magrathea, the way everyone supports each other makes it an enjoyable place to be every day.

#### What are the most significant changes in the time you have been here?

Having been here for almost 17 years, I have seen a lot of change – thankfully all positive. The company has grown significantly, when I first started I could count the team on one hand, and now we have expanded far beyond that! The telecoms industry itself is also constantly evolving from a regulatory perspective, which keeps things interesting.

#### What advice would you give someone starting out in telecoms or finance?

Be open to learning, always ask plenty of questions and don’t underestimate how much you’ll pick up from people around you!

#### What’s your all-time favourite film, book, or TV show?!

It’s hard to choose just one! I love a mix of fantasy, action and adventure. Enid Blyton’s The Magic Faraway Tree books will always hold a special place from my childhood and I’m really looking forward to seeing them brought to life on the big screen.

#### And finally, how do you enjoy spending time outside of work?

We’re a skiing family, so we’re always planning our next slopes adventure. I also love stand-up paddleboarding by the coast, keeping active at our local tennis club (where I am also Treasurer) and exploring the countryside with our two dogs.

Thank you, Donna, for taking the time to catch up with us. It’s always a pleasure – and it’s great to hear more about your role and the work that keeps everything running smoothly, as well as what you enjoy outside of the office.

# Hy-Vee CEO shares tribute to Donna Tweeten

Tweeten, Hy-Vee’s first female president, was a central figure in shaping the retailer’s brand and national profile over a career spanning more than four decades.

April 10, 2026 . 12:13 PM

1 min read

WEST DES MOINES, Iowa — Hy-Vee Chairman and CEO Jeremy Gosch paid tribute to Donna Tweeten in a LinkedIn post, reflecting on her lasting impact on the company and the broader retail industry after her passing on April 8.

Tweeten, Hy-Vee’s first female president, was a central figure in shaping the retailer’s brand and national profile over a career spanning more than four decades. She joined the company in 2006 and served as chief marketing officer and later president, helping drive a series of high-profile partnerships and marketing initiatives that expanded Hy-Vee’s reach.

In his LinkedIn post, Gosch emphasized both his professional contributions and his personal influence across the organization.

“We are simply heartbroken by the sudden loss of Donna Tweeten. Not only was Donna a trailblazer across Hy-Vee and the retail industry, she was a true friend and mentor to many, myself included. Donna leaves behind a lasting legacy and will forever be remembered for her bold ideas, creative passion and commitment to excellence. We miss her deeply and words cannot express the sorrow that is being felt across our Hy-Vee family due to the impact she had on all of us,” he wrote.

Under Tweeten’s leadership, Hy-Vee expanded its brand visibility through partnerships with athletes including Caitlin Clark, Patrick Mahomes and Travis Kelce, thereby positioning the retailer more prominently within national consumer culture.

Tweeten retired in January 2025 but remained active as a consultant until her death. She had recently been diagnosed with Stage 4 pancreatic cancer.

Gosch also noted that funeral arrangements have not yet been finalized and will be announced soon. He added that the company is keeping Tweeten’s family in its thoughts, including her husband, Alan, and their five children and grandchildren.
