Swiss banks have spent decades convincing you that premium costs more. [moneyland.ch](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fmoneyland%2Ech&urlhash=8vKf&trk=public_post-text) just published the 2026 numbers on what it actually costs to pay abroad with a Swiss card. UBS: CHF 113. Zürcher Kantonalbank: CHF 48. Basler Kantonalbank: CHF 111. [Alpian Bank](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/alpian?trk=public_post-text): CHF 6. Same country. Same currency. Same transaction. 18x cheaper than UBS. We're not a neobank trying to undercut the market. We're a premium Swiss digital bank that decided the old pricing model was a story, not a necessity. Premium service and fair pricing were never opposites. The industry just made you believe they were. Full independent study from [moneyland.ch](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fmoneyland%2Ech&urlhash=8vKf&trk=public_post-text): [https://lnkd.in/eYwpwaEj](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FeYwpwaEj&urlhash=Bq5X&trk=public_post-text) [#Alpian](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Falpian&trk=public_post-text) [#SwissBanking](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fswissbanking&trk=public_post-text) [#BankingUpgraded](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbankingupgraded&trk=public_post-text)

There are 2 million members in r/startups. Not polished founder stories. Not launch threads designed for applause. Real conversations about runway, pivots, investor updates, churn, co-founder conflict, distribution experiments, and product-market fit anxiety. If your company sells to early-stage founders or venture-backed teams, r/startups is not optional visibility. It's where uncertainty, tools, and vendors are discussed, tested, and recommended or dismissed. Most brands incorrectly try to "market" there: pushing links, inserting themselves, or manufacturing relevance. This behavior is quickly filtered. The smarter play is different. Study the top posts. Analyze which startup pain points consistently resurface. Observe what gets upvoted when founders ask for tool recommendations. Track language patterns around growth, burn, hiring, and traction. That's a live founder sentiment. And here’s the strategic layer executives should be thinking about: When founders ask AI tools questions like “best tools for early-stage startups” or “how to reduce SaaS churn,” Reddit threads from r/startups increasingly surface in citations. Which means influence inside r/startups extends beyond Reddit. It shapes AI, mediated discovery. That’s distribution leverage most teams still underestimate. If you’re evaluating how Reddit fits into a serious B2B growth strategy, this is worth exploring → [https://lnkd.in/gztibmdK](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FgztibmdK&urlhash=Dlbj&trk=public_post-text) Two million startup builders sharing unfiltered reality. Ignoring that isn’t focus. It's a missed signal. 𝕀 𝕥𝕣𝕒𝕚𝕟𝕖𝕕 𝕒 𝕣𝕠𝕠𝕞 𝕗𝕦𝕝𝕝 𝕠𝕗 𝕥𝕠𝕡 𝕤𝕒𝕝𝕖𝕤 𝕒𝕔𝕙𝕚𝕖𝕧𝕖𝕣𝕤 𝕪𝕖𝕤𝕥𝕖𝕣𝕕𝕒𝕪 𝕒𝕟𝕕 𝕟𝕠𝕥𝕚𝕔𝕖𝕕 𝕥𝕙𝕚𝕤... Many of them were already hugely successful. Hitting targets. Closing deals. Performing consistently. But what impressed me wasn’t their results. It was their hunger to learn! After the session, I had conversations with a few of them. [Lorelle Sng](https://sg.linkedin.com/in/lorelle-sng-0231863a6?trk=public_post-text) And something interesting came up… “𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽𝗲𝗱 𝘂𝘀 𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗳𝗿𝗮𝗺𝗲𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀.” These are people who are already adept at sales. They knew what worked… just not always why it worked. And that’s the difference. When you bring skill from the unconscious to the conscious level, something powerful happens: 1️⃣ You become more consistent 2️⃣ You can replicate success, not rely on luck 3️⃣ You can coach and scale others That’s when “talent” becomes a system. And systems are what turn good salespeople… into elite performers. Grateful to my speaker director [Benjamin Ang](https://sg.linkedin.com/in/benangyh?trk=public_post-text) for making this training possible, and for the opportunity to work with [Isaac Teo](https://sg.linkedin.com/in/isaac-teo-2b40b14?trk=public_post-text) HSBC Life's Top GA Principal. Always inspiring to be in a room with leaders committed to growth. If your top performers are only relying on instinct, you’re leaving performance on the table. 👉 Curious to know... Are your best salespeople operating on instinct, or do they have a system they can clearly explain and replicate? - 

 

A colleague ([Jacquie Bourne](https://au.linkedin.com/in/jacquie-bourne-82805a67?trk=public_post-text)) shared Understanding Generation Alpha with me this week and it’s helping me to continue to think about future students, employees, and consumers. Some key takeaways for me: • Generation Alpha will be the largest, wealthiest, and most formally educated generation in history, yet with notable gaps in life skills and critical thinking. • They are true “screenagers.” Generation Glass: hyper-connected and highly digital, but requiring intentional support in wellbeing, resilience, and social development. • In a world shaped by AI, automation, and multiple careers. Character, adaptability, and interpersonal skills will matter more than ever. • Parents and educators are rapidly raising expectations on schools to support holistic development, not just academic outcomes. • Despite all the technology and change, Gen Alpha will still crave acceptance, community, and belonging. Timeless human needs that must sit at the centre of how we design learning, workplaces, and organisations. A powerful reminder: if schools and organisations want to thrive in the 2030s and beyond, we must start engaging, equipping, and entrusting Generation Alpha now. - [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fericakeswin_i-cant-believe-this-is-my-life-in-this-activity-7439736987844149249-8PyS&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

“I can’t believe this is my life!” In this episode of "Honestly, We’re Learning" from [ClearEdge](https://www.linkedin.com/company/meetclearedge?trk=public_post-text), I share the story of how I first discovered the power of technology back in ‘98 when I worked remotely from Bermuda. My trusty Blackberry (which I still miss), enabled me to work from the beach. Mind blown. I remember saying to myself, “I can’t believe this is my life!! 😀” Fast forward ten years and three kids later, I was rocking TWO devices because the iPhone had just come out and apps were the shiny new toy. This time though, I was wrangling crying kids and incessant pings and dings from both phones. In the overwhelming chaos of it all, I remember saying to myself again, “I can’t believe this is my life! 😩” What a difference a decade makes. That juxtaposition between those two moments ten years apart kicked off my journey into finding the sweet spot between tech and connect: utilizing tech for all its greatness…but also knowing when and where to put it in its place (aka, away). And in the AI era, finding that sweet spot matters more than ever. I loved joining [Leslie Vickrey](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lvickrey?trk=public_post-text) and [Lesly Cardec](https://www.linkedin.com/in/leslycardec?trk=public_post-text) on this episode about Human-Centered Leadership in the Age of AI. Thanks for having me! Catch the full episode here: [https://lnkd.in/e9fBTWgM](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fe9fBTWgM&urlhash=M9B_&trk=public_post-text)

Planning approvals for battery, wind, and solar projects in Great Britain (GB) have almost doubled over the past year, with more than 45GW of capacity approved in 2025. This is 96% higher than last year’s 23GW, and enough to power 12.9 million homes. Fresh analysis from [Cornwall Insight](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/cornwallinsight?trk=public_post-text) reveals that this boom is being driven by battery storage, which climbed to 28.6GW from 14.9GW in 2024 and offshore wind, which saw planning approvals jump to 9.9GW from 1.3GW in 2024. These technologies are rapidly transforming the UK’s energy landscape. The growth reflects a longer-term shift as the UK fast tracks its transition to renewable energy. Many of the projects have been years in the making, with developers submitting plans well before recent approval dates. The sharp rise in approvals also points to more immediate factors. Technologies such as battery storage have reached a greater level of maturity, enabling developers to bring forward much larger-scale projects. At the same time, some developers accelerated applications ahead of network Connections reforms, where development status helps determine queue position under the new ‘first ready, first needed, first connected’ approach. Local elections may have also influenced timing. With uncertainty around future renewable energy planning policies, some developers may have sought approval before any potential changes in local administration. Government efforts to streamline planning processes and prioritise nationally significant infrastructure have helped cut delays and give developers greater certainty. Recent updates to the National Policy Statements and commitments to faster consenting have been widely welcomed by industry and are expected to support further growth over the next few years. We transmit, distribute and connect electricity to and from 3.5 million homes and businesses over our network, 24 hours a day, every day of the year. We operate in three of the UK's largest cities (Liverpool, Glasgow & Edinburgh) accounting for 1.6m (46%) of our customers, as well as three significant rural areas (North Wales, Scottish Borders and Dumfries & Galloway).

We take electricity generated from power stations and renewable energy sources, reduce it to the low voltage needed for homes and transport it through our vast electricity network. We also provide customers with new or upgraded connections to our network.

We are a substantial employer with a distribution workforce of 2,600 internal employees made up of field staff, engineers, technical specialists, customer service and support staff based at 17 locations in the South of Scotland and 17 locations in England and Wales. We utilise around 2,500 contractors across these areas.

Our highly trained and specialist staff work 24/7 to maintain the performance and safe condition of our electricity network, respond to customer enquiries and restore the supply as quickly as possible when a fault occurs on the network.

In the UK, we are a leading Distribution Network Operator (DNO) in distributing renewable energy, having connected around 7GW of renewable generation to our networks with more connected on a daily basis.

For many EV drivers, the path to Level 2 home charging hits an unexpected wall: their electrical panel can't support it. [ComEd](https://www.linkedin.com/company/comed?trk=public_post-text)’s recent Electric Vehicle Energy Management System (EV EMS) Pilot with WeaveGrid, [Emporia](https://www.linkedin.com/company/emporiaenergy?trk=public_post-text), and [Treehouse](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ourtreehousepro?trk=public_post-text) proves that you don’t need a new electrical panel to drive an EV and charge faster than a trickle at home. EMS technology dynamically manages EV charging to ensure the total household electricity load stays within safe limits, no expensive infrastructure upgrades required. The pilot found that it’s possible to reliably manage load within panel limits without sacrificing charging convenience. EMS alone isn't the whole picture, though. Pairing panel-level load management with active managed charging is what allows utilities to address constraints all the way from the service panel through distribution and the bulk system, and that's exactly how WeaveGrid was built to help. Huge shout out to [Ana Villarreal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ana-villarreal-55018262?trk=public_post-text) and [Tony Bustamante](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tony-bustamante-657a41?trk=public_post-text) for bringing this pilot to life! Companies and healthcare specialists need to be informed about the recent findings regarding AI. There are two key components to consider. First, the impact of AI in companies. A recent Harvard Business Review article by Arnaud Chevalier, Frederic Dalsace, and José Parra-Moyano highlights a study involving 13 widely used large language models (LLMs) including Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, DeepSeek, Mistral, Perplexity, and xAI. Each LLM was asked the same questions, 200 times, in proportions similar to those used with hundreds of executives. Important outcomes from the study reveal that when LLMs begin answering questions, one cannot assume they will ask all the questions a human would. Additionally when LLMs given suggestions, they do not merely suggest options; they actively shape the responses. While LLMs may convey confidence, this can be misleading, as a model can appear high quality even when it is not. In addition, each LLM has its own unique question mix, making it crucial to utilize different LLMs to obtain a more balanced question set and mitigate bias. Ultimately, human involvement is essential to address the blind spots created by AI. Regarding healthcare, a study by Julian Herpertz et al. examined FDA-authorized software, particularly in mental health. Some FDA-approved prototypes, such as reSET and reSET-O, were not the final commercial products. Notably, some approved software for depression showed no better results than placebo (e.g., Rejoyn), and reSET-O did not demonstrate any additional benefits compared to standard treatment. Furthermore, EndeavorRx, used for ADHD, did not show improvements in ADHD symptoms that translated to academic success, and Akii's

American citizen living in Italy since 2018. Auth to work in USA and EU, or hire my 🇺🇸 or 🇮🇹 companies as consultants. Open to remote work anywhere. Will consider hybrid work around the EU. How fast does your company need to change? Improve customer and user experiences? Achieve business goals? More efficiently, less risk, less waste? FAST, right? That's my specialty. I'm a problem-finder and problem-solver who starts getting it done within weeks of being hired. ***LI loves to not show my posts to people. You can follow me here, but if you want to see something I post or announce, please follow me elsewhere! CX/UX links at dcx.to. LifeAfterTech.info Resources page for those links. I can make a 1-yr plan, 2-yr plan, etc., but we also need to be Agile. That plan is probably going to change as we change and evolve. I go into jobs knowing that sometimes, I only have 3-12 months there, and I can be cut early if I'm not making the changes that are expected. These companies expect problems to be understood and fixed. They expect UX teams to be changed, improved, and fixed. They expect bad relationships between departments to be fixed. They expect strategy where there wasn't any. They expect standards, process, and quality where it was inconsistent on a good day. And they expect it within weeks of me starting. I can make a 2-year plan, but I have to make tremendous and palpable change immediately. I have to fix systems and records. I have to keep people from quitting and move them to the right job for their abilities. I have to make it a place where people want to tell people to come and work. I have to improve quality and process. I have to upskill people. I've done this many times, and I had to fix a lot of things within weeks of starting the job. How long can people wait for change? Not long. I set houses in order in record time. I'm hired to put out fires. I'm happy to make a multi-year plan and see it through. But in a world going fast, fast, fast, what can I do within weeks or a few months of being hired? Let's talk.

# Λύγισε η Έλλη Κοκκίνου μιλώντας για τη Μαρινέλλα: «Ήταν μια αιώνια έφηβη»

«Εγώ προσωπικά μεγάλωσα με τη μορφή της, με τη μουσική της»

29-03-2026 | 13:43

Η [Έλλη Κοκκίνου](https://www.espressonews.gr/tag/elli-kokkinoy/)μίλησε στην εκπομπή «Σαββατοκύριακο Παρέα» και στη Μαρία Κοκολάκη για την απώλεια της [Μαρινέλλας](https://www.espressonews.gr/tag/marinella/), εμφανώς φορτισμένη συναισθηματικά.

Η τραγουδίστρια θυμήθηκε την τελευταία φορά που τη συνάντησε στο Nox, όπου είχε βρεθεί για να τη δει από κοντά σε μία από τις τελευταίες ζωντανές εμφανίσεις της.

«Λίγα λόγια για τη Μαρινέλλα δεν αρκούν, φυσικά δεν μπορώ να πω πολλά. Όλος ο κόσμος έχει ταραχτεί, εμείς οι καλλιτέχνες ίσως λίγο περισσότερο. Εγώ προσωπικά μεγάλωσα με τη μορφή της, με τη μουσική της, με τη μαγική, ανεπανάληπτη, εκφραστική φωνή της. Τη θυμάμαι σαν τώρα, όταν ήμουν κοριτσάκι, να τη βλέπω στις ελληνικές ταινίες. Ήταν η πρώτη μου αγάπη αυτή. Μόνο να τη λατρέψεις μπορούσες. Η φωνή της, η προσωπικότητά της, η μοναδικότητά της, η αγάπη της για τον κόσμο, η αγάπη της για αυτό που έκανε. Ήταν μια αιώνια έφηβη. «Έφυγε» όπως εκείνη ήθελε, με ένα μικρόφωνο στο χέρι, πάνω στη σκηνή που υπεραγαπούσε, μαζί με τον κόσμο που υπεραγαπούσε, που ήθελε πάρα πολύ να δίνει στον κόσμο.

Ήταν η τελευταία φορά που εμφανίστηκε, που είχα πάει στο Nox, την είχα δει. Και αυτό που θυμάμαι ήταν ότι πήγα στα καμαρίνια… Πάντα όταν την έβλεπα, γονάτιζα στα πόδια της, σαν να ήμουν ένα πολύ μικρό παιδάκι, πολύ μικρή μπροστά της. Πάντα μου χάιδευε το κεφάλι και τους ώμους, γέλαγε με αυτό που έκανα εγώ. Εγώ το έκανα γιατί ήθελα να τη νιώσω πολύ κοντά μου και λόγω σεβασμού. Και δάκρυζα, από πολύ μικρή την έβλεπα και δάκρυζα. Πόσο μάλλον όταν την έβλεπα προσωπικά» εξομολογήθηκε συγκινημένη η Έλλη Κοκκίνου.

Έλλη Κοκκίνου: Η Μαρινέλλα ήταν μια αιώνια έφηβη, «έφυγε» όπως ήθελε με ένα μικρόφωνο στο χέρι 

 

 

Έλλη Κοκκίνου / NDPPHOTO / ΠΑΝΑΓΙΩΤΗΣ ΚΟΥΦΑΛΕΞΗΣ

Η [Έλλη Κοκκίνου](https://www.newsit.gr/tags/elli-kokkinou/)μίλησε για την [Μαρινέλλα](https://www.newsit.gr/tags/marinella/)στην εκπομπή «Σαββατοκύριακο Παρέα» του ΑΝΤ1. Ανέφερε ότι την λάτρεψε και ότι συνήθιζε να γονατίζει μπροστά της όταν την συναντούσε. Συναισθηματικά φορτισμένη, έφερε στο μυαλό της μια σειρά από εικόνες από τότε που ήταν παιδί, μέχρι τα τελευταία χρόνια όταν προσπαθούσε να βρει χρόνο για να την απολαύσει στα νυχτερινά κέντρα που εμφανιζόταν.

Όπως είπε η Έλλη Κοκκίνου: «Λίγα λόγια για τη Μαρινέλλα δεν αρκούν, φυσικά δεν μπορώ να πω πολλά. Όλος ο κόσμος έχει ταραχτεί, εμείς οι καλλιτέχνες ίσως λίγο περισσότερο. Εγώ προσωπικά μεγάλωσα με τη μορφή της, με τη μουσική της, με τη μαγική, ανεπανάληπτη, εκφραστική φωνή της. Τη θυμάμαι σαν τώρα, όταν ήμουν κοριτσάκι, να τη βλέπω στις ελληνικές ταινίες. Ήταν η πρώτη μου αγάπη αυτή. Μόνο να τη λατρέψεις μπορούσες.

Η φωνή της, η προσωπικότητά της, η μοναδικότητά της, η αγάπη της για τον κόσμο, η αγάπη της για αυτό που έκανε. Ήταν μια αιώνια έφηβη. «Έφυγε» όπως εκείνη ήθελε, με ένα μικρόφωνο στο χέρι, πάνω στη σκηνή που υπεραγαπούσε, μαζί με τον κόσμο που υπεραγαπούσε, που ήθελε πάρα πολύ να δίνει στον κόσμο.

Ήταν η τελευταία φορά που εμφανίστηκε, που είχα πάει στο Nox, την είχα δει. Και αυτό που θυμάμαι ήταν ότι πήγα στα καμαρίνια… Πάντα όταν την έβλεπα, γονάτιζα στα πόδια της, σαν να ήμουν ένα πολύ μικρό παιδάκι, πολύ μικρή μπροστά της. Πάντα μου χάιδευε το κεφάλι και τους ώμους, γέλαγε με αυτό που έκανα εγώ. Εγώ το έκανα γιατί ήθελα να τη νιώσω πολύ κοντά μου και λόγω σεβασμού. Και δάκρυζα, από πολύ μικρή την έβλεπα και δάκρυζα. Πόσο μάλλον όταν την έβλεπα προσωπικά» εξομολογήθηκε συγκινημένη η Έλλη Κοκκίνου.

Μια άλλη τραγουδίστρια της γενιάς της, [η Πέγκυ Ζήνα έγραψε για την Μαρινέλλα, πως οι συμβουλές που τους είχε δώσει αποδείχθηκαν καθοριστικές](https://www.newsit.gr/lifestyle/pegky-zina-gia-marinella-me-malones-kai-otan-dakryza-me-ekanes-amesos-na-gelao-isoun-mana-gia-olous/4637704/)για την επαγγελματική πορεία και σταδιοδρομία της.

# Έλλη Κοκκίνου: «Όταν πήρα την απόφαση να χωρίσω, ένιωσα απογοήτευση και αποτυχία»

Πέμπτη02.04.2600:23

Μια εφ’ όλης της ύλης συνέντευξη έδωσε η [Έλλη Κοκκίνου](https://www.zappit.gr/tag/elli-kokkinou), αυτή την εβδομάδα, στο νέο επεισόδιο για το vidcast των Rainbow Mermaids. Μάλιστα, μεταξύ άλλων, η εντυπωσιακή τραγουδίστρια μίλησε για τα νέα δεδομένα που έφερε η μητρότητα στη ζωή της αλλά και για την προσωπική αποτυχία που ένιωσε με το διαζύγιο της.

Ειδικότερα, η Έλλη Κοκκίνου εξομολογήθηκε αρχικά πως “ο γιος μου ήρθε στη ζωή το 2008, ήταν συνειδητή η απόφαση να γίνω μητέρα. Απόλυτα συνειδητή απόφαση. Όταν πήρα την απόφαση να χωρίσω, ο Αλέξανδρος δεν είχε κλείσει ούτε τα δύο, που ‘ναι μια πολύ πιο εύκολη ηλικία γιατί δεν έχει μνήμες και δεν έχει το δέσιμο που έχει ένα παιδί επτά ετών”.

“Άρα για μένα ήταν πολύ πιο εύκολο όσον αφορά το θέμα του παιδιού. Από κει και πέρα, επειδή έχω υπάρξει παιδί χωρισμένων γονιών με πάρα πολύ πολιτισμένο τρόπο και πάρα πολύ ώριμων γονέων, το ίδιο πράγμα κάναμε και εμείς. Έτσι ήταν και αυτό. Γιατί στην ουσία ήξερα ότι αν δεν είμαστε καλά εμείς, δεν υπάρχει κανένας λόγος να μεγαλώσει ένα παιδί σε ένα περιβάλλον που δεν θα ‘ναι καλό”.

“Σίγουρα εγώ, μετά, ένιωσα μια απογοήτευση και μια αποτυχία προσωπική μου. Αλλά μετά από την παιδοψυχολόγο, με την οποία βρισκόμουν για να με συμβουλεύσει πως θα χειριστώ το θέμα του παιδιού πριν καν μετακομίσω, ήμουν έτοιμη και κατάλαβα πως δεν ήταν προσωπική μου αποτυχία ώστε να μην το παίρνω τόσο βαριά” συμπλήρωσε, ακόμα, η Έλλη Κοκκίνου στη νέα διαδικτυακή της συνέντευξη.

Κοκκίνου για Μαρινέλλα: Πάντα όταν την έβλεπα, γονάτιζα στα πόδια της, σαν να ήμουν ένα μικρό παιδί | Gossip-tv.gr

Με βαθιά συγκίνηση και λόγια γεμάτα αγάπη, η [Έλλη Κοκκίνου](https://www.gossip-tv.gr/celebrities/celebrity/278/elle-kokkinou)μίλησε για την απώλεια της [Μαρινέλλας](https://www.gossip-tv.gr/celebrities/celebrity/821/marinella)στην εκπομπή «Σαββατοκύριακο παρέα», περιγράφοντας τη μοναδική θέση που είχε στη ζωή της, τόσο ως καλλιτέχνιδα όσο και ως άνθρωπος.

Η τραγουδίστρια δεν έκρυψε τη συναισθηματική της φόρτιση, τονίζοντας πόσο έντονα έχει επηρεαστεί από τη φωνή, την παρουσία και τη συνολική πορεία της Μαρινέλλας, την οποία –όπως είπε– κουβαλά μέσα της από τα παιδικά της χρόνια.

[Η Άντζελα Γκερέκου αποχαιρετά τη Μαρινέλλα: «Καλό ταξίδι στο φως...»](https://www.gossip-tv.gr/showbiz/story/870677/i-antzela-gerekou-apoxaireta-ti-marinella-kalo-taksidi-sto-fos)

Όπως ανέφερε χαρακτηριστικά:

«Λίγα λόγια για τη Μαρινέλλα δεν αρκούν, φυσικά δεν μπορώ να πω πολλά. Όλος ο κόσμος έχει ταραχτεί, εμείς οι καλλιτέχνες ίσως λίγο περισσότερο. Εγώ προσωπικά μεγάλωσα με τη μορφή της, με τη μουσική της, με τη μαγική, ανεπανάληπτη, εκφραστική φωνή της. Τη θυμάμαι σαν τώρα, όταν ήμουν κοριτσάκι, να τη βλέπω στις ελληνικές ταινίες. Ήταν η πρώτη μου αγάπη αυτή. Μόνο να τη λατρέψεις μπορούσες.

Η φωνή της, η προσωπικότητά της, η μοναδικότητά της, η αγάπη της για τον κόσμο, η αγάπη της για αυτό που έκανε. Ήταν μια αιώνια έφηβη. «Έφυγε» όπως εκείνη ήθελε, με ένα μικρόφωνο στο χέρι, πάνω στη σκηνή που υπεραγαπούσε, μαζί με τον κόσμο που υπεραγαπούσε, που ήθελε πάρα πολύ να δίνει στον κόσμο»

Στη συνέχεια, η Έλλη Κοκκίνου μοιράστηκε μια πιο προσωπική ανάμνηση από τις συναντήσεις τους, αποκαλύπτοντας τη συγκίνηση που ένιωθε κάθε φορά που τη συναντούσε από κοντά:

«Ήταν η τελευταία φορά που εμφανίστηκε, που είχα πάει στο Nox, την είχα δει. Και αυτό που θυμάμαι ήταν ότι πήγα στα καμαρίνια… Πάντα όταν την έβλεπα, γονάτιζα στα πόδια της, σαν να ήμουν ένα πολύ μικρό παιδάκι, πολύ μικρή μπροστά της.

Πάντα μου χάιδευε το κεφάλι και τους ώμους, γέλαγε με αυτό που έκανα εγώ. Εγώ το έκανα γιατί ήθελα να τη νιώσω πολύ κοντά μου και λόγω σεβασμού. Και δάκρυζα, από πολύ μικρή την έβλεπα και δάκρυζα. Πόσο μάλλον όταν την έβλεπα προσωπικά»

Μέσα από τις λέξεις της, αναδεικνύεται όχι μόνο ο θαυμασμός μιας καλλιτέχνιδας προς μια σπουδαία μορφή, αλλά και η βαθιά, σχεδόν παιδική αγάπη που ένιωθε για εκείνη, μια αγάπη που δεν έσβησε ποτέ.

Συναισθηματικά φορτισμένη, έφερε στο μυαλό της μια σειρά από εικόνες από τότε που ήταν παιδί, μέχρι τα τελευταία χρόνια όταν προσπαθούσε να βρει χρόνο για να την απολαύσει στα νυχτερινά κέντρα που εμφανιζόταν.

It started as a panel. Then it became completely something else. At one point, I literally felt the energy in the room shift. We stopped being a panel. Stopped being speakers and audience. We just… became one conversation. Seven founders who’ve built incredible brands had given their time to be there. To share. And what struck me most wasn’t just what they shared. It was how they shared it. Honestly. Openly. Without pretending they had it all figured out. The questions changed. The answers changed. No one was performing anymore. It wasn’t about saying the right thing. It was about saying the real thing. Both the panel but also all the BWB founders in the audience. We were meant to break into smaller groups. But no one moved. No one wanted to leave the moment. So we didn’t. We just stayed in it. That’s what [BUY WOMEN BUILT](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/buy-women-built?trk=public_post_reshare-text) is really about. It’s that magic when women on same journeys come together. Different stages. But the same journey. Because building something is hard enough. People looking for mortgages are facing a new headache (Image: Patamaporn Umnahanant via Getty Images) Fresh analysis has revealed customers of many major banks facing fresh challenges as world events have transformed the landscape since the beginning of March. A reduction to Bank of England Base Rate (BBR) appears increasingly improbable, with the turmoil in mortgage re-pricing resulting in a disappearing act of sub-4% fixed mortgages, according to [Moneyfactscompare.co.uk](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2FMoneyfactscompare%2Eco%2Euk&urlhash=OJpH&trk=public_post-text) analysis published on Tuesday morning. Summarising the current situation, Moneyfactscompare said the pool of lenders offering a sub-4% fixed rate deal had taken a “significant blow”. All of the biggest banks, namely Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds Bank, NatWest and Santander, have raised rates since the beginning of March. It added that prolonged market uncertainty “can lead to further rate increases or product withdrawals. Barclays, HSBC, NatWest, Nationwide and Santander no longer offer sub-4% fixed deals, which were available last week”. Throughout the market, it said, the last time the lowest two- and five-year fixed rates were priced above 4% was more than a year ago, in February 2025, based on first-of-month data. Year-on-year average mortgage rates across the two, five and 10-year fixed sectors have declined, but recent rises have pushed the average two and five-year rates above five per cent, it said. The Bank of England Base Rate was reduced to 3.75% in December 2025. Since then, the average standard variable rate (SVR) has dropped by 0.14%, from 7.27% to 7.13%. But when it starts improving how a bank spots risk, serves customers, and scales growth without adding cost at the same rate, that is where AI gets really interesting. ## About

Alicia García Herrero is the Chief Economist for Asia Pacific at Natixis. She also serves as Senior Fellow at European think-tank BRUEGEL and non-resident Research Fellow at Madrid-based political think tank Real Instituto Elcano. She is currently Adjunct Professor at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology and member of the advisory board of Berlin-based China think-tank MERICS. Finally, Alicia is advisor to the Hong Kong Monetary Authority’s research arm (HKIMR) and the Asian Development Bank (ADB) as well as a member of the board of the Hong Kong Forum and co-founder of Bright Hong Kong. In previous years, Alicia held the following positions: Chief Economist for Emerging Markets at Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria (BBVA), Member of the Asian Research Program at the Bank of International Settlements (BIS), Head of the International Economy Division of the Bank of Spain, Member of the Counsel to the Executive Board of the European Central Bank, Head of Emerging Economies at the Research Department at Banco Santander, and Economist at the International Monetary Fund. In addition, Alicia has always combines her professional career with academia, having served as visiting Professor for John Hopkins University, China Europe International Business School (CEIBS) in Shanghai and Carlos III University in Madrid. Alicia holds a PhD in Economics from George Washington University and has published extensively in refereed journals and books (see her publications in ResearchGate or Google Scholar. Alicia is also very active in international media (Bloomberg and CNBC among others) as well as social media (Twitter, LinkedIn and Weibo). As recognition of her leadership thoughts, Alicia has recently been nominated TOP Voices in Economy and Finance by LinkedIn.

On February 26, 2026, Taiwan’s IP Office (TIPO) ruled in favor of Dior, cancelling a “CD & device” trademark registered for education and training services (Class 41), citing likelihood of confusion with Dior’s well-known “CD” mark. TIPO found that: 1. Dior’s “CD” mark had already achieved well-known status prior to the filing of the contested mark, supported by extensive media exposure, long-term marketing efforts, and prior official recognitions. 2. Despite additional wording (“Cut Dye Hair”, Chinese characters) and a scissor design, TIPO found the stylized “C” and “D” to be the dominant elements, leading to visual and phonetic similarity with Dior’s mark. 3. The applicant’s argument that “CD” stood for “Cut” and “Dye” was not persuasive. Trademark similarity is assessed from the consumer’s perspective, not the applicant’s subjective intent. 4. Dior’s expansion into beauty-related services (e.g., salons, hair products) strengthened its position that the well-known mark can enjoy broader protection beyond its core product categories. AIA Group Limited and its subsidiaries (collectively “AIA” or the “Group”) comprise the largest independent publicly listed pan-Asian life insurance group. For World Oral Health Day, our teams around the world have been helping people build healthier oral care habits and improve everyday health. 1. Kenya & South Africa – Through Sensodyne, parodontax and Aquafresh, we are raising awareness of dentine hypersensitivity, encouraging people to speak up about common oral health concerns. 2. Egypt – Our Sensodyne Smile Bank convoys providws free check-ups, treatment and oral health education to underserved communities. In partnership with the FDI and the Egyptian Dental Association, the initiative brings care directly to people with limited access. It has reached 365,000 since launch, with a target of 3m by 2027. 3. Sri Lanka – We renewed our partnership with the Sri Lanka Dental Association, running a campaign to encourage proactive oral healthcare. 4. Germany – We launched a media campaign to raise awareness about dental issues such as sensitive teeth, which affect 1 in 2 people in Germany. Part of the focus was to demonstrate that there are therapeutic toothpastes with innovative ingredients for these and other dental problems. 5. Poland – We launched a campaign to highlight early signs such as sensitivity and bleeding gums, encourage product choices matched to personal needs and reinforce simple routines like brushing twice daily. 6. Hungary – We launched a regional campaign supported by a mobile screening bus offering free checks, education and sampling. We are also donating 10m forints to support oral health education for children in disadvantaged communities. 7. France – Haleon provided parodontax toothpaste samples to the Stroke Prevention Bus operating across the Paris. # Pooja S.’s Post

APriori Consultants•2K followers

2w

[Shubhajit Sen](https://in.linkedin.com/in/shubhajit-sen-2a90022?trk=public_post-text)’s favorite question to ask our clients is a thought experiment. Imagine someone went into a coma in 2010 and woke up today. What would they notice? The phone in their hand. The food they order without calling anyone. The brands they no longer recognise — and the ones they do, looking exactly as they remember. Now apply the same filter to your brand. Would they think anything had changed? For most brands, that question lands harder than any equity research presentation. Because the honest answer is usually: not much. But the consumer has not stood still. There are five tectonic forces — demography, economy, technology, culture, and climate — that have been reshaping how people live, what they value, and how they choose. Most brands only catch up when the numbers force them to. By then, they're already two innovation cycles behind. We’ve distilled these to 12 themes relevant to the Indian Consumer. Our consumer shifts framework maps each of these in detail. The macro data that explains what is actually happening. The implications for your specific category and brand. And the questions you should be sitting with as you build your growth strategy. Because planning without this is not conservative. It is a different kind of risk. Great discussions and inspiring perspectives last week at the [Debiopharm](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/debiopharminternational?trk=public_post-text) Digital Day! [Juan Ramón Troncoso-Pastoriza](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/juan-ram%C3%B3n-troncoso-pastoriza-4508323a?trk=public_post-text) and myself had the pleasure to attend the event. The agenda brought together leaders across AI, pharma, and data science to explore how artificial intelligence is reshaping drug development, from discovery to clinical trials and biomarker development. A few insights that stood out to me: 🔹 AI is moving from experimentation to infrastructure. The conversation around the current and future landscape of AI highlighted how organizations are transitioning from isolated pilots to embedding AI as a core capability across R&D. 🔹 Clinical trials are entering a data-centric era. AI-driven approaches are improving patient stratification, optimizing trial design, and enabling better use of real-world and multimodal data to increase trial efficiency and success rates. For us at [Tune Insight](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/tuneinsight?trk=public_post-text), these discussions reinforce an important point: the future of AI in healthcare depends on secure and collaborative data access. Many of the most promising AI applications require combining sensitive datasets across institutions: something that must be done while preserving [#privacy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fprivacy&trk=public_post-text) and [#trust](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftrust&trk=public_post-text). Excited to continue the conversation and explore how privacy-preserving analytics can help unlock the next generation of AI-powered healthcare innovation. By [Ranjay Gulati](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ranjay-gulati?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention) and [Samir Junnarkar](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sjunnarkar?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention)

If you watched the Super Bowl in February, you likely walked away with one overwhelming conclusion: AI is here, and it is everywhere. By some counts, nearly a quarter of the commercials during the game featured Artificial Intelligence, with heavyweights like [Google](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z1yGy9fELtE&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block), [OpenAI](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4rzeW4dbvlQ&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block), [Anthropic](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=De-_wQpKw0s&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block), [Amazon](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ha92_hfK9Po&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block), and [Meta](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NerlyGrv7WM&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block) investing an average of $8 million per 30-second unit. Each promised a version of the future that seemed shinier, faster, and more effortless than the last.

But for the leaders we speak with, the day after didn’t bring clarity; it brought anxiety. Make no mistake, we’re currently in the eye of the storm. The core technology is advancing at breakneck speed, creating what researchers call a “ [Jagged Frontier](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpapers%2Essrn%2Ecom%2Fsol3%2Fpapers%2Ecfm%3Fabstract_id%3D4573321&urlhash=TgH5&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block).” Inside the frontier, AI can perform certain tasks better, faster, and more efficiently than humans. Outside the frontier, humans continue to outperform AI.

Leaders are waking up to a stark reality: AI is rewriting the fundamental Production Function of the firm. We are starting to witness a shift in the basic formula of how we turn capital and talent into value. What makes this transition particularly daunting is that this Jagged Frontier is a moving target. With relentless technological progress and successive waves of product innovation shifting the frontier almost daily, it’s difficult to orient oneself in the AI fog and chart a path forward.

In this season of Leadership Unlocked, our goal is to hold a lantern in the fog. We recognize that in a period of rapid change, no one has a perfect crystal ball—ourselves included. However, we believe this moment demands more than a fragmented de

Most sustainability efforts act like a skin, a superficial layer of "green" messaging applied at the end. True circularity behaves like an operating system: it is the fundamental logic that governs your supply chain, your R&D, and your quality control. At Banyan Nation, we’ve built the OS for Better Plastic™: - The Logic: 6-stage scientific protocols that ensure resin behaves like virgin material. - The Security: 100% digital traceability for audit-ready compliance. - The Output: Brand-approved quality that doesn't fail on high-speed manufacturing lines. Stop treating the planet as a slogan. Start treating it as a system. - [ Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fbishoptodd_microsoft-hires-former-ai2-ceo-ali-farhadi-activity-7441979126711955456-pA1S&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 
- 

 

Breaking: [Microsoft](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft?trk=public_post-text) is hiring former [Ai2](https://www.linkedin.com/company/allen-ai?trk=public_post-text) CEO [Ali Farhadi](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ali-farhadi-098430b1?trk=public_post-text) along with [Hanna Hajishirzi](https://www.linkedin.com/in/hanna-hajishirzi-83359816b?trk=public_post-text), [Ranjay Krishna](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ranjay-krishna-1a344444?trk=public_post-text) and [Sophie Lebrecht](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sophie-lebrecht-522b3530?trk=public_post-text) from the Allen Institute for AI to join Mustafa Suleyman's AI Superintelligence team. Farhadi, Hajishirzi, and Krishna are expected to remain on the faculty of the [Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uw-allen-school/?trk=public_post-text). The hires come as Microsoft works to reduce its dependence on OpenAI for frontier AI models, competing against Amazon, Google, and others. Suleyman's Superintelligence team, formed in November, is part of a broader push to further develop advanced foundation models. Our story also digs into the behind-the-scenes shifts contributing to the departures, including changes brought about by the new role being played by the $3.1 billion Fund for Science and Technology, moving Ai2 away from frontier model development more toward practical applications of AI. Businesses still dependent on WLR and PSTN services are beginning to experience a noticeable shift longer delays, inconsistent processes, and reduced reliability in line provisioning and reinstatement. What was once a straightforward next-day activation is increasingly stretching into multi-day timelines. In a recent instance, a routine line reactivation was mistakenly processed as a new installation, extending the delivery timeframe to 10 working days. At the same time, inconsistencies in address records across providers are adding another layer of complexity, leading to unexpected delays and operational disruption. The underlying reality is simple. As legacy systems continue to be phased out, the capacity to support them efficiently is diminishing. Businesses that delay migration risk encountering challenges at critical moments such as relocations or operational changes. The longer the wait, the less predictable the outcome. At Pen Telecom, we support businesses in navigating this transition with clarity and confidence by: • Assessing existing telecom infrastructure • Recommending tailored solutions including Full Fibre and VoIP • Managing the migration process end to end to ensure minimal disruption Acting early allows businesses to stay in control. Delaying increases dependence on systems that are already under pressure. The scale of the transition is significant. Approximately 2.8 million lines across the UK still require migration, including around 475,000 business connections. With only about 45,000 lines moving each week, demand on infrastructure and resources will continue to rise. At the same time, costs are increasing. Nearly 3 months into my journey with EF Group, and one thing has become really clear… No two care homes operate the same — but many are facing the same underlying challenges. From managing supplier costs and stock, to juggling catering, consumables, and waste… there’s a lot to stay on top of. These are exactly the kinds of things I’ve been helping care homes improve — and every conversation gives me a better understanding of what really makes a difference day-to-day. So I’m curious 👇 If you could wave a magic wand and fix ONE thing in your care home tomorrow… what would it be? ✨ More control over costs? ✨ More reliable suppliers? ✨ Less time spent on admin? ✨ Smoother day-to-day operations? ✨ More training support for your team? Or something else? Drop your “one wish” in the comments — I’d love to see where the biggest challenges really are (and hopefully help solve a few along the way).

[Gartner](https://www.linkedin.com/company/gartner?trk=public_post-text)𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐜𝐭𝐬 𝟐𝟓% 𝐨𝐟 𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐞 𝐛𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐢𝐧𝐯𝐨𝐥𝐯𝐞 𝐀𝐈 𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐚𝐛𝐮𝐬𝐞 𝐛𝐲 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟖. Here is what most organisations are missing: it is not the AI that creates the risk. It is the non-human identities behind it. The API keys. The service accounts. The machine credentials operating with unchecked access. Non-human identities now outnumber human ones in most enterprises. Yet governance frameworks still treat identity as a human problem. That gap is why I developed the 𝐀𝐍𝐂𝐇𝐎𝐑 𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐦𝐞𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤. A practical, structured approach to governing non-human identities before they become your biggest blind spot. Grateful to [CXO Outlook®](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cxooutlook?trk=public_post-text) for publishing my latest article on this topic. If you are deploying AI at speed, ask yourself: who is governing the identities you cannot see? 📌I would love to hear from CISOs, CROs, and technology leaders. How are you governing non-human identities in your organisation today?

### Reimagining Client Registration at De Hoop

Some projects stay with you.

Not because they were easy, but because they reminded you why you do this work in the first place and why I started [Ideal Shift AI](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/ideal-shift-ai?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention).

Our collaboration with [De Hoop](https://www.linkedin.com/preload/?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block), a leading christian mental healthcare provider in the Netherlands, was one of those projects.

Together, we set out to solve a problem that many healthcare organizations quietly struggle with:

A registration process that is more efficient, more insightful and minimizes manual work.

On October 2nd, 2025, we marked an important milestone: the demo of the first major phase of this journey. And we took the picture above! :) This was the culmination of an intensive, focused period of preparation, testing, collaboration and learning.

As we continue into the next phase, our focus remains on strengthening this foundation and learning from real-world use.

### The Challenge: Less Administrative Work

Before this project began, De Hoop’s registration and screening teams were working within established processes that had evolved over many years.

These methods played an important role in supporting care delivery. They relied on dedication, experience and consistent effort from staff members who worked carefully to gather information, follow up with clients, and prepare cases for screening.

Their previous process included:

- Calling clients when information was missing; work that is reimbursed by health insurance
- Manually entering and verifying data
- Following up on forms and permissions

The system worked, but there was room for improvement.

Both De Hoop's and our team recognized that there was an opportunity to explore more structured, scalable ways of gathering and organizing information with methods that could better support future growth and evolving care demands.

### Automating the Entire Registration Process with AI and Human-Centered Design

Instead of “digitizing paperwork,” we worked side by side with De Hoop’s teams to rethink the entire experience.

Not just:

“How can this more efficient?”

But:

“How can we improve the process so the team has complete and reliable information by the time a client is screened? So that clients are helped faster and better and so that this has a positive effect

After 20 years as a Residential Property Solicitor, one frustration still stands out. Too often, when picking up the phone to progress a matter — whether to agree a completion date or resolve a simple point — the response is: “please put it in an email.” Of course, written records have their place. But when straightforward conversations are replaced with back-and-forth emails, it’s no surprise the process can feel slower than it needs to be. A quick call can resolve in minutes what might otherwise take days. If we want to improve the experience for clients and keep transactions moving efficiently, there has to be a balance — using the right method of communication at the right time. Sometimes, picking up the phone really is the most effective tool we have. 𝐓𝐨𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐈'𝐦 𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐦𝐲 𝐄𝐔 𝐀𝐈 𝐀𝐜𝐭 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐢𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐒𝐤𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐬 𝐒𝐮𝐢𝐭𝐞! These are 5 interconnected skills (that also work standalone): ⚡ Quick Assessment → 15-min triage ◈ System Classifier → full Art. 3(1) test + risk tier ⊕ Role Determination → Art. 25 quasi-provider analysis ▦ Obligations Mapper → RACI matrix + GDPR crosswalk ⊞ Report Generator → 4 formats incl. .docx export *Download Links in comments* 48 reference files. ~14,000 lines of structured legal methodology. 8 EU jurisdictions with national overlays. The skills work best as a pipeline but each one works standalone too. 𝐘𝐞𝐬, the Commission's Digital [#Omnibus](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fomnibus&trk=public_post-text) proposal could push the Annex III high-risk AI system deadline by up to 16 months. But prohibited practices and AI literacy already apply since February 2025, GPAI since August 2025, and the core Art. 50 transparency obligations stay on track for August 2026 regardless. If we do get more time for high-risk systems? More time for testing & refining the skills before enforcement hits :) For me, Agent Skills are the best example of how to actually leverage AI in the legal sector. Most business owners are overpaying for benefits… and still not getting the results they want. Here’s the truth: Benefits aren’t just a cost center—they’re a strategic advantage when designed correctly. The problem? Most plans are built reactively, not intentionally. A well-structured benefits strategy should do two things: 1. Strengthen the business • Reduce unnecessary costs while improving coverage • Protect the owner’s income and the future of the company • Provide access to resources that support smarter operations 2. Support and retain your people • Medical, dental, and vision coverage that actually matters • Retirement options that help employees plan ahead • Work-life balance through PTO and flexibility • Professional development and growth opportunities • Life and disability protection for real financial security When both sides win, everything changes. You get a more loyal team, stronger culture, and a business that’s built to last. The real question is: Is your current benefits strategy helping you grow… or quietly holding you back? # John Stewart’s Post

In 2025, [MiddleGround Capital](https://www.linkedin.com/company/middlegroundcapital?trk=public_post-text) stayed focused on what we could control: operating our businesses, building our team, and moving transactions forward. We completed the sales of Arrow Tru-Line and Lindsay Precast, in addition to signing an agreement to sell Vytl Controls Group. Portfolio milestones were hit, with Starrett significantly reducing scrap while our automation team completed 24 installations across the portfolio. We also invested in the firm, adding senior leadership in finance, exits, and capital markets. We launched STOP-6, a safety initiative targeting the six highest-risk injury categories across our portfolio. We transitioned to a centralized data platform. None of this happens without our management teams, our limited partners, and the people across this firm who execute every day. I don't take any of it for granted. ### 🔵 Major energy companies launch Humber Hydrogen 

 

SSE has joined a coalition of leading energy companies to launch Humber Hydrogen, supporting the UK’s first integrated hydrogen network and helping drive industrial decarbonisation in one of the country’s most important industrial regions. 

 

🔗 [Read the full story on](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Esse%2Ecom%2Fnews-and-views%2F2026%2F02%2Fmajor-energy-companies-launch-humber-hydrogen-to-support-uk-s-first-integrated-hydrogen-network%2F%3Futm_source%3Dlinkedin%26utm_medium%3Dnewsletter%26utm_campaign%3Dfebruary_connected&urlhash=i0T3&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block) [sse.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fsse%2Ecom&urlhash=9mkK&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block)

### 🔋Ferrybridge Battery Storage System enters full operation 

 

SSE’s new 150MW Ferrybridge battery storage project in West Yorkshire is now fully operational, providing fast‑response energy storage to help balance the grid and support UK energy security. The site can power nearly 250,000 homes.

We transmit, distribute and connect electricity to and from 3.5 million homes and businesses over our network, 24 hours a day, every day of the year. We operate in three of the UK's largest cities (Liverpool, Glasgow & Edinburgh) accounting for 1.6m (46%) of our customers, as well as three significant rural areas (North Wales, Scottish Borders and Dumfries & Galloway).

We take electricity generated from power stations and renewable energy sources, reduce it to the low voltage needed for homes and transport it through our vast electricity network. We also provide customers with new or upgraded connections to our network.

We are a substantial employer with a distribution workforce of 2,600 internal employees made up of field staff, engineers, technical specialists, customer service and support staff based at 17 locations in the South of Scotland and 17 locations in England and Wales. We utilise around 2,500 contractors across these areas.

Our highly trained and specialist staff work 24/7 to maintain the performance and safe condition of our electricity network, respond to customer enquiries and restore the supply as quickly as possible when a fault occurs on the network.

In the UK, we are a leading Distribution Network Operator (DNO) in distributing renewable energy, having connected around 7GW of renewable generation to our networks with more connected on a daily basis.

# Tesco Ditches 1D Barcodes, Embracing 2D for Real-Time Expiry Visibility

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

 

 

[](https://au.linkedin.com/in/rhyswilliamson?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Rhys Williamson🔌](https://au.linkedin.com/in/rhyswilliamson?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 

 1w 

 

- [ Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Frhyswilliamson_tesco-just-made-the-barcode-obsolete-2d-activity-7443125654268567552-0_hH&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 
- 

 

Tesco just made the barcode obsolete. 2D barcodes are replacing 1D barcodes across their shelves, and the pilot is already live across 12 product lines. Lemons, limes, steaks and sausages among them. But the Tesco trial is just the start. Across the broader 2D barcode ecosystem, the implications for brands go well beyond the shelf: 1. Real-time expiry visibility, so you're not relying on static print dates that don't account for actual storage conditions 2. Direct consumer engagement at the moment of purchase, turning a barcode scan into a branded touchpoint 3. Richer first-party data on how and when customers are interacting with your products post-purchase 4. Faster recall response, because if something goes wrong, you know exactly where a product is in the supply chain Starting with a handful of SKUs is exactly the kind of measured entry point that makes a technology shift feel manageable. And the 2027 deadline from GS1 means this isn't a "wait and see" situation for much longer. If you're mapping out what this transition looks like for your catalogue, happy to walk you through it.

This is very sad and demonstrates the narrow gap between life and death and the fragility of that period. This horse gave great pleasure to many and lived up to its name. Rest in peace in horses’s heaven. Brave Knight BRAVE KNIGHT We are very sorry to announce that Brave Knight fell at the final flight in the 5.25 at Ascot this afternoon. He did not get back up, and the racecourse vets felt he passed away the moment he hit the ground with no suffering. Thankfully, jockey Freddie Keighley walked away and looked fine afterwards. Brave Knight was a wonderfully tough and reliable hurdler who won many races on the Flat for his previous connections and over hurdles for his devoted owners. He won his last race in January despite driving rain, receiving a rapturous reception on a very gloomy day. Brave Knight was a lively character who will be much missed by all at Owners Group and at Paul Nicholls' Ditcheat stables.

A New Home for Sport in Cork 🥇 We are thrilled to announce plans for Decathlon to open a new store within [Tesco Ireland](https://ie.linkedin.com/company/tesco-ireland?trk=public_post-text) at Douglas Village Shopping Centre this summer. This marks our fourth store in Ireland and represents an exciting partnership that brings sport and everyday shopping together under one roof. Opening in Cork has been a long-standing ambition for us. As [Elena Pecos](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/elena-pecos-3a5b5578?trk=public_post-text), CEO of Decathlon UK and Ireland, shared: "Opening in Cork has been a long-standing ambition for us. It’s a city we’ve believed in for years... it truly feels like a dream realised. We can’t wait to welcome Corkonians through our doors this summer". Together, Decathlon and Tesco will become a convenient one-stop destination where customers can find the best of Tesco’s food, home, and clothing alongside Decathlon’s superb product range. With over 2,300 sqm of sales area, we are making it easier than ever for the local community to stay active. As we close out a full-on week in Madrid, I am more certain than ever that nothing replaces sitting down in person with partners and colleagues for an honest conversation. When my leadership team gets together, one of my non-negotiables is that the customer voice is part of the agenda. This week, thanks to [Tony](https://ae.linkedin.com/in/tonylteif?trk=public_post-text) and [Tuce](https://es.linkedin.com/in/tucezengin?trk=public_post-text), several key partners joined us in person to tell us exactly what's working, what isn't, and where they need us next. A very special part of the week for me was finally putting faces to names with our Madrid team. [Rodrigo](https://es.linkedin.com/in/rodrigomadrigal/en?trk=public_post-text), [Christine](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/christine-fisher-28946b65?trk=public_post-text), Ruth - thank you for making sure we had quality time together. The best Town Halls are the ones where the questions keep coming and Madrid did not disappoint! As an ELL leadership team, we spent time looking at what comes next for our business. From AI adoption to evolving migration patterns and changing government priorities, it's essential we truly understand what's needed across 50+ very distinct markets. To our teams in Madrid, thank you for your wonderful hospitality this week. And to our partners at Sky Business, Edu, Anaya and ApplyBoard - we are grateful for your honesty and your time. These conversations are where the best things start. # CV Essentials: Contact Details, Length, Stats, and More

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/taylor-nash-2001?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Taylor Nash](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/taylor-nash-2001?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

3Search•9K followers

3d

- [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftaylor-nash-2001_had-lots-of-candidates-asking-recently-what-activity-7440320578948935680-0HA9&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Had lots of candidates asking recently what we believe a good CV looks like. Everyone's preference is different, and there are usually opposing opinions in the recruitment world, but personally, I think these are the 'must haves' for all CVs. 📧 Contact details & personal statement I’ve spoken to a handful of candidates recently who didn’t have any contact details (email or phone number) on their CV. Totally understandable, you don’t want every recruiter calling, but if you also don’t have them on LinkedIn, it makes it really tricky for us to reach you. I would always recommend having at least your email address on there, and you can share your number once you've been contacted about the role. Your personal statement should be short, sharp, and tailored, a snapshot of who you are and what you’re looking for next. For example: “I’m a Performance Marketing Manager with 15+ years’ experience across B2C and D2C industries, specialising in performance marketing, paid media, and growth. I’m now looking to build further in growth strategy for my next role.” 💡 Top tip: create a separate email just for job applications, contracts, basically all things job related to keep organised. 🧾 Length Stick to two pages max. Anything before the last 5–7 years? Just a quick 1–3 bullet overview. And make sure your most recent role sits on page one, you'd be surprised how many people don’t scroll past this! 📊 Stats vs responsibilities Metrics and stats are still important; they show impact in a way words alone can’t. But employers also want to understand what you actually did day-to-day, especially in manager-level roles and above. A clear format is to list key responsibilities first, then follow with key achievements that include numbers or measurable results. For example, don’t just say “Managed campaigns”, say “Managed campaigns across p

There is an important lesson for policing in the decision by [Essex Police](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/essexpoliceuk?trk=public_post-text) to pause the use of live facial recognition technology following concerns about potential racial bias. For some time, the [National Black Police Association](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/national-black-police-association?trk=public_post-text) has consistently raised questions about the disproportionate impact that emerging technologies can have on Black, Asian and minority ethnic communities. On a number of occasions we have also been assured, by senior leaders including Sir Mark Rowley, Gavin Stephens and others, that at certain operational settings these systems do not demonstrate bias. This is why the actions taken by Essex Police and Chief Constable [Ben-Julian 'BJ' Harrington QPM](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/ben-julian-bj-harrington-qpm-b443541b?trk=public_post-text) deserve recognition. It takes moral courage and leadership to pause the deployment of a capability that is seen as operationally valuable in order to properly examine concerns about fairness, legitimacy and community confidence. This is not about resisting innovation. Policing has always evolved. Emerging technology has the potential to transform policing in the same way that DNA and fingerprints once did. But if we are to realise those benefits, we must be prepared to ask difficult questions about ethics, governance and disproportionality. If we fail to get this right, we risk damaging the confidence of communities for years to come and that is a price policing simply cannot afford to pay [https://lnkd.in/epHvRifM](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FepHvRifM&urlhash=FYt4&trk=public_post-text) [Big Brother Watch](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/big-brother-watch?trk=public_post-text) [Liberty (National Council

ONE MILLION!! 🫶🏼🎉 Sharing my journey building [Pashion Footwear](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pashion-footwear?trk=public_post-text) on [TikTok](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tiktok?trk=public_post-text) has been one of the most transformative journeys of my life - both for me personally, and for the Company. To now have a community of 1M followers tuning in to hear me talk about shoes every day....it's surreal. ~3.5 years of posting 5 days a week, often multiple times a day. Hundreds of millions of views. 45.2M likes. Over 2 million people driven directly to [www.pashionfootwear.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Epashionfootwear%2Ecom&urlhash=qZIl&trk=public_post-text)(and vastly more indirectly). Over 50K customers acquired. And the best part? Since my content has always been centered around the Pashion journey - 90% of my following demographic matches our target consumer. A warm leads pipeline I could never pay for in cash - so I pay for it in time, filming content daily from my home office (an activity that has genuinely become my favorite part of the day). Something that has always made me so excited about Tiktok is the democratic nature of the platform. While meta & other social media channels have largely become "pay to play", Tiktok has always prioritized engagement above all else - whether it's paid or not. This has become a massive equalizer for small businesses & startups. We may get outbid on meta for not having six-figure ad budgets...but on Tiktok? If you have a cell phone, a great product, and know how to tell a story - you can thrive, with no budget outside of your own sweat capital. I credit this platform for getting us on [Nordstrom.com](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2FNordstrom%2Ecom&urlhash=aFJg&trk=public_post-text). For landing me on Capitol Hill, testifying to Congress. For securing our collab with Emmy Combs. For building my own confidence - and making me comfortable using my voice publicly. For up-leveling our demand & merch planning with a real-time pipeline for customer feedback - empowering us to make smarter decisions. For playing a critical role in us surviving the supply chain crisis fallout, and surviving the current trade war. For sourcing members of the dream team. # Beitrag von Gina Drewalowski

[](https://de.linkedin.com/in/gina-drewalowski-b8a04a77?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Gina Drewalowski](https://de.linkedin.com/in/gina-drewalowski-b8a04a77?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

2 Tage

- [Diesen Beitrag melden](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fde.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fgina-drewalowski-b8a04a77_ich-bin-managerin-mutter-unternehmerin-activity-7440306236249686016-BiCh&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

Ich bin Managerin, Mutter, Unternehmerin. Und eine Sache, die ich mir gestern wieder sagen musste: Lass los. Ich habe jahrelang geglaubt, Kontrolle ist Stärke. Alles im Griff haben. Jeden Prozess, jede Entscheidung, jeden Termin. Im Job. In der Familie. Beim Aufbau unserer Marke. Was dabei passiert ist? Ich habe funktioniert. Aber ich habe nicht mehr gespürt. Heute weiß ich: Kontrolle ist nicht Stärke. Kontrolle ist Angst in einem schönen Anzug. Echte Stärke beginnt da, wo du sagst: Ich muss nicht alles halten. Ich vertraue mir. Ich vertraue meiner Vision. Und ich vertraue den Menschen um mich herum. Seit ich loslasse, passieren Dinge, die ich nie hätte planen können. Die Einladung von L’Oréal. Der Laufsteg in Paris. Diese ganze Reise, die mit 58 begann und mit 60 erst richtig Fahrt aufnimmt. Nicht weil ich mehr kontrolliere. Sondern weil ich weniger festhalte. Take it lighter. Take it with humor. Und vor allem: Trust the process. Was war euer größtes „Let it go“-Moment im Business? Erzählt mal. Eure Gina 💛

We’ve just wrapped our largest-ever SAP IBP Customer Success & Network Meeting in Melbourne. A big thank you to all customers who joined us—your engagement made this such a vibrant gathering! We focused on aligning around SAP IBP’s product vision and roadmap, exchanging best practices, and learning from real-world experiences to build more resilient, efficient, and connected supply chains. We explored decision-centric planning, Planner Workspaces, the Harmonized Planning Area, supply planning, revenue growth management, change management, and more. The most lively discussions centered on AI and AI agents—how they can augment planners, accelerate decision-making, and turn insights into action.  [SAP](https://de.linkedin.com/company/sap?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention) ’s acquisition of [SmartRecruiters](https://www.linkedin.com/company/smartrecruiters?trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-mention) strengthens its focus on AI within the SAP ecosystem. For organizations using SAP’s HR solutions, this could mean a more intuitive and powerful recruiting experience - but it will also bring questions around integration, implementation, and long-term strategy. 

 

---

 

### Why It Matters 

 

Recruitment technology has evolved rapidly in recent years. Organizations expect and need tools that are intuitive, data-driven, and built for collaboration between recruiters, hiring managers, and candidates - and, of course, tools that can leverage AI. 

 

With AI being implemented in almost every system you can think of, bringing SmartRecruiters into the SAP portfolio strengthens SAP’s position within the talent acquisition space and responds to the increased demand for more modern recruiting platforms that can adapt to evolving HR processes. 

 

By bringing SmartRecruiters into the SAP portfolio, SAP is strengthening its position in the talent acquisition space and responding to increasing demand for modern recruiting platforms that combine: 

 

- Better candidate experience - a smoother and simplified application process, especially with the ability to apply via Winston chat (the AI chatbot that can analyze résumés, provide support for candidates throughout the application and job search process, assist in interview scheduling, and reduce manual recruitment tasks for HR personnel) 
- Easier connectivity to other platforms and job boards through the new SmartRecruiters Marketplace 
- Improved user experience - strong collaboration options between hiring teams 
- Seamless HR ecosystems (over time) 

 

 

For companies already using SAP HR solutions, this will improve the daily experience for recruiters and reduce administrative workload. 

 

---

 

### For HR Leaders & Recruiters 

 

The acquisition raises important strategic considerations. 

 

Questions organizations might start asking include: 

 

- What will the future recruiting architecture look like? 
- How will SmartRecruiters integrate with existing SAP HR modules? 
- What will the roadmap look like? 

 

 

> These topics are especially important for recruiters and HR leaders working day

# SAP Acquires Reltio for Enterprise Entity Resolution

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

 

 

[](https://in.linkedin.com/in/sonalgoyal?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Sonal Goyal](https://in.linkedin.com/in/sonalgoyal?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name) 

 2d 

 

- [ Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsonalgoyal_sap-to-acquire-reltio-make-sap-and-non-sap-activity-7443301751941730304-Lyhi&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting) 
- 

 

SAP just announced it's acquiring Reltio. Terms undisclosed, expected to close Q2/Q3 2026. The headline is about MDM. But read the press release carefully and what SAP is actually buying is entity resolution at enterprise scale — the ability to identify and merge related records from different formats and applications into one reliable "golden record." That's not a coincidence. That's the market telling you something. For years, entity resolution lived in the basement of the data stack — unglamorous, invisible, assumed to be solved. Every architecture diagram showed clean, unified data flowing into analytics and AI. Nobody drew the part where you figure out that "Acme Corp", "ACME Corporation", and "Acme, Inc." are the same customer across six systems. SAP's own rationale for the deal says it plainly: "AI cannot reach its full potential when data is fragmented across business units, platforms and domains without connection or context." That fragmentation problem is the reason a company with $43 billion in annual revenue just decided it needed to acquire it rather than build it. What I find most interesting is the framing. SAP wants to unify data across both SAP and non-SAP sources — they're explicitly acknowledging that enterprise data doesn't live only in their ecosystem. Joule agents, their agentic AI layer, need clean identity data from everywhere. You can't have reliable AI actions when you can't even establish who a customer or supplier actually is. This is the moment the data world has been circling around for a while. The wave of enterprise AI investment is running into a very old, very stubborn problem — dirty identity data — and acquirers are finally paying acquisition-level prices to fix it. Curious what others in the data community make of this move. [#reltion](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?ses

- 

 

SAP Build Apps is being deprecated. As of 23rd March 2026, SAP officially retired it as a standalone product. No direct migration path for existing frontend projects. Manual CSV export for backend data. Customers told to rebuild using pro-code capabilities in the unified SAP Build platform. From No-Code to Pro-code!! We'd been raising concerns about the enterprise-grade UX limitations of Build Apps for a while. Not to be contrarian - but because the patterns didn't hold up in real SAP landscapes. The drag-and-drop promise was appealing. But real businesses, with real complexity, need Fiori (guidelines need tweaks though!). SAPUI5. Code-first. This isn't about who called what. It's a reminder that depth wins over convenience. Every time. Build your skills on foundations that don't get sunsetted. Siemens and Rittal have launched a strategic partnership to create standardized, sustainable power infrastructure for next‑generation data centers—addressing rapidly rising AI power densities that already exceed 100 kW per rack and could approach 1 MW by 2030. One early solution is a "sidecar" power rack housed in white space, bundling power electronics to provide fast, scalable, OCP‑compatible power distribution. Modular, tested platforms such as RiLineX and Ri4Power, combined with standardized low‑voltage distribution, integrated cooling and heat‑recovery approaches, promise faster deployment, higher availability, and improved tokens‑per‑watt. Standardization reduces complexity and shortens time‑to‑compute for large AI deployments. Wide adoption of these design patterns could make rapid scale‑up of AI compute more predictable, efficient and sustainable—while improving operational safety and simplifying rollout cycles. - [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Frob-finney-0185a625_the-industrial-ai-revolution-is-here-and-activity-7439736971863769088-a4L3&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

The Industrial AI revolution is here and Siemens digital manufacturing is putting it on display at Hannover Messe 2026. From reactive operations to intelligent manufacturing, Industrial AI is redefining how industry operates. At Hannover Messe (April 20–24), Siemens isn’t just talking about AI’s potential. We’re showing how it’s already transforming the entire industrial value chain. Here’s a preview of what you’ll see in Hall 27 with Siemens digital manufacturing solutions in focus: ⚙️ Digital Enterprise Showcase — CPG leading the way See Industrial AI connect consumer insight, product design, production, and supply chain into one intelligent flow. Featuring PepsiCo’s AI-powered digital twin using Siemens Digital Twin Composer and partnering with NVIDIA, cutting facility design timelines from months to days. 🧠 Technology Deep Dive — Building the Digital Enterprise Explore real customer use cases across tooling and part manufacturing, manufacturing process planning, and quality with Siemens digital manufacturing solutions. 🔬 Innovation Hub — Adaptive Manufacturing Watch Industrial AI power a design-to-manufacturing demonstration, showing how the same architecture scales across aerospace, automotive, and industrial machinery and more. 🤝 At the Microsoft Booth — Physical AI in Action Experience Siemens NX X Manufacturing AI capabilities that helps programmers navigate complex CAM workflows using natural language, powered by Microsoft Azure. The question is no longer whether to adopt Industrial AI, it’s how you can effectively deploy it. The companies that will lead the next era of industry are those that understand how to scale their efforts intelligently, connecting the power of Industrial AI to their strategic advantage. That’s precisely the experience that Siemens is bringing to Hannover Messe 2026. 📍 Hall 27 | April 20–24 | Hannover

Great case study at [#CWEnergy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcwenergy&trk=public_post-text) from [Hollie Benson Breeden](https://www.linkedin.com/in/hollie-benson?trk=public_post-text) at [EDF power solutions](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/edf-power-solutions?trk=public_post-text) and a good reminder that transformation is more human than technical. They started with a simple question: what’s frustrating the field? Answer: paperwork. 📋 So they built a mobile, offline solution to remove friction and improve safety. 🤳 But here’s the real story: Adoption was only ~25%… until they went back to the field and listened. ➡️ Many “big” issues were small (like account setup) ➡️ Time spent with users → adoption jumped to 93% ➡️ Training alone wasn’t enough—partnership was Big takeaway: 💡Trust, listening, and fixing the little things are what actually scale transformation.

🌏 Insurance AI: 900+ use cases — and still not scaling 🔍 More use cases do not mean more scale Allianz reports more than 900 AI use cases internally registered worldwide. Yet, speaking at InsurTech Insights, Jason Howes of Allianz UK noted that scaling AI remains difficult, as reported by Insurance Post (Mar 19, 2026). This is not a contradiction. “900 use cases” does not mean 900 scaled deployments. It reflects a portfolio of initiatives — from pilots to partial implementations — across the group. AI is already widespread. But most use cases remain local. They work within specific teams, on specific workflows, under specific assumptions. What they rarely do is scale across: systems workflows decision ownership That is where scaling becomes difficult. ⸻ ⚖️ Not a regulatory issue The EU, under the EU AI Act, pushes for structured, risk-based deployment. The UK, guided by institutions such as the Financial Conduct Authority, takes a more principles-based approach. Different models. Same pattern. Even in more flexible environments, AI does not automatically scale. Because the constraint is not regulatory permission. It is operational integration. ⸻ 🧩 What this means for insurers Scaling AI is not about launching more use cases. It requires: • standardising workflows where decisions can be made • defining clear ownership of AI-driven decisions • designing systems to integrate, not isolate, AI capabilities Without this, AI remains a collection of initiatives rather than an operating capability. ⸻ 🧭 Key message AI does not fail at scale because of individual firms. It fails because large organisations are not designed to absorb it consistently.
