# L.B. Bohle at Interphex 2026

- Download PDF Copy
- [View Supplier Profile](http://d2jx2rerrg6sh3.cloudfront.net/suppliers/LB-Bohle.aspx)

L.B. Bohle will be showcasing three examples of its pharmaceutical processing technology for the manufacture of oral solid doses at INTERPHEX 2026 in New York City: the [QbCon® 1](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/continuous-granulation-and-drying-qbcon-1) continuous granulation and drying system, the [BFC 5 laboratory tablet coater](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/pharma-tablet-coating-systems/laboratory-coater-bfc-5), and the [BTS 200](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/grinding-and-sieving-systems/conical-sieve-bts) pharmaceutical sieving machine.

QbCon® 1 Continuous Wet Granulation and Drying System. Image Credit: L.B. Bohle Maschinen und Verfahren GmbH

Visitors to the L.B. Bohle stand at Booth 1619 will see how the company’s latest equipment supports continuous manufacturing and improves pharmaceutical production processes and product development efficiency.

These three technology highlights will demonstrate advances in continuous oral solid dose manufacturing, laboratory tablet coating, and pharmaceutical particle processing.

## QbCon® 1 Truly Continuous Wet Granulation and Drying

The [QbCon® 1 Continuous Wet Granulation and Drying System](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/continuous-granulation-and-drying-qbcon-1) represents a significant advancement in continuous oral solid dose production. The system combines continuous raw material dosing, twin-screw wet granulation and drying in one compact, mobile unit.

Advanced features include sophisticated material conveyance, very short residence times in the dryer, automatic exhaust filter self-cleaning and high containment. Integrated control systems increase operational flexibility while enhancing operator safety.

The [QbCon® 1](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/continuous-granulation-and-drying-qbcon-1) is designed to deliver narrow residence-time distributions that simplify material traceability in continuous processes. This makes it easier to track materials from raw ingredients to finished tablets, while supporting accelerated scale-up.

Integrated process analytics also help to reduce resource consumption and shorten pharmaceutical development times.

# L.B. Bohle auf der Interphex 2026

## Continuous Manufacturing und Technologien für die Tablettenproduktion

 

QbCon 1 für die echt-kontinuierliche Feuchtgranulation und Trocknung zur Tablettenproduktion.

(PresseBox) ([Ennigerloh](https://www.pressebox.de/pressemitteilung/suche/schlagwoerter/Ennigerloh), 20.03.2026)

L.B. Bohle präsentiert auf der INTERPHEX 2026 in New York drei Maschinen seiner pharmazeutischen Prozesstechnologie für die Herstellung oraler fester Darreichungsformen: das kontinuierliche Granulier- und Trocknungssystem QbCon® 1, den Laborcoater BFC 5 sowie die Siebmaschine BTS 200. Am Stand 1619 zeigt L.B. Bohle, wie die Anlagen pharmazeutische Produktionsprozesse sowie die Effizienz in der Produktentwicklung verbessern. QbCon® 1: Echte kontinuierliche Feuchtgranulation und Trocknung QbCon® 1 markiert einen wesentlichen Fortschritt in der kontinuierlichen Herstellung oraler fester Darreichungsformen. Das System vereint die kontinuierliche Dosierung von Rohstoffen, Doppelschnecken-Granulation und Trocknung in einer kompakten, mobilen Einheit. Zu den technischen Highlights zählen ein ausgereifter Materialtransport, sehr kurze Verweilzeiten im Trockner, eine vollautomatische Filterreinigung während des Trocknungsprozesses sowie eine simple und kofortable Bedienung. Integrierte Steuerungssysteme erhöhen die Flexibilität im Betrieb und verbessern zugleich die Sicherheit für das Bedienpersonal. QbCon® 1 realisiert enge Verweilzeitverteilungen, wodurch die Materialrückverfolgbarkeit in kontinuierlichen Prozessen deutlich vereinfacht wird. Rohstoffe lassen sich so bis zur fertigen Tablette präzise nachverfolgen. Gleichzeitig unterstützt das System ein beschleunigtes und abgesichertes Scale-up. Integrierte Prozessanalytik trägt zudem dazu bei, den Ressourceneinsatz zu senken und Entwicklungszeiten in der Pharmaindustrie zu verkürzen. BFC 5 Laborcoater Ein weiteres Highlight auf dem Messestand von L.B. Bohle ist der BFC 5 Laborcoater. Das System ist als kompaktes, mobiles Stand-alone-Gerät ausgelegt und eignet sich ideal für Coating-Prozesse in der Forschung und Entwicklung oder im Pilotmaßstab. Sprühen, Mischen und Trocknen laufen simultan unter präzise definierten Bedingungen ab, um eine optimale Coating-Uniformität zu erzielen. Lufttechnik, Elektrotechnik und Steuerungstechnik sind vollständig integriert; die Bedienung erfolgt über eine intuitives Touchpanel. Der BFC 5 ist für Batchgrößen von zwei bis 13 Litern ausgelegt. The [BFC 5 laboratory tablet coater](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/pharma-tablet-coating-systems/laboratory-coater-bfc-5) will be the second major technology highlight on the L.B. Bohle stand. It provides a compact, mobile, stand-alone solution for tablet coating operations, performing spraying, mixing and drying simultaneously under tightly controlled conditions to achieve optimal coating uniformity.

Airflow management, electrical systems and process control are fully integrated, with multi-panel visualisation available via an intuitive touchscreen interface.

# L.B. Bohle at Interphex 2026

- Download PDF Copy
- [View Supplier Profile](http://news-medical.net/suppliers/LB-Bohle.aspx)

L.B. Bohle will be showcasing three examples of its pharmaceutical processing technology for the manufacture of oral solid doses at INTERPHEX 2026 in New York City: the [QbCon® 1](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/continuous-granulation-and-drying-qbcon-1) continuous granulation and drying system, the [BFC 5 laboratory tablet coater](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/pharma-tablet-coating-systems/laboratory-coater-bfc-5), and the [BTS 200](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/grinding-and-sieving-systems/conical-sieve-bts) pharmaceutical sieving machine.

QbCon® 1 Continuous Wet Granulation and Drying System. Image Credit: L.B. Bohle Maschinen und Verfahren GmbH

Visitors to the L.B. Bohle stand at Booth 1619 will see how the company’s latest equipment supports continuous manufacturing and improves pharmaceutical production processes and product development efficiency.

These three technology highlights will demonstrate advances in continuous oral solid dose manufacturing, laboratory tablet coating, and pharmaceutical particle processing.

## QbCon® 1 Truly Continuous Wet Granulation and Drying

The [QbCon® 1 Continuous Wet Granulation and Drying System](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/continuous-granulation-and-drying-qbcon-1) represents a significant advancement in continuous oral solid dose production. The system combines continuous raw material dosing, twin-screw wet granulation and drying in one compact, mobile unit.

Advanced features include sophisticated material conveyance, very short residence times in the dryer, automatic exhaust filter self-cleaning and high containment. Integrated control systems increase operational flexibility while enhancing operator safety.

The [QbCon® 1](https://www.lbbohle.com/pharma-machines-processes/continuous-granulation-and-drying-qbcon-1) is designed to deliver narrow residence-time distributions that simplify material traceability in continuous processes. This makes it easier to track materials from raw ingredients to finished tablets, while supporting accelerated scale-up.

##### Neues induktives Schweißverfahren am deutschen Markt

Brugg Pipes, Schweizer Hersteller von flexiblen und starren Rohrsystemen, führt im Frühjahr ein in der Schweiz seit Jahren etabliertes und weiterentwickeltes, patentiertes Schweißverfahren in Deutschland ein. ##### Geothermie-Machbarkeitsstudie in Wuppertal gestartet

Das Fraunhofer-Einrichtung für Energieinfrastrukturen und Geotechnologien IEG und die Wuppertaler Stadtwerke (WSW) führen ihre Zusammenarbeit zur Erforschung tiefer Geothermie fort. Ziel ist eine Machbarkeitsstudie zur Nutzung von Erdwärme aus mehreren tausend Metern Tiefe als klimafreundliche Wärmequelle für Wuppertal.

##### Deutsch-niederländische H2-Anbindung vereinbart

Der niederländische Verteilnetzbetreiber Cogas und die Thyssengas H2 GmbH haben eine Vereinbarung über den Bau eines sogenannten T-Stücks unterzeichnet, das an die geplante H2-Leitung von Vlieghuis nach Ochtrup angeschlossen werden soll. Damit wird erstmals eine Verbindung zwischen einem deutschen Fernleitungsnetzbetreiber und einem niederländischen Verteilnetzbetreiber geschaffen.

[mehr lesen](https://3r-rohre.de/industrie-wirtschaft/deutsch-niederlaendische-h2-anbindung-vereinbart/)

##### Gemeindewerke Baiersbronn setzen auf digitale Dokumentation

Die Gemeindewerke Baiersbronn haben die Dokumentation ihrer Wasserinfrastruktur digitalisiert und setzen dabei auf eine zentrale, mobile Lösung zur Erfassung und Verwaltung der sogenannten Württemberger Schächte. Die Umstellung erfolgte mit Unterstützung der Softwarelösung von Tablano, die auf die digitale Dokumentation und Verwaltung kommunaler Infrastrukturen spezialisiert ist.

[mehr lesen](https://3r-rohre.de/produkte-verfahren/gemeindewerke-baiersbronn-setzen-auf-digitale-dokumentation/)

##### Mehr Stadtgrün: München testet KI-gestützte Bewässerung

Die Stadt München plant, mehr Grün in die Innenstadt zu bringen: Mehr als 150 neue Bäume sollen in den kommenden Jahren in der Fußgängerzone gepflanzt werden. Ziel ist es, die Innenstadt in heißen Sommermonaten abzukühlen und das Stadtklima langfristig zu verbessern.

### News zum Dashboard hinzufügen

Dr. Lisa Irwin-Broß, Dr.-Ing. Ulrich Bohle, Rolf Usadel

Dr.-Ing. Ulrich Bohle (50) hat zum 1. April 2026 die Leitung der Abteilung „Wasser- und Abfallwirtschaft“ in der Bundesgeschäftsstelle der Deutschen Vereinigung für Wasserwirtschaft, Abwasser und Abfall (DWA) übernommen. Er sei eine erfahrene Führungspersönlichkeit mit hoher fachlicher Expertise, breitem Erfahrungshintergrund in der Wasserwirtschaft und engem Bezug zur Praxis, erklärte die Vorständin der DWA, Dr. Lisa Irwin-Broß.

Nach Angaben der DWA gilt Bohle in der Siedlungswasserwirtschaft als ausgewiesener Experte für Stadtentwässerung sowie für ganzheitliches Wasser- und Regenwassermanagement, mit besonderem Schwerpunkt im Bereich der Kanalisation. Der promovierte Bauingenieur ist Mitglied bzw. Obmann von sieben DWA Fachgremien, engagiert sich in DIN- und europäischen Normungsgremien und ist als Referent sowie Leiter von Fort- und Weiterbildungsveranstaltungen aktiv.

Die Arbeit der DWA kennt Bohle aus ehrenamtlicher, fachlicher und praktischer Perspektive. Er folgt auf Dr. Friedrich Hetzel, der Anfang des Jahres bei der DWA die Leitung der neuen Stabsstelle „Wasserbewusste Raum- und Siedlungsentwicklung“ übernommen hat.

Martin Bohle, Dr. és sc.ResearchGate [D-4508-2014](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Martin-Bohle)- International Association for Promoting Geoethics ([IAPG](https://www.geoethics.org/))- [Ronin Research Scholar](https://ronininstitute.org/)@ [edgeryders](https://edgeryders.eu/t/about-us/42); Member of EGU, AGU- recent publication: [Geo-societal Narratives](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-030-79028-8)- Contextualising Geosciences

Recent research into the societal context of geosciences led modern geo-philosophical frameworks to guide professionals and citizens when interacting with World and Planet Earth. These frameworks combine insights into societal and geoscientific features of the World and Earth into a joint knowledge system ([Bohle et al., 2020](https://issuu.com/wfsf.president/docs/issue_2_2020_hf_final_4_sept/28)). Hence, how they are constructed is paramount.

# Introduction

Geo-philosophical frameworks are epistemic problems at the borderline of different knowledge domains (Renn, 2020). Generally, these frameworks often are tacit about their philosophical foundations, exceptions apart (Frodeman, 2003; Marone and Bohle, 2020). This silence is disadvantageous because the particular philosophical foundation determines how societal practices are understood when shaping the Human-Earth Nexus, the bundle of planet Earth, a planetary technosphere, and a hegemonic culture (Haff, 2014; Lemmens et al., 2017; Rosol et al., 2017; Dyer-Witheford, 2018; Dryzek and Pickering, 2019).

Various modern geo-philosophical frameworks inspect the Human-Earth Nexus; see, for example (Zen, 1993; Peppoloni and Di Capua, 2012; Frodeman, 2014; Cherkashin and Sklyanova, 2016; Nikitina, 2016; Di Capua et al., 2017; Bohle, 2020). These frameworks differ by an epistemic foundation in modern Earth System Literacy from earlier frameworks like noosphere (Vernadsky, 1945; Oldfield and Shaw, 2006; Korobova and Romanov, 2014) or Gaia (Lovelock J., 1979; Lovelock, 1990; Lenton and Van Oijen, 2002; Onori and Visconti, 2012).

The modern geo-philosophical frameworks apply, at least implicitly, realist-materialist epistemologies to understand geoscientific features (Bunge, 2006; Marone et al., 2019). Other epistemological concepts for geo-philosophical frameworks like hermeneutic phenomenology exist (Raab and Frodeman, 2002; Frodeman, 2014). Hence, geo-philosophical frameworks are distinguished by their epistemic foundation and the specific philosophical foundations, which refer to insights into societal features and normative settings.

# Example: Cape Town Geoethics

The following illustrative description looks into the school of geo-philosophical frameworks called ‘geoethics’ because the author is familiar with it. The analysis starts with a variant of geoethics, which was summarised in the Cape Town Statement on Geoethics (Di Capua et al., 2017) at the occasion of the 35th International Geological Congress and detailed in 2019 (Peppoloni et al., 2019);

Your board approved AI Agent governance. Your tools can't actually govern AI Agents. The gap boards must close before the new EU AI Act deadlines*: ▸ Your AI agent reads a customer database. Permitted. ▸ It sends an external email. Permitted. ▸ Database read ➜ external email = data exfiltration. No current tool catches this. That's the core problem. ▸ Violations aren't individual actions. They're action sequences. ▸ Yet your governance tools evaluate moments, not paths. ➜ Prompting reduces bad behavior. It cannot enforce anything. Real failure: An agent told 'never share customer data' still exfiltrated a CRM export because it interpreted the task as a legitimate report. The prompt didn't stop it. ➜ Access control blocks action categories, beyond sequences. Real failure: An agent with permitted database and email access combined both steps to send financials externally. Neither permission was violated. The sequence was. ➜ Human approval reviews the step, more than the path that led there. Real failure: A reviewer approved an outbound email without knowing the agent had already pulled restricted data two steps earlier. The breach was invisible at the approval stage. ▸ Regulators will hold you accountable for what your agents do, not what you intended them to do. ▸ Boards that rely on prompts and permissions alone are exposed. ▸ Runtime path-based governance is the missing layer. ▸ It evaluates what the agent did, in sequence, before the next action is permitted. ▸ Not a nice-to-have. A structural requirement. Is your board governing AI agent behavior, or just agent access? * The European Parliament agreed on amending certain obligations. Yet, they are not final because negotiations with the EU Council still need to take place to decide on the final version of amendme

Your AI governance framework looks complete. But can it act in time? ➢ Some institutions respond to AI incidents in 72 hours. Others took 3 weeks. The difference wasn't their policies. It was their speed. ➢ Most institutions have the structures. Policies documented. Escalation paths defined. Decision authority assigned. ➢ What they don't have is speed. ➢ When an AI incident surfaces, the real question is beyond, whether governance exists. It's whether it functions under pressure. ➢ Detection delayed. Escalation stalled. Decisions deferred. Containment inconsistent. Recovery incomplete. ➢ Each gap compounds the next. ➢ This is how it looks like in practice. An AI model begins generating biased outputs. Detection takes 48 hours. Escalation to the right decision-makers takes another 36. The containment decision doesn't land for 5 days. By then, customer impact has already occurred. The governance framework existed. It just couldn't move. ➢ This is decision latency. And it's a strategic variable, not an operational footnote. ➢ The AI Decision Latency Index assesses institutional response speed across five dimensions: ➜ Detection ➜ Escalation ➜ Decision ➜ Containment ➜ Recovery Each scored 1 to 5. Maximum 25. Scores below 15 indicate governance present in form, but not in function. Where would your institution land? How do you move ahead with AI governance when the rules and timelines may be target for change (again)? Providers and deployers are watching Brussels and Berlin closely: Omnibus amendments, high-risk classification guidelines, the national implementation law, Codes of Practice for deepfake labelling. None of it is final yet. And yet internal teams are already making governance decisions, building approval processes, and putting AI systems into their products, services and processes. On 21 April 2026, we’re bringing together policymakers, regulators and industry voices together with @bitkom in Munich to exchange on exactly that. Your SEO Plan for the Rest of 2026

Agree & Join LinkedIn

By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn’s [User Agreement](https://www.linkedin.com/legal/user-agreement?trk=linkedin-tc_auth-button_user-agreement), [Privacy Policy](https://www.linkedin.com/legal/privacy-policy?trk=linkedin-tc_auth-button_privacy-policy), and [Cookie Policy](https://www.linkedin.com/legal/cookie-policy?trk=linkedin-tc_auth-button_cookie-policy).

Every quarter I sit down with my content plan and try to figure out how much of it still makes sense. This quarter, the answer was about half. Not because the plan was bad when I wrote it. Because the environment it was written for had already changed by the time I got around to reviewing it.

Three pieces of content I had scheduled for Q1 were targeting keywords where AI Overviews now dominate the results page. The organic listings I was planning to compete for had been pushed below the fold on mobile. Two other pieces were based on assumptions about search behavior that looked different six months ago than they do now. I hadn't wasted the effort on those articles yet, but I was about to.

This is the reality of SEO planning in 2026. The ground moves faster than most planning cycles account for. If your strategy for the rest of the year is a static document you wrote in January, you're executing a plan designed for a search environment that may not exist anymore. That's not a reason to stop planning. It's a reason to plan differently.

### What to Double Down On

The things that are working in SEO right now have something in common. They're the things that have always worked when you strip away the tricks. Original expertise. Specific, experience-based content. Clear answers to real questions. A coherent body of work that builds authority in a defined area rather than chasing traffic across a dozen unrelated topics.

If you're a service business, the content that earns its keep is the content that demonstrates you've actually done the work. Not "five tips for better marketing" content. Content where you describe a specific process, reference real outcomes, and write from a position that only someone with hands-on experience could write from. That kind of content gets cited by LLMs, gets linked to by other creators, and holds its rankings even as the search results page changes shape around it.

For my own business, that means I'm spending more time on fewer, deeper pieces about marketing photography strategy and less time on the high-frequency, thinner posts I used to think were necessary for momentum. The math changed. One article that demonstrates genuine expertise is worth more now than four articles that cover a topic competently. I'd rather publish twice a month with specificity than weekly with filler.

The team behind Aaru, a bilion-dollar startup, is attracting brands including McDonald’s and EY by betting AI bots can predict human behavior better than humans can, the WSJ reports. Aaru co-founders Cameron Fink and Ned Koh started the company two years ago when they were 18 and 19 years old, respectively, along with technology chief John Kessler, then 15. (Kessler isn’t yet old enough to join the board, and his father had to sign off on the investment paperwork.) Their firm’s work offers a window into how AI is automating labor-intensive, expensive tasks once controlled by research companies, consultants and Madison Avenue. ➡️ Instead of paying humans to join focus groups and complete surveys, Aaru uses thousands of AI agents, or bots, to simulate human responses. It feeds demographic and psychographic information into its models to create human profiles that match clients’ needs, and the results those bots spit out are being used for product development, pricing, identifying new customers and political polling. What do we do?
 
FinKraft - Krafting reliable automated solutions to make complex business processes easier.

How do we do it?

We have built a robust automated SaaS platform to help our clients with these processes and eliminate human error.

FNT Software aspires to make managing highly complex digital infrastructures, such as IT and telecommunications infrastructures easier for companies and public authorities. The FNT Command Platform software solution enables efficient management of IT, Telecommunication and Data Center infrastructures as a digital twin - from the physical level (cables, switches, servers, etc.) through virtual components and applications to services - independent of the manufacturer and in a uniform data model.

FNT’s solutions are used worldwide as an OSS / IT management application for communications service providers, enterprises and government organizations. Powerful, reliable and flexible infrastructures are the basis for all digital business processes and applications, especially those supporting Smart Cities, Industry 4.0 and 5G. FNT’s software solutions enable organizations to record, document and manage complex and heterogeneous IT, telecommunications and data center infrastructures from the physical level all the way to business services. 

FNT stores this information in a manufacturer independent, uniform data model to provide the transparency and tools necessary to plan and manage the IT, data center and telecommunications landscape more easily, eliminate faults faster, optimize synchronization of resources and requirements, and automate delivery of new digital services. 

Over 500 companies and public authorities worldwide rely on FNT, including almost half of DAX40-listed corporations. FNT is headquartered in Ellwangen/Jagst (Germany) and has offices in New York, Timisoara and Singapore. FNT offers its software in numerous countries through partnerships with market-leading IT service providers and system integrators.

[BlackRock](https://www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock?trk=public_post-text) CEO [Larry Fink](https://www.linkedin.com/in/laurencefink?trk=public_post-text) has warned that the rise of artificial intelligence (AI) tools could create a 'crisis' for the graduating Gen Z college students. Speaking at BlackRock's 2026 Infrastructure Summit, Fink stated that the technology could wipe out the entry-level roles that traditionally kickstart corporate careers, potentially leading to record-high unemployment. "When this year's college graduates enter the workforce, we could see the highest unemployment rate among them in years, even without a recession," said Fink as per Fortune. "The speed at which AI is changing, we're not adapting our society fast enough. Really, post World War II, the pathway to a white-collar job was a college education, and AI is going to disrupt many of those types of jobs." Fink highlighted that the usual pathway of completing a four-year college before transitioning to an entry-level job was becoming just one of several viable paths to success. “AI is going to create many jobs and we're not prepared as a society to fulfill those jobs,” Fink said. “And to me, this is a crisis.”

What are the innovative production technologies that reduce water consumption in textiles? The textile industry is one of the most water-intensive sectors globally, making water-saving technologies critically important. While producing one kilogram of textile product requires between 95 and 400 liters of water, new technologies can significantly reduce this amount. 1. Waterless Dyeing Technologies As alternatives to conventional methods, these technologies use different media instead of water: Supercritical CO₂ (ScCO₂): Requires no water; CO₂ can be recovered and reused. It is currently applied especially for synthetic fibers. Air Dyeing: Dye is applied באמצעות air flow, reducing water consumption by more than 90%. Deep Eutectic Solvents (DES): Next-generation solvents that reduce water usage, particularly in polyester and wool dyeing. Organic solvents (e.g., D5): Provide high dye uptake and fastness properties. 2. Innovative Washing and Finishing Technologies Especially in denim processing: Laser Technology: Minimizes water and chemical use. Ozone Washing: Enables bleaching with significantly less water and chemicals. Drop Washing: Achieves up to 95% water and 75% chemical savings. Plastic balls: Replace pumice stones, reducing water pollution and enabling reuse. 3. Printing and Coloring Technologies Digital Printing: Reduces water consumption to below 10 liters per meter. Foam and Slasher Dyeing: Use alternative media instead of water. 4. Recycling and Smart Systems Membrane systems (UF & RO): Enable high levels of wastewater recovery. Siemens and Rittal have launched a strategic partnership to create standardized, sustainable power infrastructure for next‑generation data centers—addressing rapidly rising AI power densities that already exceed 100 kW per rack and could approach 1 MW by 2030. One early solution is a "sidecar" power rack housed in white space, bundling power electronics to provide fast, scalable, OCP‑compatible power distribution. Modular, tested platforms such as RiLineX and Ri4Power, combined with standardized low‑voltage distribution, integrated cooling and heat‑recovery approaches, promise faster deployment, higher availability, and improved tokens‑per‑watt. Standardization reduces complexity and shortens time‑to‑compute for large AI deployments. Wide adoption of these design patterns could make rapid scale‑up of AI compute more predictable, efficient and sustainable—while improving operational safety and simplifying rollout cycles. Semiconductor design keeps getting harder: more complexity, tighter timelines and zero margin for error. That’s why AI can’t be an add‑on. It has to be engineered into the foundation of how design and verification actually work. Today, I want to highlight two important advances in our electronic design automation portfolio: the Fuse EDA AI Agent and new agentic AI capabilities in Questa One through the Questa One Agentic Toolkit. What matters most isn’t just adding more AI, it’s where and how AI is applied. These innovations move beyond isolated features toward intelligent, autonomous workflows that span the full semiconductor design lifecycle. When AI is deeply embedded in design and verification, it can automate complex tasks, accelerate design cycles and help engineers focus on the decisions that truly drive innovation. The Fuse EDA AI Agent works seamlessly with the Questa One Agentic Toolkit as well as across the breadth of the [Siemens EDA (Siemens Digital Industries Software)](https://www.linkedin.com/company/siemens-eda?trk=public_post-text) portfolio, from design conception through manufacturing sign-off, for the complete semiconductor lifecycle. They bring intelligence directly into proven engineering workflows — building on decades of domain expertise, while still giving customers the flexibility to protect existing investments and integrate with other platforms. Our collaboration with [NVIDIA](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nvidia?trk=public_post-text) further strengthens this foundation by combining advanced AI infrastructure with Siemens’ deep semiconductor and system-level understanding. This is how AI delivers lasting value in engineering: when it’s grounded in physics, verification rigor and real-world workflows, and when it’s built by teams who understand the domain end to end. Healing Words Project - 'When one voice heals, it echoes': A solo exhibition of collective voices by Kate Van Doren

Rooted in the idea of collective healing bringing together portraiture, personal mantras, and over 300 self-authored stories created in collaboration with women and gender-expansive voices from around the world. Each work carries something deeply personal, yet when experienced together, they form a shared space for reflection, connection, and understanding.

At the opening, [Nadya Layla Rasheed](https://youtu.be/ZykFV746-dA?si=tmReerYszxAhZ-j1), Ambassador to Palestine in Mexico, introduced the project and spoke about the importance of peace and the role that human rights initiatives like the Healing Words Project play in today’s world. Her words added another layer of meaning to the exhibition, reminding us how vital it is to create platforms where stories can be told and truly heard.

More than anything, this exhibition is an invitation to listen, to feel, and to recognize parts of ourselves in the experiences of others.

Ambassador Nadya Layla Rasheed with her oil portrait and artist Kate Van Doren Renound Iranian Artist Shadi Yousefian with her oil portrait and artist Kate van Doren

Sala 3: Including 400 photo portraits, their stories and the Healing Words Project dress “The power of us”

Mr lady Zen and their large scale charcoal drawing on paper Lee Asheroff opening inauguration with her painting

### Healing Words Project

It begins with people. Faces, bodies, words, offered gently, sometimes bravely, into view. Every portrait carries a presence, a lived moment, a quiet or defiant act of courage. This work has never belonged to one artist alone; it lives with those who chose to step forward and be seen, to trust that their stories and their healing matter. The Healing Words Project first took shape in Mexico in 2020, during Un Día Sin Mujeres, as a response to gender-based violence.

In those early moments, women wrote words directly onto their bodies as marks of resistance, visibility, and solidarity. But something shifted. The images opened into conversations, the conversations into stories, and slowly, into something softer and deeper, healing. What started in protest began to move toward transformation.

Sala 2: Featuring the “collective voices” interactive metal sculpture holding ribbons with written mantras from museum visitors Sala 3: Featuring women’s march photos and voices from feminist activist group Sororas y Rebeldes

Over time, the work has travelled across borders, across cultures, into different communities and contexts, gathering more than 2,000 women and gender-expansive voices along the way. At its center is a quiet but persistent belief that language shapes how we carry ourselves and how we understand our experiences. Here, words are not decoration. They are practice. Spoken, written, witnessed.

## About Yulia Van Doren

Yulia was born in Moscow. She immigrated with her mother to the US in 1984 and is now an American citizen.

Yulia received her MM from Bard College's Graduate Program of the vocal arts in 2008. She is currently an independent artist. She received her BM in classical voice in the spring of 2006 from the New England Conservatory.

A classical soprano, Yulia has won a number of prizes in competitions, including grand prize in the 2006 International J.S. Bach Vocal Competition. She has already amassed impressive professional engagements, particularly in Baroque music. This year, she made debuts at Carnegie Hall and Tanglewood, as well as her professional operatic debut in Seattle.

🧬🧪Relying on a clinical CRO is not just about countries coverage. It is about how well each project is understood, structured, and executed in its specific context. Early phase trials, early access programs, and real-world evidence studies each come with their own constraints, timelines, and regulatory expectations. ➡️ Treating them the same way creates risk where precision is required. At ICTA PM, we structure our organization accordingly. Our teams are dedicated by program type: 🔹 Early phase trials 🔹 Early access programs 🔹 Real-world evidence (RWE) studies In practice, this means: 👍Operating procedures adapted to each type of project 🧑🔬 Teams trained in the specific features of each framework 💻IT tools aligned with the realities of data collection and oversight This is how we secure both execution and compliance across Europe and worldwide with all the necessary flexibility. Because in our experience, anticipating these differences early is what allows projects to move forward with confidence. [Laura Helmick](https://www.linkedin.com/in/laura-helmick?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

LHB Clinical Inc.•10K followers

4d Edited

- [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Flaura-helmick_fda-official-offers-insights-on-agencys-activity-7440141825044074496-BJEO&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I find it truly amazing how Clinical Research has evolved over the years and can only imagine what's ahead. Never a dull moment...and I love trying to keep up with it all! One of the booming areas I've noticed (particularly in the last couple years), includes leveraging RWD and optimizing the use of RWE. I had the pleasure of selling RWE/Commercialization services while with [ICON plc](https://ie.linkedin.com/company/icon-plc-2?trk=public_post-text) several years ago. I have to admit, the acronym RWE was unknown to me prior to that time. Real-world evidence (RWE) is getting more attention in regulatory but it's important to remember that it's not about having more data. It’s about having the right data. A recent article from RAPS breaks down what the FDA is actually looking for when evaluating RWE in submissions: • Is the data fit for purpose? • Is it reliable and high quality? • Does the study design support valid conclusions? We’re seeing this shift play out in hiring as well. Regulatory teams are increasingly looking for candidates who understand both scientific rigor AND real-world data strategy. RWE isn’t replacing clinical trials, but it’s absolutely shaping how evidence is evaluated. Interesting article, in case this area interests you too! Data Extraction is Not Mechanical In RWE, data extraction is not a "copy-paste" task—it is a translation of clinical reality into analyzable variables. Unlike RCTs with standardized case report forms, RWE relies on heterogeneous sources like EHRs and claims. The fidelity of this translation determines the validity of the entire study. We use "Phenotype Algorithms" (combinations of ICD codes, labs, and NLP) to identify patients. But a code for "Heart Failure" in claims data is not a diagnosis, it’s a billing event. Validating these algorithms against a "gold standard" (like manual chart review) is the only way to ensure your study isn't built on "garbage in, gospel out. You can learn more about this in my upcoming book Real-World Evidence: Principles and Practice. Hydrogen Heavyweights: Toyota Joins Volvo & Daimler in Massive Fuel Cell Pact PLM in PTC solutions (Creo/Windchill), manufacturing in SAP environments. In a major automotive shake-up, Toyota Motor Corporation has signed a non-binding memorandum of understanding (MOU) to join Daimler Truck AG and the Volvo Group in their fuel cell joint venture, Cellcentric. Founded in 2021 by the Swedish and German giants, Cellcentric focuses on developing, producing, and commercializing hydrogen fuel cell systems for heavy-duty trucks and sustainable transportation, with operations in Germany and Canada. This move marks a significant shift in the hydrogen landscape. By joining Volvo and Daimler as an equal partner, Toyota—a pioneer in hydrogen technology—is set to accelerate the commercialization of fuel cells as a key technology for decarbonizing long-haul transport. The partnership allows the companies to combine Toyota’s deep knowledge with existing expertise, bringing significant commercial opportunities and sharing the substantial R&D costs associated with hydrogen technology. "Welcoming Toyota onboard will be a big leap towards realising decarbonisation of our industries," said Martin Lundstedt, President and CEO of Volvo Group. Following the closing of the transaction, Toyota intends to contribute its complementary technical know-how to the joint venture, solidifying cellcentric's position as a global leader in fuel cell systems. The initiative highlights the critical role of hydrogen as a long-term solution alongside battery-electric alternatives in the automotive industry's green transition. # Bijdrage van Aerts Trucks NV

708 volgers

6 d

🏆 Beste DAF Dealer voor Sales en Finance - Bedankt! Gisteren mochten we tijdens de [DAF Trucks NV](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/daf-trucks?trk=public_post-text) North West Europe dealermeeting in Westerlo de award voor Beste Dealer voor Sales en Finance in ontvangst nemen. Eerlijk? Dit is geen verhaal van een vlekkeloos jaar. Integendeel. Na een moeilijke eerste helft van 2025 hadden we dit resultaat niet zien aankomen. Net daardoor voelt deze erkenning extra verdiend. We zijn als team een versnelling hoger gaan schakelen. Met focus en vastberadenheid: dossiers strak opvolgen, klanten begeleiden en doorzetten tot het resultaat er is. Wat voor ons het verschil heeft gemaakt, is het vertrouwen van onze klanten. Zij kiezen niet alleen voor een product, maar voor een partner. En dat vertrouwen verplicht ons om telkens opnieuw mee te denken ook financieel. Hier zetten we sterk in op financieringsoplossingen via PACCAR Financial. Dat we als familiebedrijf in dat verhaal onze plaats kunnen innemen tussen vaak grotere spelers uit de Belgïe, Nederland en de Nordics, maakt ons trots. Maar vooral ook dankbaar. Dank aan iedereen die hieraan heeft bijgedragen – onze klanten, ons team én ook het team van DAF voor de sterke ondersteuning en samenwerking. # Cannabis Dispensaries Hit by Payment Processors

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

Brand partnership

3w

How many times have you heard “we’re working on it” from your payment processor this month? Over the past few weeks, sponsor banks have red‑flagged and shut off entire cannabis debit and cashless ATM networks, leaving more than 1,000 dispensaries suddenly in “cash‑only” mode with no real explanation. Lines back up, ticket sizes drop, and your team takes the blame at the counter. The issue usually isn’t the store. It’s the rails. A lot of programs still lean on cashless ATM setups that disguise cannabis sales as ATM withdrawals—the same model Visa has warned can trigger penalties and enforcement if those transactions are miscoded. When sponsor banks sweep those relationships, even fully compliant operators get caught in the blast radius. We’ve been helping operators quietly move off unstable structures and into payment setups that are built to survive these sweeps: no monthly or cancellation fees, real‑time transaction portal, free wireless terminals that also support delivery and curbside, personal support, and rails with 12+ years of uptime in high‑risk, regulated markets. If your dispensary keeps hearing “we’re working on it” every time the network goes down, I’m happy to jump on a quick 15‑minute call to walk through your options and answer any questions you have. What’s the biggest payment pain you’re seeing at your store right now—sudden outages, support, or fees?

𝗢𝗹𝗮𝗽𝗹𝗲𝘅 → 𝗛𝗲𝗻𝗸𝗲𝗹. 𝗔𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗱𝗮𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗶𝘂𝗺 𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗼 𝗽𝗶𝘃𝗼𝘁. 💇♀️🇩🇪 [Henkel](https://de.linkedin.com/company/henkel?trk=public_post-text) just agreed to acquire [OLAPLEX](https://www.linkedin.com/company/olaplex-inc?trk=public_post-text) for ~$1.4 billion — a 55% premium to Wednesday’s close, but a humbling reminder that the brand went public at a $13.6B valuation in 2021. Let that sink in. 𝟵𝟬%+ 𝗼𝗳 𝗶𝘁𝘀 𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗸 𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗲, 𝗴𝗼𝗻𝗲. But here’s what actually matters for our industry: 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝘀𝗻’𝘁 𝗮 𝗱𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝘀𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 — 𝗶𝘁’𝘀 𝗮 𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆. Henkel is doing exactly what the best operators in CPG are doing right now: ✂️ 𝗦𝗵𝗲𝗱𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗹𝗼𝘄-𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗶𝗻 𝘄𝗲𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 — they divested their North American retailer brands business in 2025 🏆 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗶𝘂𝗺 𝗰𝗼𝗿𝗲 — Schwarzkopf + Not Your Mother’s + Olaplex is a serious hair care stack 🌍 𝗟𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗴𝗹𝗼𝗯𝗮𝗹 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗰𝗵 — pairing Olaplex’s DTC and North American retail strength with Henkel’s international distribution is a genuine growth unlock And Henkel isn’t alone. [Unilever](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/unilever?trk=public_post-text), [Reckitt](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/reckitt?trk=public_post-text), [Nestlé](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/nestle-s-a-?trk=public_post-text)— the entire conglomerate class is playing the same game: 𝗳𝗲𝘄𝗲𝗿 𝗯𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗱𝘀, 𝗵𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗶𝗻𝘀, 𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗹 𝗺𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 𝗹𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗴𝗲. IMO, the era of the sprawling CPG portfolio is over. The era of the 𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗱, 𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗶𝘂𝗺-𝗳𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗼 is very much here. For brands navigating this landscape — whether you’re being acquired, acquiring, or watching from the sidelines — the question is the same: 𝗗𝗼 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗸

Congratulations | Chris Henkel

# Darleen Scherer’s Post

Black Sheep•9K followers

22h

Specialty coffee's GRAZA moment is coming. And it probably won't come from anyone who's ever pulled a shot. Graza is the olive oil brand in the bright green squeeze bottle that took over every kitchen in America. Andrew Benin, the founder, had zero food industry experience when he launched it. He came from Glossier and Harry's. Brands built on one belief: packaging is the product. He looked at olive oil and asked one question. Why does this still look like medicine? Dark glass. Slippery. Breakable. Every premium brand identical. He didn't change the oil. He changed how it felt to use it. Bright green squeeze bottle. Two products. Sizzle for cooking. Drizzle for finishing. Same oil, different purpose. $240 million valuation in under two years. No farming background. No food connections. No celebrity. Just a marketing brain in a category that had stopped asking questions. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗨𝗻𝗳𝗶𝗹𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗧𝗮𝗸𝗲: Specialty coffee is ripe for the same disruption. Look at what we've built. Kraft bags. Origin stories. Cupping notes. Minimalist packaging that all looks the same. We obsess over sourcing. We compete on quality metrics nobody understands. We've perfected the coffee. And completely ignored the experience. Someone from beauty. From DTC. From consumer brands. They're going to walk into this industry, look at our identical bags and complicated menus, and ask the question we stopped asking: Why does this still look like this? The disruption won't come from better beans. It will come from someone who's never been to origin but understands how people feel when they touch things. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗕𝗼𝘁𝘁𝗼𝗺 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗲: The most expensive words in coffee right now are "why would we change this?" Insiders built specialty coffee. Outsiders will transform it. The question isn't whether it will happen. It's whether you'll be the disruptor or the disrupted. ___ Every Thursday I send out a newsletter for coffee entrepreneurs seeking clarity in an industry obsessed with quality, drowning in sameness, and unsure what it's actually building anymore. Last week in Toledo, we spent time as Ultrasound leadership team aligning on priorities, choices, and where we need sharper focus. Being together matters. It creates space to challenge assumptions, hear different perspectives, and build real alignment. We also worked through Hoshin Kanri—translating ambition into focused action. At [Siemens Healthineers](https://de.linkedin.com/company/siemens-healthineers?trk=public_post-text), that means elevating health globally by expanding access, improving workflows, and strengthening our role in diagnostic and procedural ultrasound. Toledo felt like the right place for this conversation. A city shaped by centuries of coexistence—where different ideas, cultures, and disciplines came together to create something enduring. A reminder that progress rarely comes from one perspective alone, but from how well we integrate and align many. What this reinforced for me : a career is not a mountain to climb, but a journey we make with people who help us learn, grow, and become better every day while making a positive impact in the world. Grateful to [Jochen Schmitz](https://de.linkedin.com/in/jochen-schmitz?trk=public_post-text) for joining us—and to the team for the openness and shared commitment.

In the early stages of the development of Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) concept engineers must define several design parameters (acceleration time, maximum speed, and target range). A crucial design parameter is vehicle mass, as it affects consumption and determines the required battery energy to cover a certain range. As a result, BEV development is an iterative process, in which the vehicle’s mass is gradually corrected as the vehicle is detailed. At each new mass iteration step, the required battery energy must be adjusted to maintain the target range. Adjustments of a few kWh have a considerable impact on the battery’s volume and mass. Latter leads to further mass fluctuation during the development. The mass fluctuations do not exclusively impact the battery, but also involve other mass-dependent components such as wheels, electric machines, and gearboxes. The dimensions of these components in turn influence the available battery space, which ultimately defines the installable energy in the vehicle. To ensure

Licofarma is a research and production company with over twenty years of experience in the field of natural antioxidants and their use in the development of food supplements and functional cosmetics with targeted action, designed as a valid natural support for the health and well-being of people.

Behind Licofarma is the story of two brothers, two chemical engineers, Leonardo and Consiglio Rescio, who have always been passionate about eco-sustainable extraction processes of natural substances. After years of study, training and experience accumulated within large multinationals in the chemical-pharmaceutical sector, in 1998 the Rescio brothers, attracted by the numerous beneficial properties of Lycopene, decided to pour all their knowledge into the development and implementation of an innovative, completely ecological extraction process. An ambitious project that in 2004 led them, together with their research team, to patent the production process of Organic Lycopene extracted from tomatoes with carbon dioxide (CO2) in supercritical conditions. In 2012, all the accumulated know-how merged into a new company, Licofarma, in which, today, in addition to carrying out research on natural antioxidants, their use in the production of food supplements and functional cosmetics is studied.

Our Mission
We work with passion and ethics to develop innovative and effective functional cosmetics and food supplements, always seeking balance and synergy between nature, tradition and technology.

Our Vision
We want to be a point of reference, guide and partner for those looking for concrete, innovative and natural solutions in response to their health, well-being and beauty care needs.

Turning energy into lap time is one of the hardest problems in motorsport. This is the inside story of how Nissan [#FormulaE](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fformulae&trk=public_post-text) team took a deep dive into the regs and used a second motor spinning at 100,000rpm to gain ~𝟬.𝟴𝘀 𝗽𝗲𝗿 𝗹𝗮𝗽. Making it work wasn’t easy: multiple energy flows, complex control, and tight thermal limits. That’s where Canopy optimal control came in. Huge thanks to [Guillaume Oudot](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/guillaume-oudot-85971b75?trk=public_post-text) and [Chris Vagg](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/chrisvagg?trk=public_post-text) for making this story possible, and to [Scott Mansell](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/scottmansell?trk=public_post-text) for bringing it to life. Japan will launch the first commercial engine that generates electricity by burning a mixture containing up to 30% hydrogen Kawasaki Heavy Industries will release the world’s first commercial 8-megawatt-class gas engine in 2026, capable of running on a 30% hydrogen fuel blend, aimed at reducing CO2 emissions by approximately 1,150 metric tons annually per unit. Tested over 11 months in Kobe, this technology allows using existing natural gas infrastructure to accelerate industrial decarbonization. Key Details About the Engine Launch: Performance: The engine runs on a mixture of up to 30% hydrogen by volume blended with natural gas or city gas, according to Kawasaki Heavy Industries, Ltd.. Environmental Impact: This 30% hydrogen mix is expected to reduce emissions equivalent to the annual emissions of roughly 420 households. Compatibility: The system is designed to allow for the retrofitting of existing KG Series gas engines, allowing operators to transition to greener energy without fully replacing infrastructure. Technology: It features high-efficiency, low- design with adjustable hydrogen co-firing ratios during operation. Infrastructure Support: The initiative is supported by Japan's Green Innovation Fund and is part of a broader strategy for a "hydrogen society". 2. This development marks a significant shift from laboratory testing to practical, commercial application of hydrogen in distributed power generation, according to [vocal.media](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fvocal%2Emedia&urlhash=Z6u9&trk=public_post-text).

ispace, a global lunar resource development company with the vision, “Expand our planet. Expand our future.”, specializes in designing and building lunar landers and rovers. ispace aims to extend the sphere of human life into space and create a sustainable world by providing high-frequency, low-cost transportation services to the Moon. Toward that vision, we aim to build an economic zone between the Earth and the Moon (cislunar) through the commercialization of lunar surface development.

In 2022, we launched our first mission to the Moon. It was the culmination of years of hard work and our first step towards extending human life into outer space. Mission 2, launched in 2025, was the second and final mission of the HAKUTO-R lunar exploration program and featured the RESILIENCE lunar lander carrying the TENACIOUS micro rover, navigating deep space and entering lunar orbit before attempting to land on the Moon. With the completion of the R&D Phase, ispace moves forward, taking the lessons learned from Mission 1 and Mission 2, and transitioning into its “Early Commercialization Phase" with Missions 3 & 4 currently in development.

Jensen Huang spent this week at GTC making the case that Physical AI's moment has arrived. New humanoid robot foundation models. [Figure](https://www.linkedin.com/company/figure-ai?trk=public_post-text) and [Physical Intelligence](https://www.linkedin.com/company/physical-intelligence?trk=public_post-text) raised bilions of dollars. Humanoid robotics funding is up 80% year over year. Building robots is incredibly hard and they are doing an awesome job. But I keep coming back to the same question: who builds the brain that actually knows what to do? Not a generic brain. The specific one. The one that knows what the operator with 30 years on a machine knows. That a certain vibration means you slow down, not speed up. The one that knows what the production manager knows. How to juggle customer priority, lead times, and machine availability in a way no spreadsheet can capture. That knowledge lives in people's heads, siloed systems and spreadsheets today. It's contextual. It's messy. And it's the actual bottleneck between where manufacturing is now and where Physical AI promises to take it. At [Takt](https://www.linkedin.com/company/taktconnect?trk=public_post-text), this is what we spend every day thinking about. A contextual reasoning layer that can bridge that gap. I won't pretend we've solved it. The questions are genuinely hard. How much context do you hold? How do you build custom reasoning for every role in a factory while keeping the whole operation coherent? How do you go from a sales order to a shop floor decision in one connected flow? But I believe this layer is inevitable. And I think whoever figures it out changes how manufacturing works. If this is the kind of problem you think about, whether you're building in this space, investing in it, or just fascinated by it, I'd love to talk and learn from you. And if you're an engineer who gets excited by this, we're hiring. Come help us figure it out: [araghavan@taktconnect.co

Reimagining Sales and Marketing Effectiveness for a Global Pharma Leader At Infocepts, we recently partnered with a leading pharmaceutical company to elevate commercial performance across key international markets. As part of our Business Development team, I am excited to share how this engagement demonstrates the power of data and AI in driving real business impact. The client was struggling with fragmented regional data, limited visibility into sales representative productivity, and a weak connection between field marketing activities and sales outcomes. To address this, our team delivered: 🔹 A centralized Snowflake data platform to standardize shipment and CRM datasets 🔹 Cohesive modeling with dbt to create trusted, analytics‑ready data 🔹 Territory level and country level Power BI reporting for granular performance visibility 🔹 Custom Power Apps to streamline field data capture and enable real time insights 🔹 Targets versus actuals integration to support incentives and performance management With these capabilities in place, the client accelerated decisions and unlocked measurable impact: ✅ Eighty percent reduction in manual effort ✅ Access to critical insights in under twenty-four hours with reporting lag reduced by seventy five percent ✅ Thirty percent increase in account engagement ✅ Twenty percent revenue growth in the first year This collaboration shows how an insights‑driven approach can transform field effectiveness, improve marketing impact, and fuel sustainable growth in life sciences. Recursion (NASDAQ: RXRX) is a clinical stage TechBio company leading the space by decoding biology to radically improve lives. Enabling its mission is the Recursion OS, a platform built across diverse technologies that continuously generate one of the world’s largest proprietary biological and chemical datasets. Recursion leverages sophisticated machine-learning and AI algorithms to distill from its dataset a collection of trillions of searchable relationships across biology and chemistry unconstrained by human bias. By commanding massive experimental scale — up to millions of wet lab experiments weekly — and massive computational scale — owning and operating one of the most powerful supercomputers in the world, Recursion is uniting technology, biology and chemistry to advance the future of medicine.

Recursion is headquartered in Salt Lake City, where it is a founding member of BioHive, the Utah life sciences industry collective. Recursion also has offices in Montréal, New York, London, and the Oxford area.

Learn more at www.Recursion.com, or connect on X (formerly Twitter) and LinkedIn.

We strive to transform lives. While the science we advance is constantly evolving, our core purpose is enduring. For more than two centuries, our values have guided us to do what’s right for patients and for society.

We know that changing lives requires us to do things differently. We start by listening to and addressing what really matters to patients, the people who love them, and those in the healthcare system who provide care. And that’s what inspires us all to be bold, push boundaries and set new standards that open up greater opportunities.

person: 和田 信二 (Shinji Wada)（2025年12月31日退任）
text:
WSI is a global digital marketing and AI consulting network with 30 years of experience helping businesses grow and adapt. Our mission is simple but powerful: to unlock a world of possibility for the businesses we serve.

We believe digital transformation should elevate—not replace—the people behind a business. That’s why our approach blends innovative strategies and smart technologies with a strong human focus. Captured in our motto, Embrace Digital. Stay Human., this philosophy guides everything we do.

As one of the world’s most established digital marketing agencies, we help businesses drive real results—from increasing leads and enhancing customer experiences to building future-ready strategies powered by AI.

With 150,000+ businesses served, 180+ industry awards, and three published books on digital marketing, we bring the experience, creativity, and insight to help businesses not just keep up—but move ahead.

Partnering with WSI means working with industry experts dedicated to your success—whether in digital marketing, AI consultancy, or both.
Advertising Services · 346 employees (-0.5% YoY) · Etobicoke, Canada · Founded 1995 · 149,829 followers (+3.5% this year)

person: 和田 信二 (Shinji Wada)（2025年12月31日退任）
text:
Puig is a home of Love Brands, within a family company, that furthers wellness, confidence and self-expression while leaving a better world. Since 1914, our company’s entrepreneurial spirit, creativity and passion for innovation have made Puig a challenger in the beauty and fashion world.
 
Present in the Fragrance and Fashion, Makeup and Skincare segments, our house of Love Brands generates engagement through great storytelling that connects with people’s emotions and is reinforced by a powerful ecosystem of founders. Puig portfolio includes our brands Rabanne, Carolina Herrera, Charlotte Tilbury, Jean Paul Gaultier, Nina Ricci, Dries Van Noten, Byredo, Penhaligon’s, L’Artisan Parfumeur, Uriage, Apivita, Dr. Barbara Sturm, Kama Ayurveda and Loto del Sur as well as the beauty licenses of Christian Louboutin, Banderas and Adolfo Dominguez, among others.
 
At Puig we honor the values and principles put in place by three generations of family leadership. Today we continue to build on that legacy, through conscious commitments in our ESG Agenda (environmental, social and governance) aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
 
In 2024, Puig recorded net revenues of €4,790 million. Puig sells its products in more than 150 countries and has offices in 32 of them.

# 依田 隼弥さんの投稿

「コンテキストエンジニアリング」と、 「ハーネスエンジニアリング」の違いとは？ この2つは似ているようで、 カバーする範囲が全く異なります。 1. コンテキストエンジニアリングとは？ AIに「必要な情報や知識（マニュアル）」を与えること。プロンプトで目標を設定したり、 [xn--AGENTS-oi4el13t5n4bj9f.xn--md-f93a6be5jg2hrc4925lbwla](http://xn--agents-oi4el13t5n4bj9f.xn--md-f93a6be5jg2hrc4925lbwla/?trk=public_post-text)「情報整理」のフェーズ。 2. ハーネスエンジニアリングとは？ コンテキストを内包した上で、AIが失敗しないための「作業環境や自動チェック体制の全体基盤」を設計・構築すること。AIがミスをした際に、二度と同じミスを繰り返さない解決策をシステムとして実装する。 具体的にハーネスエンジニアリングが構築するのは、以下の4つの仕組み。 🔹 ツールの提供とフィードバック AIにテキストを読ませるだけでなく、ブラウザ操作などの権限を与え、実際にテストしてエラーを自己修正させるループを作る。 🔹 アーキテクチャの制約（ガードレール） ルール違反を物理的に防ぐため、カスタムリンターなどを組み込み、エラーを弾くだけでなく「直し方」もAIに教える。 🔹 複数エージェントによる役割分担 1つのAIにすべてを任せず、「プランナー」「ジェネレーター」「評価者（QA）」など役割を分割し、システム全体で連携させる。 🔹 コンテキストの継続的な改善 AIがミスをするたびに、マニュアル（ [AGENTS.xn--md-mb4ad](http://agents.xn--md-mb4ad/?trk=public_post-text)）を自動または半自動で書き換え、フィードバックループを回す。 【結論】 新入社員（AI）にマニュアルを渡すのが「コンテキストエンジニアリング」。 新入社員用の仕事用PCにミスを防ぐソフトを入れ、厳格なチェック体制（評価エージェント）を整備し、確実に仕事が進められるオフィス環境全体を設計するのが「ハーネスエンジニアリング」。 AIを真に自律駆動させるためには、この「ハーネス」の視点が不可欠になる。 [https://lnkd.in/gffFNMqc](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FgffFNMqc&urlhash=bIwL&trk=public_post-text)

[いいね！](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fjp%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2F%25E9%259A%25BC%25E5%25BC%25A5-%25E4%25BE%259D%25E7%2594%25B0-502240370_%25E3%2582%25B3%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2583%2586%25E3%2582%25AD%25E3%2582%25B9%25E3%2583%2588%25E3%2582%25A8%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B8%25E3%2583%258B%25E3%2582%25A2%25E3%2583%25AA%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B0%25E3%2581%25A8-%25E3%2583%258F%25E3%2583%25BC%25E3%2583%258D%25E3%2582%25B9%25E3%2582%25A8%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B8%25E3%2583%258B%25E3%2582%25A2%25E3%2583%25AA%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B0%25E3%2581%25AE%25E9%2581%2595%25E3%2581%2584%25E3%2581%25A8%25E3%2581%25AF-activity-7444158941586100224-ljkm&trk=public_post_like-cta) [コメント](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fjp%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fposts%2F%25E9%259A%25BC%25E5%25BC%25A5-%25E4%25BE%259D%25E7%2594%25B0-502240370_%25E3%2582%25B3%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2583%2586%25E3%2582%25AD%25E3%2582%25B9%25E3%2583%2588%25E3%2582%25A8%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B8%25E3%2583%258B%25E3%2582%25A2%25E3%2583%25AA%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B0%25E3%2581%25A8-%25E3%2583%258F%25E3%2583%25BC%25E3%2583%258D%25E3%2582%25B9%25E3%2582%25A8%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B8%25E3%2583%258B%25E3%2582%25A2%25E3%2583%25AA%25E3%2583%25B3%25E3%2582%25B0%25E3%2581%25AE%25E9%2581%2595%25E3%2581%2584%25E3%2581%25A8%25E3%2581%25AF-activity-7444158941586100224-ljkm&trk=public_post_comment-cta)` `` `

From offshore wind zones to new grid infrastructure laws, Asian jurisdictions are reshaping the region’s energy sector. 

 

---

 

### Japan’s 2026 plan to promote offshore wind energy in EEZ 

 

By Shunta Doki and Yosuke Nakano of Chuo Oh-Ebashi LPC & Partners 

 

In Japan, the promotion of renewable energy is expected to continue to advance through groundbreaking amendments that allow offshore wind power projects in Japan’s exclusive economic zone (EEZ) and the strengthening of the Green Transformation (GX) emissions trading system, both of which are set to take effect. 

 

The Act on the Development of Marine Renewable Energy Power Generation Facilities (the amended Act on Promoting the Utilisation of Sea Areas), which enables the installation of marine renewable energy power generation facilities in the EEZ is scheduled to come into force on 1 April 2026. 

 

The Act on the Promotion of Smooth Transition to a Decarbonised, Growth-Oriented Economic Structure (GX Promotion Act) has also been amended, and participation in the Emissions Trading System (GX-ETS) will become mandatory for specified companies starting 1 April 2026.

### Anchoring power strategy: South Korea’s 11th Basic Plan 

 

By Heejin Kim and Sang Soon Park of D&A 

 

South Korea’s 11th Basic Plan for Electricity Supply and Demand (2024-2038) serves as the policy anchor for the nation’s long-term electricity supply strategy. Finalised in February 2025 together with key statutes pursuant to article 25 of the Electric Utility Act, it provides the regulatory framework guiding Korea’s energy transition.

# B2B SaaS Competitive Landscape Shifts with In-House Development

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

Trissino (Techstars '25)•4K followers

1w

In 2026, the biggest competitor for your B2B SaaS isn’t Vendor A or Vendor B. It’s your buyer’s own team, armed with Claude Code. 🛠️ The competitive landscape has fundamentally shifted. We are no longer just fighting in a "Vendor vs. Vendor" war. We are fighting "Vendor vs. In-House Development." With the barrier to building software dropping to near zero, how do B2B SaaS companies maintain their edge when buyers suddenly have a very viable "we can just build this ourselves" option? If you are a PMM or SaaS Marketer, your GTM strategy needs an immediate update: 📊 1. The Feature Comparison Matrix Your website’s comparison page usually lists "Us vs. Competitor A vs. Competitor B." It’s time to add a new column: "Build It In-House." You must clearly articulate the true cost of ownership. It’s not just about the ease of building the initial product—it’s about the hidden nightmare of maintaining it, scaling it, and dealing with technical debt over time versus the continuous innovation your SaaS provides. ⚔️ 2. The "In-House" Battlecard "In-House Development" needs its own dedicated sales battlecard. When a prospect says on a demo call, "Our engineering team thinks they can just build this internally," does your sales team have a unified, bulletproof objection-handling framework ready? They need one today. The value of SaaS is no longer just "the code." It's the ongoing reliability, expertise, and operational peace of mind. We have to treat "In-House" as the formidable competitor it has become. Are you a B2B SaaS Marketer or PMM running into this exact objection more often on the front lines? If so, I’d love to connect. Let’s have a discussion on how we can strategically position our products against the rising tide of AI-powered in-house development. Drop a comment below or send me a DM! ## About

Dr. Prakash Chaki obtained his master's degree in electrical engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Bombay, and Ph.D. degree in information and communication engineering from The University of Tokyo. A senior researcher in wireless communications, Dr. Chaki is presently working on 5G-Advanced and 6G, at NEC laboratories, Greater Tokyo area. As a 3GPP RAN1 delegate for channel coding in the standardization of 5G new radio (NR), Dr. Chaki has actively contributed to RAN1 for 5G control channel coding scheme. He has many patented inventions covering diverse areas of WLAN, coding theory and MIMO communications, most of which have already been granted in USA, Japan, or Europe. With demonstrated skills in inventing and patenting, Dr. Chaki is a recipient of NEC Young Researcher Award in 2019, two-time winner of NEC Excellent Invention award in 2015 and 2018, NEC Patent Award for contribution in terms of large number of issued patents in 2021, and Young Researcher Award from the Institute of Electronics, Information and Communication Engineers (IEICE) in 2017. In 2023, he was recognized for Notable Services and Contributions towards the advancement of IEEE and the Engineering Professions. Dr. Chaki has served as an Executive Committee (EXCOM) member of IEEE Japan Council, as well as an EXCOM member of IEEE Tokyo Section. He has also served as the Chair of IEEE Tokyo Section Young Professionals, and as the Chair of IEEE Tokyo Section Educational Activities. He currently serves as an Editor for IEEE CTN, IEEE WCL, and IEEE OJVT, and also as a technical reviewer for many esteemed journals including IEEE Transactions on Comm., IEEE Transactions on Wireless Comm., IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Tech., IEEE Journal on Selected Topics in Signal Processing, IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Comm., IEEE Wireless Comm. Letters, IEEE Signal Processing Letters, IEEE OJCOMS, IEEE OJVT, Elsevier Physical Communications, IET, ETRI, Wiley, and IEICE journals. An avid sports enthusiast, public speaker, and a member of Toastmasters International where he also served as a Secretary, Dr. Chaki communicates in 4 languages (English, Japanese, Hindi and Bengali). In his time away from work, he is found reading, sketching, volunteering or doing sports. He is passionate about team building, with recognized leadership across diverse roles. He is an active blood donor; if you are in Tokyo area and in need of blood for medical emergency, do reach out. Dr. Chaki held a Guinness world record. Website: https://www.nec.com/en/global/rd/people/prakash_chaki.html

The bottleneck for physical AI just shifted. NVIDIA’s EgoScale paper showed that 20,000 hours of egocentric human video outperforms robot teleoperation for teaching dexterity. 54% improvement. Simply put - more human video, better robot performance. The implication: robot learning no longer scales with robot-hours. It scales with human-hours. That changes everything about how training data gets sourced. You don’t need 50 people in a lab. You need thousands of humans in real kitchens, factories, and construction sites - across geographies and task types. That’s the infrastructure we’re building at [Humyn Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/humyn-labs?trk=public_post-text). 1M+ hours of egocentric video. 7 countries. Commercial, residential, agricultural settings. If you’re working on embodied AI and thinking about data pipelines - happy to share what we’re learning. We ship 25 to 30 product updates a month. That's not a quarterly release cycle. That's not a big annual update with a flashy announcement. Almost every single day, something inside Recruit CRM gets better. Here's what most people don't realise about how we do it. 60-65% of all the code we deploy today is written by AI. Not by a human sitting and typing it line by line. Engineers direct, review, and ship what AI produces. And the output is a product that moves faster than it ever has before. When people ask me what AI has actually changed about building software, this is my answer. It's not the chatbot in your product. It's not the fancy demo. It's the speed at which a small, focused team can now build and ship something genuinely good. That's the real advantage.

# Anyako Armbruster’s Post

1w

The best hiring decisions are made in the field. As a Recruitment Lead, I make it a priority to spend time on the ground — with our users, our agents, and our teams. It’s the only way to truly understand how the business operates, how deeply we’re penetrating the market, and what success actually looks like. Job descriptions don’t tell you that. Interviews don’t tell you that. The field does. These visits directly shape how we hire: → what “great” really looks like → which profiles will actually perform → where we’re misaligned with reality At Wave, we’re encouraged to stay close to the business, not just the pipeline. That flexibility is a real strength — and something I don’t take for granted.🩵 Because at the end of the day, recruiting isn’t about filling roles. It’s about building teams that win in the real world.

Absolutely. Directly meeting with end-users of products and services we offer exposes us to hear their feedbacks and complaints they have, and this helps shape hiring decisions: what type of professionals to bring onboard that'd close the gaps excellently.

Welcome!

We are Xpheno (pronounced ‘ex-feeno’), a Specialist Talent Solutions company that offers Direct Hire, RPO, IT Staff Augmentation, Executive Search, Engineering Professional Services & Sales/Support Staffing services.

Founders Kamal Karanth and Anil Kumar ET, who have served in marquee staffing companies as Managing Directors in their previous stints, have come together with a seasoned, like-minded team, to lead Xpheno. 

Xpheno the name, is short for extended phenotype. It is derived from the concept of genes and their effect on the environment. We believe that this concept lends itself to the world of human resources perfectly because when placed in a role that best suits their abilities, people are able to influence large teams and companies. Thereby, having a huge, positive impact on the Eco-system and very often on the way we live.

At Xpheno, we understand that organisations need to get talent at the right speed, scale, quality, and at the right costs, or what we call SSQC. However, at the foundation of this are people, and the relationships we build with them. It’s the conversations we have, and the bonds we form that are critical for aspirants to make a decision.

Our team’s industry expertise in Life Sciences, Consumer & Retail, Automotive, Construction, Infrastructure and IT enables us to find the right people, for the right role and thereby ensuring that we fulfill our promise every time.

「コンテキストエンジニアリング」と、 「ハーネスエンジニアリング」の違いとは？ この2つは似ているようで、 カバーする範囲が全く異なります。 1. コンテキストエンジニアリングとは？ AIに「必要な情報や知識（マニュアル）」を与えること。プロンプトで目標を設定したり、 [xn--AGENTS-oi4el13t5n4bj9f.xn--md-f93a6be5jg2hrc4925lbwla](http://xn--agents-oi4el13t5n4bj9f.xn--md-f93a6be5jg2hrc4925lbwla/?trk=public_post-text)「情報整理」のフェーズ。 2. ハーネスエンジニアリングとは？ コンテキストを内包した上で、AIが失敗しないための「作業環境や自動チェック体制の全体基盤」を設計・構築すること。AIがミスをした際に、二度と同じミスを繰り返さない解決策をシステムとして実装する。 具体的にハーネスエンジニアリングが構築するのは、以下の4つの仕組み。 🔹 ツールの提供とフィードバック AIにテキストを読ませるだけでなく、ブラウザ操作などの権限を与え、実際にテストしてエラーを自己修正させるループを作る。 🔹 アーキテクチャの制約（ガードレール） ルール違反を物理的に防ぐため、カスタムリンターなどを組み込み、エラーを弾くだけでなく「直し方」もAIに教える。 🔹 複数エージェントによる役割分担 1つのAIにすべてを任せず、「プランナー」「ジェネレーター」「評価者（QA）」など役割を分割し、システム全体で連携させる。 🔹 コンテキストの継続的な改善 AIがミスをするたびに、マニュアル（ [AGENTS.xn--md-mb4ad](http://agents.xn--md-mb4ad/?trk=public_post-text)）を自動または半自動で書き換え、フィードバックループを回す。 【結論】 新入社員（AI）にマニュアルを渡すのが「コンテキストエンジニアリング」。 新入社員用の仕事用PCにミスを防ぐソフトを入れ、厳格なチェック体制（評価エージェント）を整備し、確実に仕事が進められるオフィス環境全体を設計するのが「ハーネスエンジニアリング」。 AIを真に自律駆動させるためには、この「ハーネス」の視点が不可欠になる。 [https://lnkd.in/gffFNMqc](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FgffFNMqc&urlhash=bIwL&trk=public_post-text)

# Human Insights vs AI Knowledge Transfer

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bfretwell?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Brian Fretwell](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bfretwell?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

5d

- [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fbfretwell_learn-ai-connection-activity-7440164881204584448-aRfV&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

I've been using claude to help identify where we WON'T need Claude... Specifically, by getting really clear about the relationship between learning and connection. Or rather, outlining the difference between simple knowledge transfer and the magic that happens between humans when they mine their different unique experiences for shared insights. What's coming up again and again is something I've seen consistently in the connection work we've done over the last five years. When people learn to ask about experiences in a way that is valuable for both people (instead of asking for SOFA -- suggestions, opinion, feedback or advice -- they ask the person how they DID something) the output is almost always a singular or shared INSIGHT. And, it's those collective insights that cannot be replicated. It's the interactions and communication that create those insights that cannot be replicated. And, while AI can give us plenty of knowledge...it cannot fill the knowing/doing gap as its the hundreds and thousands of big and little shared insights generated from exploring unique experiences that does it everyday. The risk is in the teams and organizations that are relying on expertise, on a handful of "knowledge-able" people, or a framework that is static because they aren't generating the moat that only human experience can generate. I'm not sure how claude feels about me trying to find where it won't be needed. But the more clearly I see the gap between what it can do and what we can do, the more confident I am that we'll all be needed...perhaps more than ever. ## About

I recently realized that I have been in higher education for over 46 years. That is a long time and a lot of experience to share. I studied civil engineering at the University of Ghent (Belgium), worked there as a research assistant for 8 years. Seeing the need for professional and pragmatic education in companies, I founded my own company IT Works in 1992, empowering businesses and professionals to accelerate their digital transformation journeys. I ran this company for 30+ years but Covid was not particularly a friend of my hands-on face-to-face business model. Since 2023, I have returned to the academic world at Howest University of Applied Sciences because there is a huge potential there in education, research and networking. My expertise and interests include cutting-edge technologies like (Generative) Artificial Intelligence, Big Data and Data Engineering, Blockchain, Robotic Process Automation, HyperAutomation, Web3, Immersive Technologies, Quantum Computing, ... but my strength lies in bringing people together around emerging technologies. I was born to learn, and have always been on a mission to inspire, educate, and connect forward-thinking individuals and organisations. Through my extensive network of industry experts, clients, and partners, I have organised over 1500 high-impact, vendor-agnostic events - both virtual and in-person - that served as knowledge hubs and learning networks In addition to event curation, I provide tailored strategic advice, coaching, and mentorship programs aimed at amplifying the impact and reach of startups, speakers, and consultants in the IT space. Yes, I am a boomer who recently joined Howest University of Applied Sciences (howest.be), where many teachers and researchers are half my age. That keeps me young and up-to-date with current tech. This was definitely not an end-of-career switch, and also not for the money (bye bye high salary, decades of seniority, company car, meal vouchers, bonuses), but a well thought-out move to have more impact on what is really needed in the workplace. At Howest, I can influence decisions on emerging technology education that is really needed in the field, and stimulate the dialogue between high-tech educational institutes and the labour market. My unofficial title "Community Builder" highlights the need and the desire to build communities of interest (learning and living networks) that communicate about these technologies. Contact me if you are interested in my journey!
